investments.company: ACP Avenu Midco LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: The operating company is Avenu Insights & Analytics, a SaaS platform that helps more than 4,000 state and local government agencies across the U.S. and Canada recover revenue, process payments, and manage day-to-day administrative operations. Its product suite includes compliance and audit solutions, permitting and licensing, digital payment processing, court case management, and public administration software. Arlington Capital Partners acquired Avenu from Mill Point Capital in October 2023; the business subsequently merged with Intellectual Technology Inc. and acquired GovOS to form a broader government modernization platform called Neumo.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are state and local government bodies — counties, municipalities, court systems, and state agencies — that use Avenu's software to collect taxes and fees, automate citizen-facing transactions, and run back-office functions. The value proposition is revenue maximization without tax increases and operational cost reduction, making the software deeply embedded in government workflows.

investments.company: Actfy Buyer, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Software provider of end to end fraud management workflow solutions
investments.business_description: Actfy Buyer, Inc. is the acquisition vehicle used by Accel-KKR to acquire Accertify, a fraud prevention and chargeback management software company carved out from American Express in May 2024. Accertify provides machine-learning-powered fraud detection, account protection, and payment gateway solutions to large enterprises, integrating into checkout and payment flows to score transaction risk in real time. The company is headquartered in Itasca, Illinois.
investments.end_customers: Accertify's customers include 40% of the top 100 U.S. retailers, major global travel and hospitality companies, and large financial services enterprises. These businesses use Accertify to reduce chargebacks, prevent account takeover, and minimize fraud losses without adding friction to legitimate customer transactions.

investments.company: Activate Holdings (US) Corp. and CrossPoint Capital AS SPV, LP
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: The operating company is Absolute Security (formerly Absolute Software), a cybersecurity platform acquired by CrossPoint Capital Partners in July 2023 for approximately $870 million. Absolute provides self-healing endpoint resilience software that is firmware-embedded in over 600 million devices from major PC manufacturers, giving enterprises persistent visibility and control over devices, data, and network connections even after a device is wiped or reimaged. The company is headquartered in Vancouver, Canada.
investments.end_customers: Customers are enterprise IT and security teams across healthcare, financial services, government, and education verticals — any organization with a large fleet of managed endpoints. The firmware-level persistence makes Absolute particularly attractive to regulated industries where device compliance and data protection are mandatory.

investments.company: Adonis Bidco Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of intellectual property management lifecycle software
investments.business_description: Adonis Bidco Inc. is the U.S. holding entity in the CVC Capital Partners-led consortium acquisition of WithSecure Corporation, a Finnish cybersecurity software company taken private in 2025 at an enterprise value of approximately €292 million. WithSecure provides cloud-native endpoint protection, detection and response, and managed security services focused on small and mid-sized businesses and the partners (MSSPs and resellers) who serve them. The company spun out from F-Secure in 2022 and is headquartered in Helsinki, Finland.
investments.end_customers: WithSecure's customers are primarily SMBs reached through a channel of managed security service providers, IT resellers, and telecom operators in Europe and internationally. These partners embed WithSecure's endpoint security into their managed service offerings, making WithSecure's revenue recurring and channel-driven.

investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)

investments.company: AI Titan Parent, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of plant maintenance / scheduling software
investments.business_description: AI Titan Parent, Inc. is identified by Bloomberg as a software company in the Silver Lake portfolio, most likely the parent holding structure for Zuora, Inc., a leading subscription management and billing platform that Silver Lake and GIC acquired for approximately $1.7 billion in February 2025. Zuora's platform automates the entire subscription order-to-revenue process — from quoting and billing to collections and revenue recognition — and serves companies across technology, media, telecommunications, and manufacturing. The company is based in Redwood City, California.
investments.end_customers: Zuora's customers are mid-market and enterprise companies transitioning to subscription or consumption-based business models, including software vendors, media companies, IoT manufacturers, and professional services firms. Its software handles billing complexity (tiered pricing, usage metering, multi-currency invoicing) that standard ERP systems cannot support at scale.

investments.company: Anaplan, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of cloud-based connected planning platforms for business analytics
investments.business_description: Anaplan is a cloud-native connected planning platform that allows large enterprises to model and plan across finance, sales, supply chain, and HR functions on a single platform. Thoma Bravo took Anaplan private in June 2022 for approximately $10.7 billion. The platform uses a proprietary in-memory calculation engine (Hyperblock) that enables complex, enterprise-scale scenario modeling without the data movement and latency of traditional BI tools. Anaplan is headquartered in San Francisco.
investments.end_customers: Anaplan's customers are Fortune 500 and Global 2000 enterprises — particularly large finance, sales operations, and supply chain teams that need to synchronize planning across business units. Industries served include financial services, retail, technology, life sciences, and manufacturing, where the value is eliminating siloed spreadsheets and enabling real-time cross-functional planning.

investments.company: Aptean, Inc. and Aptean Acquiror Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of CRM, ERP and supply chain software application
investments.business_description: Aptean is a global provider of industry-specific enterprise resource planning (ERP) and supply chain management software targeting mid-market manufacturers, distributors, and food and beverage companies. The company has grown through more than 50 acquisitions of vertical-market ERP products, assembling a portfolio of purpose-built systems for industries such as process manufacturing, discrete manufacturing, meat and poultry processing, and consumer goods. Aptean is backed by TA Associates and Insight Partners and is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
investments.end_customers: Customers are mid-market manufacturers and distributors — typically $50M to $1B in revenue — in industries where generic ERP systems lack the regulatory compliance, traceability, and industry-specific workflows they require. Aptean's ERP systems are core operating infrastructure, making switching costs extremely high.

investments.company: Archduke Buyer, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Developer and operator of Java runtime solutions for enterprises applications
investments.business_description: Archduke Buyer, Inc. is an Ares Management-backed acquisition vehicle in the software and services sector. The specific operating company has not been publicly identified by name; Ares Management regularly uses anonymized BidCo/Buyer entities as borrowing structures in its direct lending and private equity portfolios. Given the first-lien classification and software industry designation in ARCC's portfolio, the underlying business is likely a vertical or horizontal SaaS company with recurring revenue and strong retention metrics typical of Ares' software underwriting criteria.
investments.end_customers: The end customer base, consistent with Ares Management's software portfolio profile, is likely enterprise or mid-market businesses depending on the software for mission-critical workflows, with high renewal rates and multi-year contract structures.

investments.company: Arrow Borrower 2025, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of accounts payable automation software and payment solutions
investments.business_description: Arrow Borrower 2025, Inc. is a 2025-vintage Ares Management-sponsored borrowing entity in the software and services sector. The '2025' suffix indicates it was established as a refinancing or new issuance vehicle in 2025. The underlying operating business has not been publicly identified, but the structure is consistent with Ares Management's direct lending practice of creating fresh borrower entities for repricing or add-on facilities in existing software portfolio companies.
investments.end_customers: As with other Ares software borrowers, the underlying company's customers are likely enterprise or mid-market businesses in a specialized software vertical with subscription-based revenue, consistent with Ares' underwriting focus on durable, low-churn software businesses.

investments.company: Artifact Bidco, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Supply chain risk management SaaS platform for global enterprise clients
investments.business_description: Artifact Bidco, Inc. is the acquisition vehicle EQT used to acquire WSO2, a Sri Lanka- and U.S.-headquartered open-source enterprise software company, in August 2024 for more than $600 million. WSO2 provides API management, identity and access management (IAM), and integration platform software that enterprises use to build and secure digital services. Its products compete with Apigee (Google), MuleSoft (Salesforce), and Okta in the API gateway and identity management markets.
investments.end_customers: WSO2's customers are large enterprises across financial services, healthcare, government, and telecommunications that need to expose and govern APIs, manage customer identity, and integrate disparate backend systems. Over 80% of revenue comes from blue-chip customers in the Americas and EMEA, reflecting deep entrenchment in regulated, complex IT environments.

investments.company: Aston Bidco (Holding) Limited
investments.investment_type: Provider of enterprise software solutions
investments.business_description: Aston Bidco is the holding structure for Advanced (formerly Advanced Computer Software Group), the UK's third-largest software company, which BC Partners acquired a 50% stake in from Vista Equity Partners in 2019 at a reported valuation of approximately £2 billion. Advanced provides cloud-based ERP, finance, HR, payroll, and vertical market software to UK public sector agencies, mid-market businesses, healthcare organizations, and charities. The company is headquartered in Birmingham, England.
investments.end_customers: Customers span UK local government, NHS trusts, housing associations, charities, and mid-market commercial businesses that rely on Advanced's software for finance, payroll, and operational management. The public sector and not-for-profit concentration makes Advanced's revenue particularly sticky, as switching costs and procurement cycles in those segments are long.

investments.company: Auctane, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of mailing and shipping solutions
investments.business_description: Auctane (formerly Stamps.com) is the world's leading e-commerce shipping software platform, acquired by Thoma Bravo in October 2021 for $6.6 billion. The company operates a portfolio of shipping brands — ShipStation, Stamps.com, Endicia, ShippingEasy, ShipEngine, ShipWorks, Packlink, and Metapack — that together serve consumers, small businesses, and enterprise shippers with access to discounted carrier rates across 350+ carriers and integrations with 300+ e-commerce platforms. Thoma Bravo is combining Auctane with WWEX Group to form a global logistics leader.
investments.end_customers: Auctane's customers range from individual sellers on eBay and Etsy to mid-market DTC brands and large enterprise shippers, all of whom use its software to print approved carrier labels, compare rates, manage returns, and track shipments. The multi-carrier rate access and marketplace integrations make Auctane's platforms the default shipping layer for a large segment of U.S. e-commerce.

investments.company: Banyan Software Holdings, LLC and Banyan Software Intermediate, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Vertical software businesses holding company
investments.business_description: Banyan Software is a permanent-hold acquirer of vertical market software (VMS) businesses, modeled after Constellation Software. Backed by Norwest Venture Partners, Ares Management, and Rock Creek Capital, Banyan acquires and operates niche SaaS and legacy software companies that serve specialized industries — including healthcare, legal, construction, and professional services — providing them with a permanent home rather than a time-limited PE exit. The company has made more than 40 acquisitions and raised over $2.5 billion.
investments.end_customers: Each Banyan portfolio company serves a distinct vertical — for example, OR Trax serves surgical services teams, while other holdings serve law firms, contractors, or specialty trade businesses. The common thread is that each software product is mission-critical within its niche, creating durable customer retention and predictable recurring revenue.

investments.company: BCTO Ignition Purchaser, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Enterprise software provider
investments.business_description: BCTO Ignition Purchaser, Inc. is the acquisition vehicle used by Bain Capital Tech Opportunities (BCTO) for an investment in a software company named Ignition or operating under the 'Ignition' brand. The most likely target is Ignition (getignition.com / ignitionapp.com), an Australian-founded proposal and engagement automation platform for accounting and professional services firms that reached approximately $46.7 million in ARR in 2024. Ignition automates client proposals, engagement letters, payment collection, and recurring billing for accounting practices.
investments.end_customers: Ignition's customers are accounting firms, bookkeepers, and professional services businesses that use the platform to send digital proposals, obtain client signatures, and collect payments automatically. The platform reduces time spent on administrative tasks and accelerates cash conversion for practices of all sizes.

investments.company: Bobcat Purchaser, LLC and Bobcat Topco, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Healthcare software provider
investments.business_description: Bobcat Purchaser, LLC and Bobcat Topco, L.P. are Thoma Bravo-created acquisition and holding entities for a software company that appears in ARCC's portfolio with both first-lien debt and equity (Class A-1 units). Thoma Bravo regularly uses animal-named entities (Bobcat, Cheetah, etc.) for its buyout vehicles. Given the Tallahassee, Florida registration and Thoma Bravo's portfolio, the underlying company may be a government technology or compliance software business; the exact operating company name has not been confirmed in public sources.
investments.end_customers: Consistent with Thoma Bravo's software investment thesis, the underlying business likely serves mid-market or enterprise customers with a mission-critical SaaS product, high net revenue retention, and durable switching costs.

investments.company: Borrower R365 Holdings LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of restaurant enterprise resource planning systems
investments.business_description: Borrower R365 Holdings LLC is the holding structure for Restaurant365, a cloud-native, restaurant-specific ERP platform backed by KKR, L Catterton, and ICONIQ Growth. Restaurant365 integrates accounting, inventory management, scheduling, payroll, and operations analytics into a single platform purpose-built for restaurant groups and franchisors. The company serves tens of thousands of restaurant locations across the U.S. and is headquartered in Irvine, California.
investments.end_customers: Customers are multi-unit restaurant operators and franchisors — from regional chains to large enterprise groups — that need an integrated back-office system designed for the restaurant industry's specific workflows (food cost management, recipe costing, labor law compliance, franchise royalty tracking). Restaurant365 replaces a patchwork of QuickBooks, Excel, and generic HR tools with one purpose-built system.

investments.company: Bottomline Technologies, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of payment automation solutions
investments.business_description: Bottomline Technologies is a cloud-based financial technology company acquired by Thoma Bravo in January 2022 for approximately $2.6 billion. The company provides business payment solutions including digital banking platforms, business-to-business payment networks, fraud and financial crime management, and healthcare payment automation. Bottomline processes trillions of dollars in payment flows annually for banks, corporations, and healthcare organizations. It is headquartered in Portsmouth, New Hampshire.
investments.end_customers: Customers include commercial banks offering digital payment services to business clients, large corporations managing accounts payable automation, healthcare payers and providers processing insurance transactions, and financial institutions using Bottomline's fraud detection capabilities. The software is embedded in bank back-offices and corporate treasury systems, creating high switching costs.

investments.company: Businessolver.com, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of SaaS-based benefits solutions for employers and employees
investments.business_description: Businessolver is a cloud-based benefits administration technology platform backed by Stone Point Capital, Warburg Pincus, and JMI Equity. The company provides employers with a SaaS platform to manage employee benefits enrollment, administration, and communication, including an AI-powered benefits assistant called Sofia that helps employees navigate their options. Businessolver serves millions of employees across hundreds of employers and is headquartered in Des Moines, Iowa.
investments.end_customers: Customers are mid-market to enterprise employers across industries including healthcare, financial services, retail, and manufacturing. HR departments and benefits managers use Businessolver to simplify open enrollment, reduce benefits administration costs, and improve employee engagement with their benefits package.

investments.company: Cardinal Parent, Inc. and Packers Software Intermediate Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Cardinal Parent is the holding structure for Zywave, a Milwaukee-based insurance technology platform that provides software to insurance brokers, agencies, carriers, and HCM service providers. Zywave's platform includes agency management systems, benefits quoting tools, client engagement and compliance content, and data analytics. The company has grown through acquisitions including Insurance Technologies Corporation (ITC) and Strategic Insurance Software (SIS), and serves more than 6,000 insurance firms globally including all top 100 U.S. insurance companies. Accel-KKR was an investor in ITC prior to its sale to Zywave.
investments.end_customers: End customers are P&C and employee benefits insurance brokers and agencies that use Zywave to manage client relationships, generate quotes, distribute compliance content, and run agency operations. The company is deeply embedded in the insurance distribution workflow, with all top 100 U.S. insurance firms as clients.

investments.end_customers: Consistent with Haveli's investment criteria, the underlying company's customers are likely businesses within an enterprise software vertical with high renewal rates, multi-year contracts, and strong customer concentration around mission-critical use cases.

investments.business_description: CentralSquare Technologies is the leading provider of software to U.S. public safety and local government agencies, created in 2018 through the Bain Capital and Vista Equity Partners-backed merger of Superion, TriTech, Zuercher Technologies, and Aptean's public sector division. Its product suite covers 911 call handling, computer-aided dispatch (CAD), records management systems (RMS), jail management, finance, HR, payroll, utility billing, and community development for more than 8,000 government agencies. The company is headquartered in Lake Mary, Florida.
investments.end_customers: Customers are state and local government agencies — police and fire departments, 911 dispatch centers, courts, jails, and city finance offices — that depend on CentralSquare's software for mission-critical public safety and administrative operations. The public-sector customer base creates highly sticky, long-term contract relationships with predictable renewal dynamics.

investments.company: Computer Services, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Infrastructure software provider to community banks
investments.business_description: Computer Services, Inc. (CSI) is a fintech and regtech software and services company serving community and mid-size banks, credit unions, and other financial institutions. Centerbridge Partners and Bridgeport Partners took CSI private in 2022 for approximately $1.6 billion. CSI provides core banking systems, digital banking platforms, cybersecurity, compliance management, and payment processing to roughly 3,000 financial institutions across the U.S. The company is headquartered in Paducah, Kentucky.
investments.end_customers: Customers are community banks, savings institutions, and credit unions that rely on CSI's core banking software and adjacent services for deposit processing, loan origination, online banking, and regulatory compliance reporting. CSI's bank-specific software is deeply integrated into customer operations, making it a classic sticky, recurring-revenue business.

investments.company: CoreLogic, Inc. and T-VIII Celestial Co-Invest LP
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: CoreLogic is the leading U.S. property data and analytics platform, taken private by Stone Point Capital and Insight Partners in 2021 for approximately $6 billion. The company aggregates data on virtually every U.S. property — covering ownership, valuation, tax assessments, liens, flood risk, and mortgage history — and delivers analytics, workflow tools, and data services to mortgage lenders, insurers, real estate professionals, and government agencies. CoreLogic is headquartered in Irvine, California.
investments.end_customers: Customers include major U.S. mortgage originators and servicers, property and casualty insurers, real estate agents and brokers, appraisers, and government housing agencies that rely on CoreLogic data for property valuation, mortgage underwriting, flood risk assessment, and fraud detection. CoreLogic's near-universal property data coverage makes it an essential infrastructure provider across the U.S. real estate ecosystem.

investments.company: Cority Software Inc., Cority Software (USA) Inc., and Cority Parent, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of environmental, health and safety software to track compliance data
investments.business_description: Cority is a Thoma Bravo-backed enterprise SaaS platform for environmental, health, safety, and quality (EHSQ) management, serving large industrial and manufacturing companies globally. The company's software manages occupational health records, industrial hygiene monitoring, safety incident reporting, environmental compliance, and quality management programs. Cority is headquartered in Toronto, Ontario, Canada, and serves customers across mining, oil and gas, chemicals, automotive, and utilities.
investments.end_customers: Customers are large multinational manufacturers, energy producers, and industrial companies with significant EHS regulatory obligations that use Cority to track incidents, manage compliance workflows, and report to regulatory bodies. The software is deeply embedded in EHS operations and compliance programs, making it critical infrastructure for regulated industrial businesses.

investments.company: Cornerstone OnDemand, Inc. and Sunshine Software Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of a cloud-based, SaaS platform for talent management
investments.business_description: Cornerstone OnDemand is a cloud-based talent management and learning management software platform taken private by Clearlake Capital in 2021 for approximately $5.2 billion. The company's suite includes learning management (LMS), performance management, recruiting, and workforce planning tools that large enterprises use to develop and manage their employees. Cornerstone serves approximately 7,000 customers with over 100 million users in 180+ countries and is headquartered in Santa Monica, California.
investments.end_customers: Customers are large enterprises and government agencies across financial services, healthcare, retail, and manufacturing that need a unified platform to manage employee training compliance, performance reviews, succession planning, and talent development. Cornerstone's scale and global compliance library make it particularly valuable for regulated industries and multinationals managing complex workforce development requirements.

investments.business_description: Coupa Software is a leading business spend management (BSM) platform taken private by Thoma Bravo in February 2023 for approximately $8 billion. investments.end_customers: Customers are Global 2000 enterprises — including major Fortune 500 companies in technology, financial services, manufacturing, and retail — that use Coupa to manage and optimize their enterprise purchasing, supplier relationships, and corporate spend. The network effects of the Coupa Business Spend platform, which connects buyers with millions of suppliers, create significant competitive moats.

investments.company: Databricks, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Cloud-based data and AI platform that helps companies scale and manage data
investments.business_description: Databricks is the leading cloud-native data intelligence platform that unifies data engineering, data science, machine learning, and analytics on an open-source lakehouse architecture (Apache Spark, Delta Lake, MLflow). The company provides a platform for large enterprises to ingest, process, and analyze massive datasets and build AI/ML models, competing directly with Snowflake in the data warehouse and analytics market. Databricks is VC/growth-backed (Andreessen Horowitz, NVIDIA, Google, and others) at a valuation of over $60 billion and is headquartered in San Francisco. ARCC's debt position reflects a late-stage private credit facility in advance of an anticipated IPO.
investments.end_customers: Customers are data engineering and data science teams at large technology, financial services, healthcare, and media companies that need to process petabyte-scale datasets, build ML models, and run analytics across structured and unstructured data. Databricks' open-source foundation and multi-cloud support make it appealing to enterprises avoiding vendor lock-in.

investments.company: Datix Bidco Limited and RL Datix Holdings (USA), Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Datix Bidco Limited is the holding structure for RLDatix, the world's largest software company exclusively dedicated to patient safety, healthcare quality, and governance, risk, and compliance (GRC). RLDatix was formed in 2018 through the merger of RL Solutions and Datix, and was subsequently backed by Nordic Capital (which invested in January 2022), Five Arrows, and TA Associates. The company's platform covers incident reporting, risk management, infection surveillance, peer review, claims management, and workforce scheduling for healthcare organizations across 19 countries. Hg Capital was a previous investor in Allocate Software, which RLDatix acquired.
investments.end_customers: Customers are hospitals, health systems, NHS trusts, and healthcare networks globally that use RLDatix to manage patient safety incidents, comply with accreditation requirements, track infection rates, and manage clinical governance. The regulatory mandate around incident reporting makes the software essential infrastructure for accredited healthcare organizations.

investments.company: Digicert, Inc., Dcert Buyer, Inc., DCert Preferred Holdings, Inc. and Destiny Digital Holdings, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Provider of internet security tools and solutions
investments.business_description: DigiCert is the world's leading provider of digital trust solutions, including TLS/SSL certificates, code signing certificates, IoT device security, and public key infrastructure (PKI) management. The company serves millions of websites and enterprise customers globally, securing billions of connections per day. Clearlake Capital, TA Associates, and CrossPoint Capital are co-investors following Clearlake's acquisition of a majority stake from Thoma Bravo. DigiCert is headquartered in Lehi, Utah.
investments.end_customers: Customers range from large enterprises and cloud providers managing complex certificate lifecycles to SMBs securing their websites. Industries with particularly heavy usage include financial services, healthcare, government, and e-commerce, where TLS certificate failures create regulatory and reputational risk. DigiCert's Certificate Manager platform is used by enterprises to automate certificate issuance and renewal at scale.

investments.company: DriveCentric Holdings, LLC

investments.company: Elemica Parent, Inc. & EZ Elemica Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: SaaS based supply chain management software provider focused on chemical markets
investments.business_description: Elemica is a supply chain network and digital integration platform focused on process industries including chemicals, life sciences, energy, and food and beverage. The company provides a multi-enterprise B2B connectivity hub that automates order management, logistics, and supplier collaboration across complex global supply chains. Eurazeo acquired Elemica to grow the platform internationally and expand into adjacent verticals.
investments.end_customers: Customers are large enterprise manufacturers and distributors in chemicals, specialty materials, and related process industries who need to exchange electronic transactions (purchase orders, invoices, shipment notices) with hundreds or thousands of trading partners. They use Elemica because it eliminates manual EDI mapping, reduces order errors, and provides real-time supply chain visibility across the extended enterprise.

investments.company: EP Purchaser, LLC and TPG VIII EP Co-Invest II, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Provider of entertainment workforce and production management solutions
investments.business_description: EP Purchaser, LLC is the acquisition vehicle through which TPG Capital acquired Entertainment Partners (EP), the leading global provider of production payroll, workforce management, and automated production accounting software and services for the entertainment industry. EP processes payroll for hundreds of thousands of crew, cast, and background performers on film, television, and streaming productions. TPG completed the acquisition in 2019.
investments.end_customers: Customers are film studios, television networks, streaming platforms, and independent production companies that need to process complex entertainment industry payroll — governed by guild and union contracts — as well as track production costs and manage production accounting. EP is effectively the industry standard for studio payroll, giving it a deeply embedded, mission-critical role in the production workflow.

investments.company: eResearchTechnology, Inc. and Astorg VII Co-Invest ERT
investments.investment_type: Provider of mission-critical, software-enabled clinical research solutions
investments.business_description: eResearchTechnology (ERT) is a global clinical research technology company that provides cardiac safety services (central ECG reading and analysis), eClinical solutions (ePRO/eCOA patient-reported outcomes), and respiratory function testing to biopharmaceutical and medical device companies running clinical trials. The company operates from a network of specialized laboratories and a global field technology infrastructure. Nordic Capital and Astorg are co-owners of the business.
investments.end_customers: Customers are pharmaceutical, biotechnology, and medical device companies running Phase I–IV clinical trials who require FDA-compliant cardiac safety data, patient-reported outcome endpoints, and pulmonary function assessments. ERT's services are mandated by trial protocols and regulatory requirements, making them a non-discretionary, embedded part of the clinical development process.

investments.company: Extrahop Networks, Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: ExtraHop is a network detection and response (NDR) cybersecurity company that uses real-time stream analytics on network traffic to detect threats, investigate incidents, and respond to cyberattacks across enterprise, cloud, and OT environments. Its Reveal(x) platform decrypts and analyzes network packets at wire speed to provide behavioral analytics and threat intelligence without requiring agents on endpoints. Bain Capital took ExtraHop private in 2021.
investments.end_customers: Customers are large enterprises and government agencies — particularly in financial services, healthcare, energy, and technology — that need deep visibility into their networks to detect advanced threats, ransomware, and insider activity that evade perimeter and endpoint controls. Security operations teams choose ExtraHop because it provides full network visibility without the complexity of deploying agents across every device.

investments.business_description: Finastra is one of the world's largest financial technology companies, formed from the 2017 merger of Misys and D+H. It provides a broad suite of banking software platforms covering retail and commercial lending, payments, treasury and capital markets, and core banking to financial institutions globally. Vista Equity Partners owns the company and has been supporting Finastra's transformation toward a cloud-native, open-platform model through its FusionFabric.cloud ecosystem.
investments.end_customers: Customers are banks, credit unions, building societies, and other financial institutions — from global tier-1 banks to community lenders — across more than 130 countries. investments.company: Flexera Software LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Flexera is a software asset management (SAM) and IT asset management (ITAM) platform that helps enterprises manage their software licenses, optimize technology spend, and gain visibility into hybrid IT environments. Its products include FlexNet Manager Suite for license compliance and optimization, and Technopedia, an IT product catalog. Thoma Bravo has owned Flexera since 2008 and has built it through multiple acquisitions into a comprehensive technology spend management platform.

IT finance, procurement, and compliance teams choose Flexera to reduce overspending on software and avoid costly audit penalties from vendors like Microsoft, Oracle, and IBM.

investments.company: Forescout Technologies, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Network access control solutions provider
investments.business_description: Forescout is a cybersecurity company specializing in device visibility and network access control (NAC), providing continuous automated asset discovery and policy enforcement across IT, OT, and IoT environments without requiring endpoint agents. Its platform identifies and classifies every connected device in real time and can automatically quarantine non-compliant or compromised devices. Advent International took Forescout private in 2020.
investments.end_customers: Customers are enterprise organizations — particularly in critical infrastructure sectors such as energy, manufacturing, healthcare, and government — that operate complex networks with thousands of diverse and unmanaged devices, including industrial control systems and medical equipment. They use Forescout because traditional agent-based security cannot cover unmanageable OT and IoT assets.

investments.company: GHP-VGS Purchaser LLC

Vector Solutions is a SaaS provider of training management, compliance training, continuing education, workforce scheduling, and safety management software serving approximately 31 million users across industries including public safety, utilities, healthcare, and education. Genstar acquired Vector Solutions from Golden Gate Capital in 2021 with co-investment from Insight Partners.
investments.end_customers: Customers are organizations in regulated industries — including fire departments, law enforcement agencies, utilities, construction companies, and healthcare organizations — that need to track and enforce workforce training requirements, manage CE credits, and demonstrate compliance with safety regulations. investments.company: GI Ranger Intermediate LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of payment processing services and software to healthcare providers
investments.business_description: GI Ranger Intermediate LLC is the holding entity through which GI Partners owns Euna Solutions (formerly GTY Technology Holdings), a public-sector cloud software company providing budgeting, procurement, grants management, payments, and permitting solutions to state and local governments in North America. GI Partners acquired GTY Technology Holdings in a July 2022 go-private transaction at $6.30 per share, and the company subsequently rebranded as Euna Solutions in May 2023. The platform serves government agencies through six specialized product lines including Bonfire (procurement), Questica (budgeting), and eCivis (grants).
investments.end_customers: Customers are state, county, and municipal government agencies, as well as school districts and other public entities, that must manage procurement transparency, budget preparation, and federal grant compliance. They purchase Euna's software because the procurement and budget workflows are subject to statutory requirements that make purpose-built government solutions far less risky than generic ERP customizations.

investments.company: HS Purchaser, LLC, and Help/Systems Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of IT operations management and cybersecurity software
investments.business_description: HS Purchaser, LLC and HelpSystems Holdings represent the holding structure for HelpSystems (now rebranded as Fortra), a cybersecurity and IT automation software platform assembled through over 30 acquisitions by TA Associates and partner investors. Fortra's portfolio covers data security (data loss prevention, email security, encryption), vulnerability management, and IT automation (robotic process automation, job scheduling). TA Associates backed the original HelpSystems platform starting in 2019, and the company rebranded as Fortra in 2022 to reflect its security focus.
investments.end_customers: Customers are mid-market and enterprise organizations that use Fortra's tools for secure file transfer, data protection, phishing simulation, email filtering, and IT process automation. IT security teams and administrators adopt these tools to reduce manual processes, maintain data compliance, and defend against phishing and data exfiltration without requiring a large in-house security team.

investments.company: Huskies Parent, Inc., GI Insurity Parent LLC and GI Insurity TopCo LP
investments.investment_type: Insurance software provider
investments.business_description: This holding structure represents Insurity, Inc., a Hartford, CT-based provider of cloud-based policy administration, billing, claims, and analytics software for property and casualty (P&C) insurance carriers, managing general agents, and third-party administrators. GI Partners acquired Insurity in 2019 from TA Associates, General Atlantic, and Genstar Capital, and later brought in Warburg Pincus as a co-investor. Insurity serves more than 200 insurance organizations with both on-premise and multi-tenant SaaS deployments.
investments.end_customers: Customers are specialty, commercial, and personal lines P&C insurance carriers and managing general agents that need core systems for underwriting, policy issuance, billing, and claims management. Insurers choose Insurity because its configurable platform can accommodate complex coverage structures and rate tables without extensive custom development, reducing the time to market for new products.

investments.company: Hyland Software, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Enterprise content management software provider
investments.business_description: Hyland Software is an enterprise content management (ECM) and intelligent automation platform provider, best known for its OnBase platform, which helps organizations capture, manage, and automate unstructured content and business processes. The company serves healthcare, financial services, insurance, government, and higher education verticals, and has expanded through acquisitions including Alfresco and Nuxeo. Thoma Bravo has owned Hyland since 2007 and has grown it into a major ECM platform through consolidation.
investments.end_customers: Customers are large organizations — hospitals, banks, insurers, and government agencies — that need to digitize paper-intensive workflows such as accounts payable, claims processing, student records, and case management. OnBase is deeply integrated into back-office operations, making it a mission-critical system with extremely high switching costs.

investments.company: Hyphen Solutions, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of residential construction supply chain management software
investments.business_description: Hyphen Solutions provides cloud-based supply chain and construction management software specifically for the residential homebuilding industry. Its flagship products include BuildPro (residential construction management for home builders) and SupplyPro (supply chain collaboration for trades, suppliers, and manufacturers), collectively enabling real-time communication, purchase order management, and scheduling across the entire residential construction supply chain. Hyphen claims to support construction of approximately 1 in 3 homes built in America. Thoma Bravo acquired the company to build on its dominant position in homebuilder software.
investments.end_customers: Customers are production homebuilders ranging from large national builders (such as D.R. Horton, Lennar, and PulteGroup) to regional operators, as well as the trades, subcontractors, and material suppliers that serve them. Builders use Hyphen to improve cycle time, reduce purchase order errors, and gain real-time visibility into job site progress and supply chain commitments.

investments.company: Icefall Parent, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of customer engagement software and integrated payments solutions
investments.business_description: Icefall Parent, Inc. is the Vista Equity Partners acquisition vehicle that took EngageSmart private in January 2024 in a transaction valued at approximately $4.0 billion at $23.00 per share. EngageSmart is a vertically tailored customer engagement and integrated payments SaaS platform serving healthcare, government, utilities, and nonprofits through products including SimplePractice (behavioral health practice management), InvoiceCloud (government and utility bill payment), and DonorDrive (peer-to-peer fundraising).
investments.end_customers: Customers span multiple verticals: behavioral health practitioners use SimplePractice to manage appointments, billing, and telehealth; local government agencies and utilities use InvoiceCloud to accept electronic payments from residents; and nonprofits use DonorDrive to run online fundraising campaigns. In each case, the platform replaces manual, paper-based, or legacy billing workflows with integrated digital payment experiences.

investments.company: ID.me, LLC and ID.me, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of remote digital identity verification solutions
investments.business_description: ID.me is a digital identity verification and credentialing platform that enables individuals to verify their identity once and share verified credentials across government agencies, healthcare systems, and commercial enterprises. The platform uses a combination of document verification, biometric matching, and video review to comply with NIST identity assurance standards, and is heavily used by federal and state government agencies including the IRS, Social Security Administration, and VA to reduce identity fraud. Viking Global and T. Rowe Price are among its major growth investors.
investments.end_customers: Customers are federal agencies, state government portals, healthcare systems, and large employers that must verify the identity of individuals seeking access to benefits, tax records, medical records, or secure online services. Government agencies choose ID.me as an alternative to building their own identity verification infrastructure, particularly after Social Security Administration and IRS mandated its use for online access to taxpayer accounts.

investments.company: Internet Truckstop Group LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of freight-moving lifecycle software
investments.business_description: Internet Truckstop Group, operating as Truckstop.com, is one of the largest digital freight matching and load board platforms in North America, connecting shippers, freight brokers, and carriers to post and find available loads in real time. Founded in 1995 as the first load board on the internet, Truckstop has expanded to offer transportation management, rate negotiation tools, cargo insurance, factoring, and compliance tools across the full freight lifecycle. Warburg Pincus invested in the company to support continued growth.
investments.end_customers: Customers are freight brokers, carriers (primarily owner-operators and small trucking companies), and shippers seeking transparent freight rate benchmarks and counterparty discovery. Brokers post loads to attract carriers quickly; carriers search for backhauls and empty miles optimization. Truckstop's large network creates a marketplace flywheel where more participants attract more participants.

investments.company: IQN Holding Corp.
investments.investment_type: Provider of extended workforce management software
investments.business_description: IQN Holding Corp. is the holding entity for Beeline (formerly IQNavigator / IQN), a leading vendor management system (VMS) and extended workforce management platform that helps large enterprises manage, source, and pay their contingent workforce including contractors, freelancers, and statement-of-work project labor. Beeline was formed through the 2016 merger of IQNavigator and Beeline, and New Mountain Capital acquired the combined company in 2018 from the Adecco Group. The platform provides sourcing, on-boarding, time and expense management, and analytics for non-employee labor.
investments.end_customers: Customers are large enterprises in financial services, technology, healthcare, and manufacturing that spend tens to hundreds of millions annually on contingent labor and need a centralized system of record to manage program compliance, spend visibility, and worker risk. Procurement and HR teams use Beeline to enforce preferred supplier lists, track contractor engagements, and maintain co-employment risk controls.

investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)

investments.company: JAMS Holdings LP and Jams Buyer LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of IT operations management and cybersecurity software
investments.business_description: JAMS Holdings LP and Jams Buyer LLC form the TowerBrook Capital Partners-backed holding structure for a business productivity or enterprise software company; the 'JAMS' acronym and its presence in ARCC's Software and Services portfolio point to a niche B2B software platform, likely in the field service, job management, or workflow automation space. The specific operating company underlying this entity is not publicly disclosed in available sources at the time of this research.
investments.end_customers: Based on the software and services classification and TowerBrook's portfolio focus, likely customers are businesses or government organizations needing workflow, scheduling, or operational management software for service-oriented or administrative functions.

investments.company: Jeppesen Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of aviation software solutions
investments.business_description: Jeppesen Holdings, LLC is the Thoma Bravo-owned holding entity for Jeppesen ForeFlight, the combined aviation navigation, charting, and digital operations platform created when Thoma Bravo acquired Jeppesen, ForeFlight, AerData, and OzRunways from Boeing in a $10.55 billion transaction that closed in November 2025. Jeppesen has a 90-year heritage as the global standard for aeronautical navigation charts and instrument procedures, while ForeFlight is the leading electronic flight bag (EFB) app for general aviation and commercial pilots.
investments.end_customers: Customers span the full spectrum of aviation: general aviation pilots use ForeFlight for flight planning and weather; commercial airlines use Jeppesen charts and navigation databases that are embedded in flight management systems; business aviation operators use the platform for trip planning and crew scheduling; and military customers use the systems for mission planning. The aeronautical data and chart products are safety-critical, making switching costs extremely high.

investments.company: Kairos Bidco Limited
investments.investment_type: Provider of financial data analytics software
investments.business_description: Kairos Bidco Limited is the TA Associates acquisition vehicle used to take FD Technologies plc (formerly First Derivatives) private in a £570 million transaction completed in 2025. FD Technologies is a Northern Ireland-based software and consulting company best known for its KX platform — a high-performance time-series database and analytics engine widely used by global investment banks, hedge funds, and quantitative trading firms to process massive volumes of market data at extremely low latency.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are tier-1 global investment banks, hedge funds, and quantitative trading firms that process billions of financial events per day and require microsecond-latency analytics on streaming market data. KX is embedded in trading infrastructure at many of the world's largest financial institutions, and its performance advantages at extreme data volumes make it difficult to displace.

investments.company: LeanTaaS Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of SaaS tools to optimize healthcare asset utilization
investments.business_description: LeanTaaS is an AI-powered hospital capacity management and scheduling software company whose iQueue platform helps health systems optimize the utilization of operating rooms, infusion centers, and inpatient beds. The software applies machine learning and prescriptive analytics to forecast demand, level-load patient flow, manage surgical block time, and reduce wait times for expensive clinical resources. LeanTaaS has received growth investment from Bain Capital Ventures and Goldman Sachs.
investments.end_customers: Customers are health systems, academic medical centers, and large multi-site hospital networks that face operational bottlenecks in their highest-cost care settings — particularly operating rooms and infusion centers. Hospital executives and perioperative leaders adopt LeanTaaS to reduce first-case delays, improve OR block utilization, and increase patient throughput without adding physical capacity.

investments.company: Majesco and Magic Topco, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Insurance software provider
investments.business_description: Majesco (operating under the Magic Topco holding structure created by Thoma Bravo) is a global cloud insurance software company providing core systems — including policy administration, billing, claims management, and distribution management — to life, annuity, and property and casualty insurance carriers. Thoma Bravo completed its $729 million acquisition of Majesco in September 2020, taking it private from the Nasdaq. The company serves insurers of all sizes with a modern, cloud-native platform designed to accelerate digital transformation.
investments.end_customers: Customers are insurance carriers across life and annuity, group benefits, and property and casualty lines that need to modernize legacy policy administration systems to support new product launches, digital distribution channels, and regulatory requirements. Carriers choose Majesco for its pre-built insurance content and cloud deployment model, which reduces implementation time compared to building on a generic platform.

investments.company: Merit Software Finance Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Vertical software businesses holding company
investments.business_description: Merit Software Finance Holdings, LLC is a Thoma Bravo-related holding entity for a financial software platform; based on the 'Finance' designation and Thoma Bravo sponsorship, the underlying operating business likely provides specialized financial management, lending, or credit-related software to financial institutions or corporate finance teams. The specific operating company is not fully identified in publicly available sources at the time of this research.
investments.end_customers: Based on the sponsor profile and 'Finance' designation, likely customers are financial institutions, lenders, or corporate treasury teams using specialized software for lending origination, financial reporting, or credit management workflows.

investments.company: Metatiedot Bidco OY and Metatiedot US, LLC
investments.investment_type: Enterprise content management platform
investments.business_description: Metatiedot Bidco OY is the Finnish acquisition vehicle (Bidco) used by Nordic Capital for a software investment in Finland; 'Metatiedot' is Finnish for 'metadata' or 'master data,' and the structure is consistent with Nordic Capital's active technology and payments investment strategy in the Nordic region. Nordic Capital has a Helsinki office and has backed numerous Scandinavian software businesses. The specific underlying operating company is not publicly identified in available sources.
investments.end_customers: Given Nordic Capital's focus on financial technology, healthcare technology, and B2B software in the Nordic region, likely customers are financial institutions, healthcare organizations, or enterprise businesses in Finland and the broader Nordic market using specialized data management or enterprise software.

investments.company: Mimecast Borrowerco, Inc. and Magnesium Co- Invest SCSp
investments.investment_type: Cybersecurity solutions provider
investments.business_description: Mimecast is a cybersecurity company specializing in email security, threat protection, and cyber resilience, providing cloud-native services including advanced threat detection, email archiving, continuity, and security awareness training to protect organizations from phishing, malware, ransomware, and email data loss. Permira took Mimecast private in May 2022 in a transaction valued at approximately $5.8 billion, with Magnesium Co-Invest SCSp as the co-investment vehicle for the deal.
investments.end_customers: Customers are organizations of all sizes — from mid-market businesses to large enterprises — that need to protect their Microsoft 365 or Google Workspace email environments from sophisticated phishing attacks, business email compromise, and accidental data leakage. IT security teams choose Mimecast because it layered on top of native email security controls and provides archiving, continuity, and training in an integrated platform.

investments.company: Ministry Brands Holdings, LLC and RCP MB Investments B, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Software and payment services provider to faith-based institutions
investments.business_description: Ministry Brands is the leading SaaS provider of church management, accounting, giving, background check, messaging, and digital engagement software serving more than 55,000 faith-based organizations in the United States. The company is an aggregator of vertical software for churches and nonprofits, having made dozens of acquisitions to build a comprehensive platform. Insight Partners backed Ministry Brands' growth, and RCP MB Investments B, L.P. represents a related capital structure entity.
investments.end_customers: Customers are churches, parachurch ministries, and faith-based nonprofits of all denominations and sizes that need to manage membership databases, process online donations, run background checks on volunteers, and communicate with their congregations. Ministry Brands' integrated suite is purpose-built for the operational and accounting requirements of religious organizations, which have unique needs around fund accounting and donor management.

investments.company: ML Holdco, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Cloud-based, SaaS lending solutions provider to financial institutions
investments.business_description: Based on the Genstar Capital sponsor and Software and Services classification in ARCC's portfolio, ML Holdco, Inc. is likely the holding entity for a Genstar-backed software business acquired in the 2021–2023 timeframe; Genstar's software portfolio during this period included companies in financial services software, data analytics, and vertical SaaS. The precise operating company behind this holding entity is not confirmed in publicly available sources at the time of this research.
investments.end_customers: Given Genstar's software investment focus areas — financial services, industrials, and healthcare software — likely customers are mid-market or enterprise organizations in regulated industries using a purpose-built vertical software platform.

investments.company: Modernizing Medicine, Inc. and ModMed Software Midco Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of practice management, EHR, and RCM software to medical specialists
investments.business_description: Modernizing Medicine (ModMed) is a healthcare SaaS company providing specialty-specific electronic health records (EHR), practice management, revenue cycle management, and patient engagement software to outpatient specialty physician practices. Its flagship EMA platform uses an adaptive learning engine that pre-populates clinical notes based on physician behavior within each specialty, significantly reducing documentation burden. Warburg Pincus made a $231 million investment in 2017; the company was later acquired by Clearlake Capital in 2025 at a $5.3 billion valuation.
investments.end_customers: Customers are specialty physician practices in dermatology, ophthalmology, orthopedics, gastroenterology, OBGYN, ENT, and other outpatient specialties that need an EHR tailored to their specific clinical workflows and coding requirements. Physicians choose ModMed over general-purpose EHRs because its specialty-specific content and adaptive learning significantly reduce the time physicians spend documenting patient encounters.

investments.company: Motor Vehicle Software Corporation
investments.investment_type: Provider of electronic registration and titling software to auto dealers
investments.business_description: Motor Vehicle Software Corporation (MVSC), which operates primarily as Vitu, is the largest private provider of electronic vehicle registration and titling services in the United States, providing DMVdesk software that processes vehicle title and registration transactions through electronic dealer and government connectivity. The platform enables automotive dealers, fleet operators, and financial institutions to process registration and title paperwork electronically rather than through paper-based DMV processes. Accel-KKR made a growth equity investment in 2015, and MVSC subsequently expanded and rebranded its consumer-facing services as Vitu, which in 2024 agreed to acquire Cox Automotive's Dealertrack registration and titling businesses.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are automotive dealerships that need to process vehicle registrations and title transfers on behalf of car buyers efficiently and compliantly, and state DMV agencies that partner with MVSC to provide electronic registration services. The software eliminates the need for dealers to hand-process paper title and registration documents, reducing errors and accelerating the customer delivery experience.

investments.company: MRI Software LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of real estate and investment management software
investments.business_description: MRI Software is a leading global provider of real estate management software serving commercial property owners, operators, and investors across the full property lifecycle — including lease accounting (ASC 842 and IFRS 16 compliance), property management, facilities management, investment management, and residential property management. The company has grown through aggressive acquisitions, including ANGEL, Springboard, and Palace Software, and serves more than 17,000 clients in over 170 countries. TA Associates, GI Partners, and Harvest Partners are co-investors in the business.
investments.end_customers: Customers include commercial real estate investment trusts (REITs), institutional property investors, property management companies, corporate occupiers, and residential landlords that need software to manage lease obligations, track property financials, automate workflows, and comply with lease accounting standards. MRI's deep integration into property accounting workflows creates high switching costs.

The company has grown through aggressive acquisitions, including ANGEL, Springboard, and Palace Software, and serves more than 17,000 clients in over 170 countries. TA Associates, GI Partners, and Harvest Partners are co-investors in the business.
investments.end_customers: Customers include commercial real estate investment trusts (REITs), institutional property investors, property management companies, corporate occupiers, and residential landlords that need software to manage lease obligations, track property financials, automate workflows, and comply with lease accounting standards. MRI's deep integration into property accounting workflows creates high switching costs.

investments.company: Netsmart, Inc. and Netsmart Technologies, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Developer and operator of health care software and technology solutions
investments.business_description: Netsmart is a leading provider of electronic health record (EHR), care coordination, and analytics software specifically for behavioral health, post-acute care, intellectual and developmental disability (IDD), and social services providers. Its myEvolv and CareRecords platforms serve more than 40,000 care organizations with software that supports clinical documentation, billing, scheduling, outcomes tracking, and interoperability with broader healthcare systems. GI Partners and TA Associates co-own the business.
investments.end_customers: Customers are behavioral health agencies, addiction treatment providers, community mental health centers, home health and hospice agencies, and IDD service providers — organizations that have historically been underserved by mainstream EHR vendors focused on acute care hospitals and physician offices. Netsmart's specialization in the complex billing and clinical requirements of these settings, including Medicaid and mental health-specific regulatory compliance, makes it the dominant platform in this niche.

investments.company: North Star Acquisitionco, LLC and Toucan Bidco Limited
investments.investment_type: Literacy solution software provider for grades k-12
investments.business_description: The operating company is Agilio Software, a UK-based frontline healthcare operations software provider backed by Five Arrows (Rothschild & Co) and TA Associates, with North Star Acquisitionco (US) and Toucan Bidco Limited (UK, incorporated December 2020) forming the acquisition vehicle structure. Agilio offers a comprehensive suite of SaaS products that automate and optimize critical operational functions for healthcare professionals, including workforce management, compliance tracking, and clinical asset management. The company has established itself as a market leader in the UK dental and primary care sectors and has expanded into veterinary practices and continental Europe.
investments.end_customers: Agilio's primary customers are UK dental practices, primary care (GP) surgeries, and veterinary clinics that must meet stringent regulatory compliance requirements. These customers subscribe to Agilio's platform to automate CQC compliance, staff training records, and workforce scheduling, reducing administrative burden and regulatory risk. The platform is mission-critical for practice managers and principals who face significant penalties for non-compliance.

investments.company: Optimizely North America Inc. and Optimizely Sweden Holdings AB
investments.investment_type: Provider of web content management and digital commerce solutions
investments.business_description: Optimizely (formerly Episerver) is a leading digital experience platform (DXP) providing integrated CMS, A/B and multivariate experimentation, content marketing, e-commerce, and customer data capabilities in a single SaaS platform. Originally founded in Sweden in 1994 as Episerver, the company acquired and rebranded around the Optimizely name after Insight Partners-backed Episerver purchased Optimizely in 2020. The combined platform serves enterprises globally and includes AI-powered personalization, omnichannel content management, and conversion rate optimization tools.
investments.end_customers: Optimizely's customers are mid-to-large enterprises across retail, financial services, media, healthcare, and technology that need to manage complex digital content at scale and run continuous product and marketing experiments. Marketers, product managers, and digital experience teams use the platform to improve conversion rates, personalize user journeys, and accelerate content publication. The company counts major global brands among its clients, who value the platform's ability to unify content management and experimentation in one workflow.

investments.company: PCMI Parent, LLC and PCMI Ultimate Holdings, LP
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: PCMI Corporation (the operating entity under PCMI Parent, LLC) is a global SaaS provider of cloud-based contract and claims administration software for the automotive finance and insurance (F&I) industry, backed by Thoma Bravo. Its flagship platform, PCRS, processes end-to-end policy administration, claims processing, and refund payment workflows for third-party administrators, dealers, OEMs, and lenders, and has processed over 89 million contracts and 39 million claims with 140+ integrations. With more than 200 employees across North America, Europe, and Asia, PCMI serves the rapidly growing vehicle service contract and ancillary product administration market.
investments.end_customers: PCMI's end customers are automotive F&I product administrators (TPAs), franchise and independent car dealerships, OEM captive finance arms, and specialty lenders that need a modern, scalable platform to administer vehicle service contracts, GAP insurance, tire and wheel protection, and similar warranty-like products. These buyers are motivated by the need to automate high-volume claims workflows, reduce manual errors, and provide real-time data to dealer partners. The platform is deeply embedded in dealer management system (DMS) ecosystems through its 140+ integrations.

investments.company: PDDS HoldCo, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of cloud-based dental practice management software
investments.business_description: PDDS HoldCo, Inc. is the holding company for Planet DDS, a leading provider of cloud-based dental practice management software serving dental practices and DSOs (dental service organizations) across North America, backed by Aquiline Capital Partners and Level Equity. Planet DDS offers an integrated suite including Denticon (cloud practice management), Apteryx XVWeb (digital dental imaging), and Legwork (patient relationship management and marketing automation), covering the full practice workflow from scheduling through imaging and patient engagement. The company has served the dental industry since 2003 and focuses on enabling both single-location practices and large multi-site DSOs.
investments.end_customers: Planet DDS's customers are dental practices ranging from solo dentist offices to large dental service organizations (DSOs) with hundreds of locations that need cloud-native practice management infrastructure. Office managers and practice owners subscribe to streamline appointment scheduling, billing, digital imaging, and patient communications in one integrated platform rather than stitching together point solutions. DSOs in particular value the multi-location reporting and centralized administration capabilities that support rapid practice consolidation strategies.

investments.company: PDI TA Holdings, Inc., Peachtree Parent, Inc. and Insight PDI Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: PDI Technologies (the operating entity behind these holding companies, owned by Insight Partners, Genstar Capital, and TA Associates) is a leading global provider of enterprise software to the convenience retail, wholesale petroleum, and logistics industries. Founded in 1983 in Alpharetta, Georgia, PDI's platform covers fuel pricing, back-office accounting, loyalty programs, foodservice management, and supply chain software used by thousands of convenience store networks and petroleum distributors worldwide. The company has grown through more than 15 acquisitions including GasBuddy (fuel price data and consumer app) and GreenPrint (environmental compliance).
investments.end_customers: PDI's customers are convenience store chains and independent operators, wholesale petroleum distributors, and logistics companies that need to manage complex fuel supply chains, pricing, inventory, and loyalty programs across large retail networks. Major c-store brands and fuel distributors rely on PDI's back-office and site systems as mission-critical infrastructure for daily operations, including fuel pricing reconciliation and regulatory compliance. The company also serves the millions of consumers who use GasBuddy to find low fuel prices.

investments.company: Petvisor Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of veterinarian-focused SaaS solutions
investments.business_description: Petvisor is a vertical SaaS platform for the veterinary health and pet services industries, serving as the parent company of brands including PetDesk, Vetstoria, WhiskerCloud, Kontak, and Groomer.io, backed by Apax Digital Funds alongside existing investors Frontier Growth and PeakSpan Capital (Diversis Capital was an earlier investor). The platform provides veterinary clinics and pet service businesses with appointment scheduling, two-way client communication, telemedicine, payment processing, digital marketing, and grooming management tools. Petvisor supports over 10,000 veterinary clinics, 400 grooming facilities, and more than 20 million pet parents globally.
investments.end_customers: Petvisor's end customers are independent veterinary clinics, multi-location veterinary groups, and pet grooming businesses that need to improve client communication, reduce no-shows, and build recurring client relationships in a competitive market. Practice owners and office managers use the platform to automate appointment reminders, enable online booking, and run targeted marketing campaigns to reactivate lapsed clients. Pet owners interact through the consumer-facing PetDesk app for appointment management and health records access.

investments.company: Pluralsight, LLC and Pluralsight Holdings, LLC and Paradigmatic Holdco LLC
investments.investment_type: Online education learning platform
investments.business_description: Pluralsight is a cloud-based technology skills development platform with a library of over 7,500 expert-authored courses covering software development, cloud, data science, AI/ML, cybersecurity, and IT operations, originally acquired by Vista Equity Partners for $3.5 billion in 2021 before a financial restructuring transferred control to a lender consortium. Enterprises use Pluralsight Skills to assess workforce technology skill gaps, assign personalized learning paths, and track development progress against business objectives, with features including hands-on labs, skill assessments, virtual instructor-led training, and analytics. The platform also offers Flow (engineering intelligence analytics) to help engineering leaders measure development productivity and team health.
investments.end_customers: Pluralsight's primary customers are enterprise technology organizations — software engineering, DevOps, cloud infrastructure, data, and security teams — that need to upskill and retain technology talent at scale. CIOs, heads of engineering, and L&D leaders use the platform to reduce skill gaps amid rapid technology change, particularly around cloud migration, AI adoption, and cybersecurity. The platform is mission-critical for organizations trying to build internal capability rather than relying entirely on external hiring.

investments.company: Poseidon IntermediateCo, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of practice management software to law firms
investments.business_description: Poseidon IntermediateCo, Inc. is a KKR-backed holding entity for an enterprise software business operating in the Software and Services sector; the underlying operating company provides enterprise software solutions to business customers. Based on available Bloomberg profile data, the entity provides enterprise software solutions, though the specific operating brand and product line are not publicly disclosed under this holding company name. The entity is structured as an intermediate holding company typical of leveraged buyout transactions, sitting between the KKR fund vehicles and the operating software subsidiary.
investments.end_customers: The end customers of the underlying operating business are enterprise organizations that rely on the platform's software for mission-critical business processes; the specific customer profile depends on the operating subsidiary's vertical focus, which has not been publicly confirmed under this holding entity name. Given the BDC first lien loan structure and KKR sponsorship, the business likely serves mid-to-large enterprise customers with high switching costs and recurring subscription revenue.

investments.company: PracticeTek Purchaser, LLC, PracticeTek MidCo, LLC and GSV PracticeTek Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Software provider for medical practitioners
investments.business_description: PracticeTek is one of the largest retail healthcare software platforms in the United States, formed through the merger of the original PracticeTek (backed by Lightyear Capital and Greater Sum Ventures) with Integrated Practice Solutions (IPS, backed by Waud Capital Partners) in September 2023. The company offers practice management, EHR, billing, and patient engagement software purpose-built for dental, orthodontic, chiropractic, optometry, and dermatology specialty practices, serving thousands of independent and group practices. Its software suite includes well-known vertical products such as ChiroTouch (chiropractic), Curve Dental, and related specialty PM platforms.
investments.end_customers: PracticeTek's customers are specialty healthcare practices — chiropractors, dentists, orthodontists, optometrists, and dermatologists — that operate as small businesses and require all-in-one practice management, billing, and patient communication software tailored to their specific specialty workflows. Office managers and practitioners choose PracticeTek's products because they are designed specifically for each specialty's coding, insurance, and clinical workflow requirements rather than generic EHR systems. Group practice owners and DSO-like multi-location organizations value centralized billing and reporting features.
