investments.company: Project Potter Buyer, LLC and Project Potter Parent, L.P.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Project Potter Buyer, LLC and Project Potter Parent, L.P. are the acquisition vehicle entities used by KKR to acquire Potter Global Technologies from Gryphon Investors, a transaction announced in November 2023. Potter Global Technologies is the leading independent designer and manufacturer of life safety and emergency communication solutions, headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri, offering fire suppression systems, fire alarm and signaling products, mass notification systems, first responder RF radio communications, and advanced power products through multiple brand subsidiaries including Potter Electric Signal, Valcom, and others. The company serves the full building safety and emergency communications market from product manufacturing through system integration.
investments.end_customers: Potter Global Technologies' end customers are commercial and institutional building owners, general contractors, fire protection contractors, system integrators, and AHJs (authorities having jurisdiction) who specify, install, and maintain life safety systems in hospitals, schools, government buildings, commercial offices, and industrial facilities. The company sells through a network of independent fire protection contractors and system integrators who install and service Potter-branded fire suppression, notification, and communication products. End-user demand is driven by fire code compliance requirements and building safety regulations.

investments.company: Proofpoint, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Cybersecurity solutions provider
investments.business_description: Proofpoint is a global leader in human- and agent-centric cybersecurity, protecting organizations against email-based threats, data loss, and compliance risks across email, cloud, and collaboration platforms, acquired by Thoma Bravo in 2021 for approximately $12.3 billion in what was then the largest private equity take-private in cybersecurity history. The platform provides enterprise email security, cloud account protection, threat intelligence, security awareness training, data loss prevention (DLP), and e-discovery/archiving, serving over 80 of the Fortune 100 and more than 10,000 large enterprises globally. Proofpoint has expanded its platform through acquisitions including Tessian (AI email security) and Hornetsecurity to address the full spectrum of human-targeted cyberthreats.
investments.end_customers: Proofpoint's customers are large and mid-market enterprises across financial services, healthcare, government, technology, and retail that face significant email-borne threat exposure and regulatory requirements around data protection and e-discovery. CISOs and security operations teams deploy Proofpoint as a primary layer of defense against phishing, business email compromise (BEC), malware, and insider data theft, while compliance and legal teams rely on the archiving and DLP modules for regulatory obligations. The platform's deep integration with Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace makes it a critical add-on security layer for organizations relying on cloud productivity suites.

investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Proofpoint is a global leader in human- and agent-centric cybersecurity, protecting organizations against email-based threats, data loss, and compliance risks across email, cloud, and collaboration platforms, acquired by Thoma Bravo in 2021 for approximately $12.3 billion in what was then the largest private equity take-private in cybersecurity history. The platform provides enterprise email security, cloud account protection, threat intelligence, security awareness training, data loss prevention (DLP), and e-discovery/archiving, serving over 80 of the Fortune 100 and more than 10,000 large enterprises globally. Proofpoint has expanded its platform through acquisitions including Tessian (AI email security) and Hornetsecurity to address the full spectrum of human-targeted cyberthreats.
investments.end_customers: Proofpoint's customers are large and mid-market enterprises across financial services, healthcare, government, technology, and retail that face significant email-borne threat exposure and regulatory requirements around data protection and e-discovery. CISOs and security operations teams deploy Proofpoint as a primary layer of defense against phishing, business email compromise (BEC), malware, and insider data theft, while compliance and legal teams rely on the archiving and DLP modules for regulatory obligations. The platform's deep integration with Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace makes it a critical add-on security layer for organizations relying on cloud productivity suites.

investments.company: QBS Parent, Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: QBS Parent, Inc. is the holding entity for Quorum Software, a leading provider of enterprise software solutions for the oil and gas industry, backed by Francisco Partners (which agreed to acquire Quorum from Thoma Bravo in March 2025). Quorum Software's platform covers the full oil and gas operational and financial value chain, including production accounting, land and royalty management, revenue accounting, well lifecycle management, and joint interest billing (JIB), used by E&P operators, midstream companies, and oil field service providers. The company was formed from the merger of multiple energy software brands and serves thousands of users at hundreds of energy companies globally.
investments.end_customers: Quorum Software's customers are oil and gas exploration and production (E&P) companies, midstream pipeline and processing operators, and oil field service firms that need specialized accounting and operational software built for the unique requirements of the energy industry, including complex royalty calculations, regulatory reporting, and joint venture accounting. Accounting, land, and operations teams at energy companies use Quorum's platform as the system of record for production data, land agreements, and revenue distribution. The platform is particularly entrenched at mid-size independent E&P companies where switching costs are very high.

investments.company: QF Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: SaaS based electronic health record software provider
investments.business_description: QF Holdings, Inc. is a Bain Capital-backed holding company for a software business operating in the healthcare software sector; the underlying operating entity provides software solutions to healthcare organizations, though the specific operating brand has not been publicly identified under this holding company name in available sources. Based on Bloomberg profile data, the entity operates as a holding company delivering healthcare software solutions to clients in the healthcare and financial services adjacencies. The BidCo/HoldCo naming convention is consistent with Bain Capital's typical deal structuring for software platform acquisitions.
investments.end_customers: The end customers of QF Holdings' underlying operating software business are healthcare organizations — likely health plans, providers, or administrative services companies — that rely on the platform for mission-critical data or workflow solutions with high recurring revenue characteristics. Given Bain Capital's focus on healthcare IT, the customer base likely includes payers, health systems, or specialty care providers seeking technology solutions to automate complex administrative or clinical processes.

investments.company: Raptor Technologies, LLC, Sycamore Bidco LTD and Rocket Parent, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of SaaS-based safety and security software to the K-12 school market
investments.business_description: Raptor Technologies is the nation's leading provider of integrated school safety software, acquired by Warburg Pincus from Thoma Bravo in a deal valuing the company at approximately $1.8 billion (announced late 2025), with Sycamore Bidco Ltd and Rocket Parent LLC serving as the international and domestic holding entities. Raptor's platform covers the full school safety lifecycle, including visitor and volunteer management (screening against sex offender registries), emergency response planning, threat assessment case management, student wellness monitoring, and drills management, serving over 60,000 K-12 schools across the U.S. and internationally. JMI Equity retained a minority stake alongside Warburg Pincus in the transaction.
investments.end_customers: Raptor's customers are K-12 public and private school districts and individual campuses that are mandated or highly motivated to implement comprehensive visitor screening and emergency response protocols. District administrators, school principals, and campus security officers use Raptor to comply with state visitor management laws, manage sex offender screenings at entry, coordinate lockdown and evacuation drills, and monitor at-risk student behavior. The platform has high regulatory stickiness given increasing state-level school safety mandates and is deeply embedded in district security operations.

investments.company: Revalize, Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Revalize is a cloud-based software platform for manufacturers and their distribution channels, providing an integrated suite of Configure, Price, Quote (CPQ), product selection, CAD/PLM visualization, and Product Information Management (PIM) tools built specifically for industrial and specialty manufacturers, created by TA Associates and HG Capital in 2021 through the combination of multiple manufacturing software companies. The platform helps 15,000+ customers globally design, configure, quote, and sell over $8 billion in products monthly, spanning markets such as HVAC, pumps and flow control, electrical, foodservice equipment, and specialty industrial goods. Revalize has grown aggressively through acquisitions of vertical-specific software companies serving niche manufacturing end markets.
investments.end_customers: Revalize's customers are manufacturers and their sales channels — independent reps, distributors, and direct sales teams — that sell configurable, engineered-to-order products where accurate quoting requires complex technical configuration rules. Sales engineers, inside sales teams, and channel partners use Revalize's CPQ tools to generate technically valid and priced proposals for custom equipment without requiring direct engineering involvement for every quote. The company's deep vertical focus in industrial equipment means its software is a mission-critical revenue workflow tool for mid-market manufacturers.

investments.company: RMS HoldCo II, LLC & RMS Group Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Based on S&P rating disclosures, RMS HoldCo II, LLC and RMS Group Holdings, Inc. operate as TEAMS, a workforce management and time-and-attendance software company — separate from the Moody's RMS catastrophe modeling business. TEAMS provides cloud-based workforce management software covering time and attendance, scheduling, and HR compliance for employers in sectors including hospitality, retail, and service industries. The company (rated B- by S&P) operates as a PE-backed software business with significant debt, serving clients that require compliant time-tracking and scheduling infrastructure.
investments.end_customers: TEAMS' end customers are employers in labor-intensive industries — including hospitality, food service, retail, and healthcare staffing — that need to manage hourly worker scheduling, track punches accurately for payroll compliance, and handle complex overtime and labor law requirements across multiple locations. HR managers and payroll administrators use the platform to reduce wage theft exposure, automate shift scheduling, and integrate time data with payroll systems. Multi-location restaurant chains and hotel groups are representative core customers.

investments.company: Runway Bidco, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of workload automation software
investments.business_description: Runway Bidco, LLC is the acquisition vehicle for Redwood Software, a leading provider of automation fabric solutions acquired by Vista Equity Partners and Warburg Pincus from Turn/River Capital in a deal announced September 2024. Redwood's platform enables enterprises to compose, orchestrate, and manage business and IT processes across applications, services, and servers, supporting use cases including finance close automation, ERP automation, and enterprise job scheduling across SAP, Oracle, and other major ERP environments. The company serves more than 7,500 customers globally including 28% of the Fortune 500 and 40% of the Fortune 50.
investments.end_customers: Redwood Software's customers are large enterprises — particularly those running complex ERP environments like SAP, Oracle, and PeopleSoft — that need to automate high-volume, scheduled business processes such as financial close, batch reporting, order-to-cash, and supply chain workflows without human intervention. Finance teams, IT operations, and shared services centers use Redwood to eliminate manual batch processing errors and reduce close cycle times. The Fortune 500 customer penetration reflects the platform's positioning as mission-critical operational middleware for large-scale enterprise computing environments.

investments.company: Sapphire Software Buyer, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of application security testing solutions
investments.business_description: Sapphire Software Buyer, Inc. is the acquisition vehicle created by Clearlake Capital Group and Francisco Partners to acquire Synopsys' Software Integrity Group in a $2.1 billion carve-out completed in September 2024, which subsequently rebranded as Black Duck Software. The business provides application security testing (AST) software and services — including static analysis (SAST), software composition analysis (SCA), dynamic analysis (DAST), penetration testing, and security auditing — to software development organizations worldwide. As the largest standalone application security testing company post-carve-out, Black Duck serves enterprises and software vendors that need to identify and remediate security vulnerabilities in their code and open-source dependencies before deployment.
investments.end_customers: Black Duck's customers are enterprise software development and DevSecOps teams at technology companies, financial services firms, automotive manufacturers, and other organizations building or procuring software who must comply with security mandates and software bill of materials (SBOM) requirements. CISOs, application security leads, and DevOps engineers use the platform to embed security scanning into CI/CD pipelines and to assess the security posture of third-party and open-source components. Increasingly, the regulatory push around software supply chain security (e.g., Executive Order 14028) is driving demand among government contractors and critical infrastructure operators.

investments.company: Smarsh Inc. and Skywalker TopCo, LLC
investments.investment_type: SaaS based communication archival service provider
investments.business_description: Smarsh is the global market leader in electronic communications archiving, compliance, and supervision software for regulated industries, created when K1 Investment Management combined Smarsh and Actiance in 2017, with Skywalker TopCo LLC serving as the K1-controlled holding structure. The platform provides comprehensive archiving across email, social media, instant messaging, mobile/text, voice, and collaboration tools (e.g., Slack, Microsoft Teams, Bloomberg), enabling financial services firms, government agencies, and other regulated organizations to meet SEC, FINRA, CFTC, and global supervisory retention requirements. Smarsh serves more than 6,500 financial services firms including the top 15 global banks, and has expanded into AI-driven supervision and surveillance through acquisitions.
investments.end_customers: Smarsh's primary customers are broker-dealers, investment advisers, banks, and other registered financial services firms that are required by FINRA, the SEC, and international regulators to retain, supervise, and produce electronic communications in response to regulatory inquiries and litigation. Compliance officers and e-discovery teams at wealth management firms, hedge funds, and bulge-bracket banks use Smarsh's platform to fulfill supervisory obligations and respond to regulatory examination requests. The company also serves government agencies and healthcare organizations with similar electronic records retention requirements.

investments.company: Spaceship Purchaser, Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Spaceship Purchaser, Inc. is the acquisition vehicle through which Permira took Squarespace, Inc. private in a $7.2 billion transaction completed in October 2024, using a two-step merger structure with Spaceship Group MergerCo as the merger subsidiary. Squarespace is a leading cloud-based website building, hosting, and e-commerce platform enabling businesses and individuals to create professional websites and online stores without coding, offering integrated tools for domain registration, e-commerce (payments, inventory, shipping), email marketing, scheduling, and analytics. The company serves millions of subscribers globally across creative industries, small businesses, and service providers who need a professional web presence.
investments.end_customers: Squarespace's customers are small businesses, freelancers, creative professionals (photographers, designers, musicians), and e-commerce entrepreneurs who need an all-in-one platform to establish and manage their online presence without technical expertise or the cost of custom web development. Pricing is subscription-based with tiered plans, and customers typically stay on the platform long-term as their website becomes a core part of their brand and business operations. The platform is particularly dominant among creative professionals and service businesses (restaurants, salons, fitness studios) seeking an aesthetically polished, easy-to-manage website.

investments.company: Spark Purchaser, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Software platform for Medicare application process
investments.business_description: Spark Purchaser, Inc. is the KKR acquisition vehicle for SunFire (formerly SunFireMatrix), a SaaS technology and tech-enabled services platform serving the Medicare insurance distribution and health plan markets, with KKR investing through its Health Care Strategic Growth Fund II in a transaction announced April 2024. SunFire's core platform provides quoting and enrollment technology enabling insurance brokers to compliantly sell Medicare Advantage, Prescription Drug, and Medicare Supplement plans through a cloud-based system, along with post-enrollment engagement tools including third-party verification and health risk assessments to ensure beneficiary intent and plan activation. Stone Point Capital, the company's prior majority owner, retained a significant minority stake alongside KKR.
investments.end_customers: SunFire's customers are Medicare insurance brokers, field marketing organizations (FMOs), and health plans that need to streamline compliant Medicare plan enrollment in a highly regulated market. Independent insurance agents use SunFire's quoting platform to compare plans side-by-side and complete CMS-compliant enrollments electronically, while health plan clients use the post-enrollment engagement tools to verify enrollee intent and activate benefits, reducing CMS compliance risk. The rapidly growing Medicare Advantage market — driven by baby boomer aging — creates strong secular demand for technology that can support high-volume, compliant enrollment at scale.

investments.company: Spirit RR Holdings, Inc. and Winterfell Co-Invest SCSp
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Spirit RR Holdings, Inc. is the Thoma Bravo holding entity for Reorg (now rebranded as Octus), the leading global provider of credit intelligence, data, and workflow technology for the leveraged finance and restructuring markets, acquired by Thoma Bravo from Warburg Pincus, as evidenced by the 2018 FTC early termination notice (File No. 20181423). Octus (formerly Reorg) delivers daily news, legal and financial analysis, fundamental credit data, and AI-powered tools across the full credit lifecycle — from leveraged loan origination and high-yield bond issuance through performing, stressed, and distressed credit — to over 35,000 professionals at the world's leading hedge funds, banks, law firms, and advisory firms. Permira subsequently acquired Reorg from Thoma Bravo in 2022, rebranding the company as Octus in 2024.
investments.end_customers: Octus/Reorg's customers are credit-focused institutional investors (hedge funds, CLO managers, distressed debt funds), investment banking leveraged finance and restructuring teams at all major bulge-bracket banks, law firms advising on bankruptcy and restructuring, and financial advisors working on distressed transactions. These professionals rely on Octus as an essential, near-real-time intelligence source for credit agreement terms, covenant analysis, bondholder dynamics, and restructuring developments that are unavailable through Bloomberg or generic financial data providers. The platform's coverage of 100% of the top 10 bulge-bracket banks and 88% of the AM Law 50 reflects its mission-critical position in professional credit workflows.

investments.company: Sundance Group Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of cloud-based document management and collaboration solutions
investments.business_description: Sundance Group Holdings, Inc. is the holding entity for NetDocuments, the leading cloud-based document management, email management, and collaboration software platform for law firms, corporate legal departments, and government agencies, acquired by Warburg Pincus from Clearlake Capital in 2021 for approximately $1.4 billion. NetDocuments provides a purpose-built legal cloud DMS with matter-centric organization, enterprise search, email management (including Outlook integration), team collaboration, and built-in disaster recovery, used by over 3,150 professional services firms and corporate legal departments worldwide. The platform is deeply integrated into law firm workflows and competes primarily with iManage (formerly Autonomy iManage) as one of the two dominant legal DMS platforms.
investments.end_customers: NetDocuments' customers are law firms of all sizes (from solo practitioners to Am Law 100 firms), corporate legal departments, and government legal offices that need a secure, cloud-native platform to store, organize, collaborate on, and retrieve legal documents and emails organized by client and matter. Legal secretaries, attorneys, and paralegals use NetDocuments as their daily document repository for drafting contracts, managing correspondence, and locating precedent documents, while IT teams value the cloud architecture that eliminates on-premises server management. The platform's matter-centric structure and deep Microsoft Outlook/Office integration make it deeply embedded in attorney workflows.

investments.company: Sundance Group Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Sundance Group Holdings, Inc. is the holding entity for NetDocuments, the leading cloud-based document management, email management, and collaboration software platform for law firms, corporate legal departments, and government agencies, acquired by Warburg Pincus from Clearlake Capital in 2021 for approximately $1.4 billion. investments.company: Superman Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of ERP software for the construction industry
investments.business_description: Superman Holdings, LLC (doing business as Foundation Software) is the Thoma Bravo-backed holding entity for Foundation Software, the leading provider of construction accounting, payroll, and ERP software for specialty subcontractors in the United States, acquired by Thoma Bravo in 2020. Foundation's FOUNDATION® software — branded as "America's #1 construction accounting software" — serves electrical, plumbing, roofing, HVAC, and other trade contractors with integrated modules for job cost accounting, payroll (including union and certified payroll), bidding and estimating, project management, mobile field apps, equipment tracking, and HR compliance. Founded in Ohio in 1985 by Fred Ode (who retained a minority stake post-acquisition), the company has served specialty subcontractors for over 35 years.
investments.end_customers: Foundation Software's customers are small-to-midsized specialty subcontracting businesses — electricians, plumbers, roofers, HVAC contractors, and other trade contractors — that need construction-specific accounting and payroll software that handles the unique complexities of the construction industry, including job cost tracking, AIA billing, certified payroll, and union reporting. Contractors' office managers, controllers, and payroll administrators rely on FOUNDATION as their primary ERP and payroll system, with switching costs that are very high given the embedded nature of job costing and historical data. The company's long tenure gives it strong brand loyalty among trade contractors, particularly in the Northeast and Midwest where union reporting requirements are more complex.

investments.company: Tamarack Intermediate, L.L.C. and Tamarack Parent, L.L.C.
investments.investment_type: Provider of environment, health, safety, and sustainability software
investments.business_description: Tamarack Intermediate, L.L.C. (doing business as Verisk 3E, subsequently sold by Verisk to New Mountain Capital for approximately $950 million) is a leading global provider of chemical regulatory compliance, environmental health and safety (EHS), and product stewardship software and data solutions. The platform enables chemical manufacturers, retailers, pharmaceutical companies, and industrial users to manage SDS (Safety Data Sheet) authoring and distribution, regulatory list monitoring, GHS labeling, PFAS risk management, supply chain chemical compliance, and emergency response across over 250,000 supplier relationships globally, with a proprietary database of over 10 million safety data sheets. Graham Partners' involvement reflects the prior investment structure before the Verisk carve-out.
investments.end_customers: 3E's customers are chemical manufacturers, specialty chemical companies, retailers (for product compliance), pharmaceutical companies, and industrial manufacturers that are legally required to maintain accurate SDS documentation, comply with OSHA HazCom regulations, and track thousands of chemical substances across global regulatory lists. Environmental health and safety (EHS) managers, regulatory affairs teams, and product stewardship professionals use 3E's platform to stay current with rapidly changing global chemical regulations (EU REACH, TSCA, GHS revisions) and fulfill PFAS disclosure requirements. The mission-critical nature of chemical compliance — with potential penalties including product recalls and OSHA violations — creates very high switching costs.

investments.company: Tamarack Intermediate, L.L.C. and Tamarack Parent, L.L.C.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Tamarack Intermediate, L.L.C. (doing business as Verisk 3E, subsequently sold by Verisk to New Mountain Capital for approximately $950 million) is a leading global provider of chemical regulatory compliance, environmental health and safety (EHS), and product stewardship software and data solutions. The platform enables chemical manufacturers, retailers, pharmaceutical companies, and industrial users to manage SDS (Safety Data Sheet) authoring and distribution, regulatory list monitoring, GHS labeling, PFAS risk management, supply chain chemical compliance, and emergency response across over 250,000 supplier relationships globally, with a proprietary database of over 10 million safety data sheets. Graham Partners' involvement reflects the prior investment structure before the Verisk carve-out.
investments.end_customers: 3E's customers are chemical manufacturers, specialty chemical companies, retailers (for product compliance), pharmaceutical companies, and industrial manufacturers that are legally required to maintain accurate SDS documentation, comply with OSHA HazCom regulations, and track thousands of chemical substances across global regulatory lists. Environmental health and safety (EHS) managers, regulatory affairs teams, and product stewardship professionals use 3E's platform to stay current with rapidly changing global chemical regulations (EU REACH, TSCA, GHS revisions) and fulfill PFAS disclosure requirements. The mission-critical nature of chemical compliance — with potential penalties including product recalls and OSHA violations — creates very high switching costs.

investments.company: TCP Hawker Intermediate LLC
investments.investment_type: Workforce management solutions provider
investments.business_description: TCP Hawker Intermediate LLC is the holding entity for TCP Software (TimeClock Plus), a cloud-based workforce management software platform covering time and attendance, leave management, scheduling, and document management, acquired by Providence Equity Partners in 2019 and rebranded from TimeClock Plus to TCP Software. Founded in 1988 in San Angelo, Texas, TCP serves approximately 30,000 public and private sector customers with a SaaS workforce management system that integrates with major payroll and ERP/HCM systems including ADP, Paycom, and various education-specific systems. The company is particularly strong in K-12 school districts and public sector organizations that require detailed FLSA-compliant time tracking and substitute teacher management.
investments.end_customers: TCP Software's customers span K-12 school districts, state and local government agencies, healthcare organizations, and private sector businesses of all sizes that need robust, compliance-focused time and attendance software beyond basic punch clocks. School district administrators and HR departments use TCP's specialized education modules for substitute management, employee scheduling, and FMLA/leave tracking, while government agencies rely on the platform for FLSA compliance and audit trails. The company's deep penetration in public sector education creates sticky, long-term customer relationships with high renewal rates.

investments.company: Three Rivers Buyer, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Developer of cloud-based software for government entities
investments.business_description: Three Rivers Buyer, Inc. is an Ares Management-backed acquisition vehicle for a software company in the Software and Services sector; the specific operating brand and product details have not been confirmed in publicly available sources under this holding entity name. The entity is structured as a standard leveraged buyout acquisition vehicle, typical of Ares Management's private equity software acquisitions, sitting above the operating software subsidiary. The company operates in the software and services sector based on ARCC's portfolio classification.
investments.end_customers: The end customers of Three Rivers Buyer's underlying operating software business are enterprise or mid-market organizations relying on the software for recurring, mission-critical workflows. The specific customer profile is tied to the operating subsidiary's vertical focus, which has not been publicly confirmed under this holding entity name in available sources.

investments.company: Trading Technologies International, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Order and execution management software provider
investments.business_description: Trading Technologies International (TT) is a Chicago-based provider of high-performance multi-asset trading software and market access technology for professional derivatives traders, acquired by 7RIDGE in December 2021 with Thoma Bravo subsequently taking a new strategic investment stake. TT's cloud-native platform provides order entry, algo development, market data analytics, trade surveillance, clearing, and post-trade services across global derivatives exchanges covering futures, options, fixed income, FX, and crypto, connecting traders at Tier 1 banks, brokerages, hedge funds, commodity trading advisors (CTAs), and proprietary trading firms to major venues including CME Group, ICE, Eurex, and others. The company's TT platform (a successor to the legacy X_TRADER) is deeply embedded in professional trading workflows as a primary front-end for derivatives execution.
investments.end_customers: Trading Technologies' customers are professional derivatives traders at hedge funds, proprietary trading firms, futures commission merchants (FCMs), bulge-bracket banks, and commodity trading advisors that require ultra-low-latency, highly reliable order execution software with sophisticated algorithmic trading and risk management capabilities. Traders, quants, and trading technology teams select TT for its speed, exchange connectivity breadth, and customizable API infrastructure for algorithmic strategy deployment. The platform is especially dominant among futures traders who rely on TT Algo as a foundation for automated strategy execution across global derivatives markets.

investments.company: Transit Technologies LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Transit Technologies LLC is a Warburg Pincus-backed provider of integrated transit technology solutions for public transit agencies, paratransit operators, and specialized transportation providers, serving over 2,000 clients in the US and globally. The company's platform covers computer-aided dispatch (CAD) and automatic vehicle location (AVL), real-time passenger information systems, automated scheduling and route optimization, fleet maintenance, passenger counting, digital fare payment, and autonomous/electric vehicle management — functioning as an all-in-one operating system for transit agencies. Transit Technologies powers over 100 million rides annually across fixed route, paratransit, NEMT (non-emergency medical transportation), and microtransit services.
investments.end_customers: Transit Technologies' customers are municipal transit authorities, county transportation departments, university campus shuttle systems, airport ground transportation services, and private paratransit operators that need software to manage fleets, schedule trips, communicate real-time arrival information to passengers, and comply with federal reporting requirements (NTD reporting, ADA paratransit mandates). Dispatch supervisors, operations managers, and IT teams at transit agencies use the platform to reduce deadhead miles, improve on-time performance, and provide the GTFS real-time data feeds required for integration with Google Maps and transit apps. The federal funding dependence of public transit agencies creates long procurement cycles but also multi-year contract stability.

investments.company: UFS, LLC and BV-UFS Aggregator, LLC
investments.investment_type: Managed IT services and banking solutions provider to community banks
investments.business_description: UFS, LLC (doing business as UFS Technology) is a technology outfitter for community banks and credit unions, providing managed IT services, cybersecurity, cloud hosting, digital banking solutions, and core banking system support, co-founded in 1991 by Bank First and other community banks and backed by BV Investment Partners. The company serves over 370 financial institution clients across the US with services including cloud-hosted infrastructure, end-user device management, cybersecurity monitoring, cash management technology, and regulatory compliance support, and has grown through acquisitions including Safe Systems and BankOnIT. UFS Technology operates as an outsourced technology department for community banks that lack the scale to maintain sophisticated IT capabilities in-house.
investments.end_customers: UFS Technology's customers are community banks and credit unions — typically with $500 million to $5 billion in assets — that cannot cost-effectively maintain their own cybersecurity operations centers, cloud infrastructure, and full IT departments. Bank CEOs, COOs, and IT officers engage UFS to manage their entire technology stack under a managed services model, giving them access to enterprise-grade cybersecurity and cloud capabilities at a per-institution cost structure. The community banking sector's regulatory pressure around cybersecurity (FDIC, OCC, FFIEC guidance) and the increasing sophistication of financial institution cyberattacks drive sustained demand for outsourced IT services.

investments.company: UserZoom Technologies, Inc.
investments.investment_type: User experience research automation software
investments.business_description: UserZoom Technologies (acquired by Thoma Bravo and subsequently merged with UserTesting in 2023 to operate under the UserTesting brand) was a leading enterprise UX research platform providing multi-method research capabilities, proprietary benchmarking methodology, and a research repository for conducting quantitative and qualitative user experience studies at scale. The combined UserTesting platform — formed by merging UserZoom's research depth with UserTesting's proprietary contributor network and enterprise experience testing capabilities — serves 3,400+ customers globally including 75 of the Fortune 100 companies across more than 45 countries. The unified platform enables product teams, UX researchers, and marketing teams to run usability tests, surveys, card sorts, and tree tests with statistically significant samples.
investments.end_customers: UserTesting's (formerly UserZoom's) customers are enterprise product development, UX research, and marketing teams at technology companies, financial services firms, healthcare organizations, and consumer brands that need to incorporate continuous user feedback into product and experience design decisions. UX researchers, product managers, and CX strategists use the platform to run moderated and unmoderated usability studies, measure NPS and task completion, and benchmark their digital experiences against competitors. The Fortune 100 penetration reflects demand among the largest enterprises for a scalable, institutionalized approach to voice-of-customer research that complements traditional analytics.

investments.company: Vamos Bidco, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of software and data services to the alcoholic beverage industry
investments.business_description: Vamos Bidco, Inc. is the acquisition vehicle for Vermont Information Processing (VIP), a leading software provider for the beverage distribution industry founded in 1972 in Colchester, Vermont, acquired by Warburg Pincus in a deal valued at approximately $1 billion (with TowerBrook Capital Partners as the prior sponsor during the ARCC loan period). VIP's platform covers the full beverage distributor technology stack — from warehouse management and inventory control to route accounting, sales order management, EDI with suppliers and retailers, and business intelligence — serving beer, wine, and spirits distributors across the United States. The company began as a billing service for distributors in 1972 and evolved into a full-suite ERP provider for the three-tier beverage alcohol distribution system.
investments.end_customers: VIP's customers are beer, wine, and spirits wholesale distributors of all sizes — from large regional distributors operating in multiple states to smaller independent craft beer distributors — that need software purpose-built for the unique regulatory and operational requirements of the three-tier alcohol distribution system. Operations managers and salespeople at distributors use VIP's platform for route accounting, delivery management, DEX/NACS electronic invoicing, state regulatory reporting, and sales analytics that generic ERP systems cannot support. The embedded nature of route accounting software in daily delivery operations and the complexity of state-by-state alcohol compliance requirements create very high switching costs.

investments.company: Victors Purchaser, LLC and WP Victors Co-Investment, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Third-party maintenance provider for hardware and data center infrastructure
investments.business_description: The operating company is Vermont Information Processing (VIP), a Colchester, Vermont-based software provider founded in 1972 that sells ERP and distribution management software to beverage distributors — primarily beer, wine, and spirits wholesalers. Its platform handles warehouse management, route accounting, inventory control, delivery logistics, and sales force automation for distributors serving major brewers such as Heineken, Boston Beer, and Diageo. Warburg Pincus acquired VIP in early 2025 for approximately $1 billion, creating Victors Purchaser LLC as the acquisition vehicle.
investments.end_customers: VIP's direct customers are beverage distributors (wholesalers) that sit between manufacturers and retail accounts. These distributors rely on VIP's software to run mission-critical daily operations — truck routing, keg deposit tracking, regulatory compliance, and DSD (direct-store-delivery) order management — making the software deeply embedded and sticky.

investments.company: WebPT, Inc. and WPT Intermediate Holdco, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Electronic medical record software provider
investments.business_description: WebPT is a Phoenix, Arizona-based SaaS company founded in 2008 that provides an electronic medical records (EMR), practice management, and billing platform purpose-built for outpatient rehabilitation therapy — physical therapy, occupational therapy, and speech-language pathology. As of recent filings, more than 200,000 rehab therapists across more than 20,000 clinics use WebPT's platform. Warburg Pincus acquired a majority stake in 2019; WPT Intermediate Holdco is the holding company layer in the acquisition structure.
investments.end_customers: WebPT's customers are outpatient rehabilitation therapy practices ranging from solo practitioners to large multi-location clinic groups. These practices use WebPT to document patient visits, submit insurance claims, schedule appointments, and manage billing — workflow that is deeply integrated into daily clinical operations and subject to strict healthcare compliance requirements.

investments.company: Wellington Bidco Inc. and Wellington TopCo LP
investments.investment_type: Provider of ERP and payments software for local governments
investments.business_description: Wellington Bidco Inc. and Wellington TopCo LP represent a Permira-backed software business providing ERP and payments software to local governments and municipalities. The platform enables cities and counties to handle billing, collections, utility payments, permitting, and related civic financial transactions. Permira, with its established track record in government and vertical software investments, used the Bidco/TopCo structure as its acquisition vehicle.
investments.end_customers: End customers are state and local government agencies — cities, counties, utility authorities, and special districts — that rely on the platform for revenue collection and constituent-facing payment processing. These are long-duration, recurring software contracts with public-sector clients that have high switching costs given the deep integration with finance and utility billing workflows.

investments.business_description: Wellness AcquisitionCo, Inc. is the acquisition vehicle through which TA Associates made an investment in a software or technology-enabled wellness business. The LEI was registered in July 2023, consistent with a mid-2023 platform acquisition by TA Associates in the health and wellness software vertical. TA Associates is a growth-oriented private equity firm with extensive experience in vertical SaaS platforms serving healthcare and wellness operators.
investments.end_customers: Depending on the specific operating company, end customers would be wellness operators (gyms, studios, clinics, or corporate wellness programs) or healthcare-adjacent businesses using the platform for scheduling, billing, member management, or care coordination. These customers benefit from purpose-built software that reduces administrative overhead and integrates with insurance or payment workflows.

investments.company: WorkWave Intermediate II, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of cloud-based field services and fleet management solutions
investments.business_description: WorkWave is a Holmdel, New Jersey-based SaaS company providing field service management software for the green industry (lawn care, landscaping), pest control, cleaning and janitorial services, and route-based businesses. Originally acquired by IFS (then owned by EQT), WorkWave was spun out as a standalone business in 2021 and is now backed by EQT and co-investors including TA Associates and Hg. The platform includes scheduling, dispatching, CRM, billing, and payments modules tailored to field service operators.
investments.end_customers: WorkWave's customers are primarily small-to-medium-sized field service companies in the pest control, lawn care, and cleaning verticals — segments where efficient route optimization and technician scheduling directly drive profitability. The software is mission-critical for daily operations, and WorkWave holds particularly strong market share in the U.S. pest control industry.

investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)

The software is mission-critical for daily operations, and WorkWave holds particularly strong market share in the U.S. pest control industry.

investments.company: ZenDesk, Inc., Zoro TopCo, Inc. and Zoro TopCo, LP
investments.investment_type: Provider of cloud-based customer support solutions
investments.business_description: Zendesk is a San Francisco-based SaaS company founded in 2007 that provides AI-powered customer service and engagement software, including its flagship support ticketing platform, Zendesk Support, as well as chat, voice, sales CRM (Zendesk Sell), and workforce management tools. In November 2022, a consortium led by Hellman & Friedman and Permira took Zendesk private in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $10.2 billion; Zoro TopCo is the holding company structure created for that take-private. The platform serves over 100,000 companies globally.
investments.end_customers: Zendesk's customers span virtually every industry — e-commerce, technology, financial services, healthcare, and media — ranging from startups to Fortune 500 enterprises. They use Zendesk to manage customer support interactions across email, chat, social media, voice, and web, with the software acting as the central hub for customer service operations and agent workflows.

investments.company: Aduro Advisors, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of fund administration services
investments.business_description: Aduro Advisors is a Denver, Colorado-based fund administrator founded in 2012 that provides outsourced fund administration services to venture capital and private equity managers. The firm manages over $120 billion of assets under administration (AUA) across more than 550 client firms and thousands of funds, using its proprietary FundPanel.io platform. Vitruvian Partners made a significant growth investment in May 2024, with existing investor PPC Enterprises retaining a stake.
investments.end_customers: Aduro's clients are VC and PE fund managers — primarily small to mid-sized general partners — that outsource their back-office fund administration, including capital call and distribution processing, investor reporting, waterfall calculations, and regulatory compliance. These clients value Aduro's combination of experienced staff and purpose-built technology to reduce operational overhead.

investments.company: Beacon Pointe Harmony, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of comprehensive wealth management services
investments.business_description: Beacon Pointe Harmony LLC is the holding entity for Beacon Pointe Advisors, an Irvine, California-based registered investment advisor (RIA) founded in 2002. Beacon Pointe manages over $30 billion in client assets through more than 110 financial advisors across 27 offices nationwide. KKR took a minority stake in 2021, providing growth capital to support the firm's national expansion and RIA acquisition strategy. It is the largest female-led independent RIA in the United States.
investments.end_customers: Beacon Pointe serves high-net-worth individuals, foundations, endowments, family offices, and retirement plans. Clients receive comprehensive wealth management services including investment management, financial planning, tax optimization, and retirement planning through Beacon Pointe's proprietary allWEALTH® approach.

investments.company: Clearstead Advisors, LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Clearstead Advisors is a Cleveland, Ohio-based independent investment advisory firm founded in 1989 that serves wealthy families and institutional clients with approximately $3 billion in illiquid private investments under management and advisement. The firm provides integrated wealth management including investment consulting, outsourced CIO (OCIO) services, tax planning, trust administration, and financial planning. Flexpoint Ford, a PE firm specializing in financial services and healthcare, owns a majority stake.
investments.end_customers: Clearstead serves two primary segments: wealthy families who require comprehensive private wealth management, and institutions (foundations, endowments, defined benefit plans, university endowments) that engage Clearstead as an investment consultant or OCIO to manage asset allocation, manager selection, and portfolio governance.

investments.company: Convera International Holdings Limited and Convera International Financial S.A R.L.
investments.investment_type: Provider of B2B international payment and FX risk management solutions
investments.business_description: Convera is the rebranded entity formed from the 2021 acquisition of Western Union Business Solutions (WUBS) by Goldfinch Partners and The Baupost Group for approximately $910 million. Convera is a standalone B2B cross-border payments and foreign exchange (FX) solutions provider, processing over $110 billion in total payment volume annually, with access to over 140 currencies across 200+ countries and territories, serving more than 30,000 business customers globally.
investments.end_customers: Convera's customers are businesses — predominantly SMEs and mid-market companies — that need to make or receive international payments, hedge currency risk, or manage FX exposure. Typical clients include importers/exporters, educational institutions, nonprofits, and professional services firms that transact across borders and use Convera's platform to optimize FX rates and reduce payment friction.

investments.company: eCapital Finance Corp.
investments.investment_type: Consolidator of commercial finance businesses
investments.business_description: eCapital is a Miami, Florida-based specialty finance company founded in 2006 that provides alternative lending solutions to small and middle-market businesses across more than 80 industry sectors. Its core products include invoice factoring, freight factoring for trucking companies, asset-based lending (ABL), healthcare receivables financing, and payroll funding for staffing companies. eCapital operates across the U.S., Canada, and the UK with over 850 employees.
investments.end_customers: eCapital's borrowers are businesses with immediate working capital needs that cannot access traditional bank credit — trucking companies waiting 30-60 days for freight payment, staffing firms that must fund weekly payroll before clients pay invoices, healthcare providers with slow insurance reimbursement cycles, and SMEs using their receivables or inventory as collateral. Speed of funding and flexible underwriting are key differentiators.

investments.company: Endeavor Bidco LLC and Endeavor TopCo, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Global securities finance trading platform
investments.business_description: Endeavor Bidco LLC and Endeavor TopCo are the acquisition vehicle entities used by Silver Lake for its take-private of Endeavor Group Holdings (NYSE: EDR) in a transaction valued at approximately $25 billion completed in early 2025. Endeavor (now rebranded as WME Group) is a global sports and entertainment company that owns talent agency WME, brand licensing business IMG Licensing, marketing agency 160over90, and retains a ~61% controlling stake in publicly traded TKO Group (UFC + WWE). Silver Lake was the preexisting majority owner prior to the full take-private.
investments.end_customers: Endeavor's clients are professional athletes, entertainers, brands, and sports rights holders who use WME for representation and IMG for licensing and marketing. On the B2B side, corporate brands partner with Endeavor's marketing and licensing arms for sponsorships, branded content, and athlete endorsements. The UFC and WWE properties (in TKO) sell live events, media rights, and merchandise to global sports fans.

investments.company: GAPCO AIV Interholdco (CP), L.P.
investments.investment_type: Wealth management and financial planning firm
investments.business_description: GAPCO AIV Interholdco (CP), L.P. is a General Atlantic co-investment vehicle — a Delaware-registered limited partnership that holds General Atlantic's investment in a financial services portfolio company. GAPCO (General Atlantic Portfolio Company) AIV structures are used to pool co-investment capital alongside GA's main funds. Based on General Atlantic's financial services strategy and the CP suffix (potentially Creative Planning or another wealth management platform), this entity most likely represents a co-investment in a wealth management or fintech business.
investments.end_customers: The ultimate end customers depend on the specific operating company, but General Atlantic's financial services portfolio typically includes wealth management platforms, fintech companies, and financial data businesses serving high-net-worth individuals, institutional investors, or other financial intermediaries.

investments.company: Grit Buyer, Inc. and Integrum Grit Co-Invest LP
investments.investment_type: Specialized finance and accounting advisory firm
investments.business_description: Grit Buyer, Inc. is the acquisition vehicle through which Integrum Holdings acquired Stout (formerly Stout Risius Ross), a Chicago-based global advisory firm specializing in investment banking, valuation, financial disputes, forensic accounting, and transaction advisory services. The transaction closed in July 2025. Stout employs over 1,000 professionals across North America, Asia, and Europe, and was previously owned by Audax Private Equity (which drove 2x growth in headcount and revenue). Integrum Grit Co-Invest LP is the co-investment vehicle alongside Integrum's main fund.
investments.end_customers: Stout's clients are corporations, private equity firms, law firms, and financial institutions that need valuation opinions, fairness opinions, M&A advisory, dispute analysis, and forensic accounting support. Stout frequently serves as an expert witness in litigation and a trusted advisor in complex transactions where independent, defensible analysis is required.

investments.company: GTCR F Buyer Corp. and GTCR (D) Investors LP
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: GTCR F Buyer Corp. and GTCR (D) Investors LP are the acquisition and co-investment vehicles used by GTCR to acquire AssetMark Financial Holdings, a Concord, California-based wealth management technology platform serving independent financial advisors. GTCR completed the $2.7 billion take-private of AssetMark in September 2024. AssetMark provides turnkey asset management programs (TAMPs), portfolio construction tools, and practice management support to independent advisors, with over $100 billion in platform assets.
investments.end_customers: AssetMark's direct customers are independent financial advisors (RIAs and hybrid advisors) who use the platform to outsource investment management, gain access to curated model portfolios, and deliver a seamless client experience. The end beneficiaries are the advisors' retail clients — individual investors — who gain access to professionally managed, diversified portfolios through the advisor-intermediated model.

investments.company: HighTower Holding, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of investment, financial and retirement planning services
investments.business_description: HighTower Holding LLC is the holding company for Hightower Advisors, a Chicago, Illinois-based RIA aggregator founded in 2008. Hightower is one of the largest RIA consolidators in the U.S., managing approximately $326 billion in assets under advisement across more than 100 partner advisory firms in 33 states. Thomas H. Lee Partners (THL) acquired a majority stake in 2017 for $350 million; OMERS (Ontario Municipal Employees' Retirement System) is a co-investor. ARCC holds subordinated debt in the capital structure.
investments.end_customers: Hightower's partner advisory firms serve high-net-worth individuals, families, foundations, and retirement plans. Hightower provides its advisors with technology, compliance, accounting, HR, investment management, and marketing infrastructure, allowing them to focus on client relationships while benefiting from the scale and resources of a large platform.

investments.company: Ivy Hill Asset Management, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Asset management services
investments.business_description: Ivy Hill Asset Management is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Ares Capital Corporation (ARCC) that manages approximately $6 billion in middle-market collateralized loan obligation (CLO) funds and private credit vehicles. Ivy Hill's investment strategy focuses on senior secured first- and second-lien loans to middle-market U.S. companies, structured through CLOs, commingled private funds, and separately managed accounts. It is a core affiliate of ARCC's ecosystem, recycling capital and co-investing alongside ARCC's balance sheet.
investments.end_customers: Ivy Hill's investors are institutional — insurance companies, pension funds, banks, and other sophisticated investors — seeking structured credit exposure to the middle-market loan asset class through CLO vehicles. The underlying borrowers are the same middle-market companies that ARCC lends to directly, creating a vertically integrated credit platform.

investments.company: Lido Advisors, LLC and LAL Group Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Wealth management and financial planning firm
investments.business_description: Lido Advisors is a Los Angeles-based independent RIA founded in 1999, managing over $30 billion in regulatory assets under management across 40 offices nationwide. Lido employs a family office-style investment model emphasizing downside protection and access to institutional-quality alternative investments. Charlesbank Capital Partners took a PE stake in 2021; in 2024, Lido also received a minority investment from HPS Investment Partners (now part of BlackRock), and Charlesbank retained minority governance rights. LAL Group Holdings is a holding company layer.
investments.end_customers: Lido's clients are high-net-worth and ultra-high-net-worth individuals, family offices, businesses, charities, and foundations who seek comprehensive wealth management with a family office ethos. Lido differentiates by offering clients co-investment access to private equity, real estate, and hedge fund opportunities alongside traditional investment management.

investments.company: Mai Capital Management Intermediate LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: MAI Capital Management is a Cleveland, Ohio-based registered investment advisor founded in 1973 (originally as Investment Advisors International) with over $50 billion in assets under management. The firm provides comprehensive wealth management including investment management, financial and estate planning, tax planning and preparation, insurance consulting, and family office services, with specialized capabilities for professional athletes and entertainers. Ares Management is a financial sponsor. MAI merged with Evoke Advisors to create a combined AUM of approximately $67 billion.
investments.end_customers: MAI Capital's clients include high-net-worth and ultra-high-net-worth individuals, multi-generational families, foundations, and institutional clients. investments.company: Medlar Bidco Limited
investments.investment_type: Provider of fund and corporate services to alternative assets industry
investments.business_description: Medlar Bidco Limited is the UK acquisition vehicle used by Cinven for a financial services investment. Based on Cinven's portfolio and UK financial services strategy, the most likely underlying business is True Potential, a Newcastle-upon-Tyne-based technology-led wealth management and financial advice platform that Cinven acquired a majority stake in through a £2.5 billion transaction announced in 2021. True Potential serves over 1.4 million customers through its proprietary digital platform and partners with approximately 20% of UK financial advisers.
investments.end_customers: True Potential (the likely underlying business) serves retail investors in the UK — primarily mass market and mass affluent individuals — who invest through IFAs (independent financial advisers) or directly through True Potential's consumer-facing digital investment platform. The platform's trueRetirement and trueWealth products allow individuals to build and manage ISAs, pensions, and investment portfolios online.

Constellation Wealth Capital (CWC) made a minority strategic investment, and CWC Fund I Co-Invest (MFA) LP is the co-investment vehicle. investments.company: Nexus Buyer LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of FDIC-insured deposit placement services
investments.business_description: Nexus Buyer LLC is the acquisition vehicle for IntraFi Network (formerly CDARS and ICS), a Charlottesville, Virginia-based financial technology company that operates the largest network for FDIC-insured deposit placement in the United States. IntraFi's platform allows banks to offer customers full FDIC insurance on large-dollar deposits by spreading them across its network of over 3,000 member financial institutions. Ares Management holds second-lien debt; the company has approximately $2 billion in total credit facilities.
investments.end_customers: IntraFi's customers are banks and other FDIC-insured depository institutions that join the network to attract and retain large depositors (businesses, municipalities, nonprofits, and wealthy individuals) who need deposit balances exceeding FDIC insurance limits. End depositors use IntraFi-enabled accounts to achieve full FDIC insurance on balances in the tens of millions without managing relationships at multiple banks.

investments.company: Oak Funding LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of wealth management services
investments.business_description: Oak Funding LLC is an Ares Management-originated specialty finance vehicle structured as a first-lien senior secured lending facility. Within ARCC's portfolio, Oak Funding functions as a directed lending or co-investment vehicle through which ARCC provides structured capital to a specific underlying credit or asset pool. This type of entity is common in Ares's broader credit ecosystem, where SPVs facilitate leverage, capital recycling, or targeted exposure to specific loan pools or asset classes.
investments.end_customers: The end obligors are the underlying borrowers within the vehicle — typically middle-market companies or financial sponsors whose loans are financed through the Oak Funding facility. The investors in the vehicle are Ares-affiliated funds seeking levered exposure to a defined pool of assets.

investments.company: Pathstone Family Office LLC and Kelso XI Tailwind Co-Investment, L.P.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Pathstone Family Office is an Englewood, New Jersey-based multi-family office (MFO) founded in 2010 that provides integrated wealth management for ultra-high-net-worth families, single family offices, endowments, and foundations. As of December 2024, Pathstone manages over $100 billion in total regulatory assets ($62.6 billion discretionary, $37.8 billion non-discretionary) following its merger with Hall Capital Partners. Kelso & Company and Lovell Minnick Partners are the PE sponsors; Kelso XI Tailwind Co-Investment LP is a co-investment vehicle for Kelso's XI fund.
investments.end_customers: Pathstone's clients are UHNW families (typically $30M+ investable assets), single family offices, private foundations, and institutional endowments that require a fully integrated family office — investment management, tax, estate planning, philanthropy, and generational wealth governance — from a single advisory firm without the overhead of a dedicated in-house staff.

investments.company: PCIA SPV-3, LLC and ASE Royal Aggregator, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of comprehensive wealth management services
investments.business_description: PCIA SPV-3, LLC and ASE Royal Aggregator, LLC are structured investment vehicles associated with Pathstone Family Office and co-sponsored by Kelso & Company and Lovell Minnick Partners. investments.company: PCS MidCo, Inc. and PCS Parent, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Provider of 401K recordkeeping software solutions
investments.business_description: PCS MidCo, Inc. and PCS Parent, L.P. are holding entities for PCS Retirement, an independent retirement plan recordkeeper based in suburban Philadelphia. PCS is one of the nation's fastest-growing independent and conflict-free retirement solution providers, administering over 19,000 retirement plans representing more than $26 billion in assets for approximately 850,000 eligible participants. Genstar Capital is the prior PE sponsor; Lee Equity Partners made a majority investment in March 2024 and named a new CEO.
investments.end_customers: PCS's customers are financial advisors and plan sponsors — primarily small-to-mid-sized businesses — that need a technology-forward, advisor-friendly recordkeeping platform for 401(k), 403(b), 457, SIMPLE IRA, and other ERISA and non-ERISA retirement plans. Financial advisors value PCS's open-architecture, conflict-free model that does not compete with proprietary investment products.

investments.company: Perigon Wealth Management, LLC, Perigon Wealth Advisors Holdings Company, LLC and CWC Fund I Co-Invest (Prism) LP
investments.investment_type: Wealth management and financial planning firm
investments.business_description: Perigon Wealth Management is a San Francisco-based independent RIA with over $6.5 billion in client assets under management, operating as an advisor-led platform that acquires and affiliates with other advisory practices. The firm focuses on providing comprehensive financial planning and investment management while preserving advisor independence. Constellation Wealth Capital (CWC) holds a minority stake; CWC Fund I Co-Invest (Prism) LP is the co-investment vehicle. Perigon has built an emerging multi-family office segment through its acquisition of Matthai Capital Management.
investments.end_customers: Perigon serves high-net-worth individuals, ultra-high-net-worth families, and institutional clients across its network of advisor partners. Clients receive personalized financial planning, investment management, and — through the multi-family office segment — comprehensive estate, tax, and philanthropic advisory services.

investments.business_description: Rialto Management Group is a Miami, Florida-based vertically integrated commercial real estate investment and asset management platform. The platform includes Rialto Capital Management (investment management), Rialto Capital Advisors (loan servicing and special servicing), and direct real estate investing capabilities spanning debt and equity strategies. investments.company: RWA Wealth Partners, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of comprehensive wealth management services
investments.business_description: RWA Wealth Partners is a national registered investment advisor formed in 2023 from the merger of Ropes Wealth Advisors and Adviser Investments (rebranded as RWA). The firm manages approximately $17-20 billion in assets under management and is one of the largest women-led RIAs in the United States. Summit Partners took a PE stake in 2020; the firm is actively seeking a new investor or strategic acquirer as Summit pursues an exit. RWA has expanded its footprint through acquisitions including Moirai Wealth Management on the West Coast.
investments.end_customers: RWA serves high-net-worth individuals and families — predominantly in the $1M-$10M+ investable asset range — with personalized financial planning, investment management, and retirement planning. The firm emphasizes fee-only, fiduciary advice and serves clients primarily in the Boston area and across the U.S. through its growing multi-office platform.

investments.company: Steward Partners Global Advisory, LLC, Steward Partners Investment Advisory, LLC, Steward Partners Intermediate II, LLC, and Steward Partners New Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Wealth management platform
investments.business_description: Steward Partners Global Advisory is a Washington, D.C.-founded (2013) employee-owned independent financial services firm that provides comprehensive wealth management for families, businesses, and multigenerational investors. The firm manages approximately $44 billion in client assets across its advisor network and operates as a wirehouse-alternative that recruits experienced advisors from firms like Morgan Stanley, Merrill Lynch, and Raymond James. Ares Credit provided $475 million in financing as a key capital partner. Rockefeller Capital Management also has a strategic relationship.
investments.end_customers: Steward Partners serves high-net-worth individuals, families, closely held businesses, and multi-generational investors who prefer the independent advisor model but want the resources of a large institution. Clients access portfolio management, financial planning, estate planning, and alternative investments through Steward's advisors, who often have long-tenured client relationships carried from prior wirehouse employment.

investments.company: The Edelman Financial Center, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of investment, financial and retirement planning services
investments.business_description: The Edelman Financial Center is the holding entity for Edelman Financial Engines (EFE), the nation's largest independent RIA formed through the 2018 merger of Edelman Financial Services (founded 1986 by Ric Edelman) and Financial Engines (founded 1996). Hellman & Friedman is the majority PE sponsor. EFE manages over $270 billion in assets for more than 1.3 million clients across all 50 states, providing both technology-enabled automated advice and in-person personalized financial planning through its national planner network. In early 2026, EFE launched a firmwide planner ownership program.
investments.end_customers: EFE's mission is to serve the mass affluent — working Americans with moderate to substantial savings who have historically been underserved by traditional financial advisors. Clients include individuals approaching retirement, 401(k) participants, and families seeking holistic financial planning, with a particular focus on making professional advice accessible at scale through both digital and human-delivered channels.

investments.company: The Mather Group, LLC, TVG-TMG Topco, Inc., and TVG-TMG Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of comprehensive wealth management services
investments.business_description: The Mather Group is a Chicago, Illinois-based fee-only RIA with approximately $14 billion in assets under management across 14 offices and about 4,000 clients. Founded as an independent fiduciary, The Mather Group provides comprehensive financial planning, investment management, tax planning and preparation, estate planning, and family office services. The Vistria Group (a Chicago-based PE firm) acquired a majority stake in 2022 in the firm's first PE partnership; TVG-TMG Topco and Holdings are the investment holding structure. The Mather Group has been ranked on Barron's Top 100 RIA list for four consecutive years.
investments.end_customers: The Mather Group serves approximately 4,000 clients who are primarily high-net-worth individuals, pre-retirees, and retirees seeking fee-only, fiduciary financial planning. The firm's integrated tax preparation and estate planning capabilities appeal to clients who want a single firm to handle complex, multi-dimensional financial needs without hidden product commissions.

investments.company: The Ultimus Group Midco, LLC, The Ultimus Group, LLC, and The Ultimus Group Aggregator, LP
investments.investment_type: Provider of asset-servicing capabilities for fund managers
investments.business_description: Ultimus Fund Solutions is one of the largest independent fund administration providers in the United States, formed through the 2019 combination of Ultimus Fund Solutions and The Gemini Companies. The firm provides full-service fund administration, transfer agent, compliance, and middle office services to registered funds (mutual funds, ETFs, interval funds, CITs), private funds, and public plans such as 529s. Ultimus was backed by Genstar Capital. Its clients include ProShares and over 550 investment managers.
investments.end_customers: Ultimus's clients are investment managers — mutual fund companies, ETF issuers, alternative asset managers, and public plan administrators — that outsource fund accounting, regulatory filing, shareholder reporting, and transfer agent services. These managers benefit from Ultimus's scale, compliance expertise, and technology platform, allowing them to focus on portfolio management rather than back-office administration.

investments.company: TPG IX Cardiff Debt HoldCo I, LLC, TPG IX Cardiff Debt Holdco II, LLC, TPG IX Cardiff CI I, L.P., and TPG IX Cardiff CI II, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Provider of comprehensive wealth management services
investments.business_description: TPG IX Cardiff Debt HoldCo I & II and the associated co-investment limited partnerships are the structured investment vehicles through which TPG Capital (Fund IX) holds its minority investment in Creative Planning, a Overland Park, Kansas-based independent wealth management firm with over $375 billion in combined assets under management and advisement. TPG made a substantial minority investment in Creative Planning in September 2024, valuing the firm at approximately $16 billion. General Atlantic retains its existing minority stake; founder and CEO Peter Mallouk holds the majority.
investments.end_customers: Creative Planning serves high-net-worth and ultra-high-net-worth individuals and families across all 50 states and 90 countries, offering comprehensive wealth management including investment management, financial planning, tax services, estate planning, and family office services. The firm is known for its fiduciary, fee-only model and its broad geographic reach serving over 100,000 clients.

investments.company: Waverly Advisors, LLC and WAAM Topco, LLC

investments.end_customers: WEG serves high-net-worth individuals and families across the US seeking integrated, holistic wealth management and financial planning services.
