investments.company: Wellington-Altus Financial Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Wellington-Altus Financial Inc. is the parent company of Wellington-Altus Private Wealth Inc., the top-rated investment dealer in Canada, along with related subsidiaries in private counsel, insurance, and US operations. Founded in 2017 and headquartered in Winnipeg, the firm has grown to over 1,000 employees and manages over $45 billion in assets under administration. Kelso & Company acquired a 25% minority stake in 2025 at a $1.5+ billion CAD enterprise valuation, with existing investor Cynosure Group retaining its position.
investments.end_customers: Wellington-Altus serves high-net-worth and ultra-high-net-worth Canadians through independent financial advisors who offer investment management, financial planning, and insurance solutions. The firm attracts experienced investment advisors and their client books from larger bank-owned dealers.

investments.company: Wharf Street Ratings Acquisition LLC
investments.investment_type: Global credit rating agency
investments.business_description: Wharf Street Ratings Acquisition LLC is the acquisition vehicle through which Wharf Street acquired a majority stake in Kroll Bond Rating Agency (KBRA), a nationally recognized statistical rating organization (NRSRO) founded in 2010. KBRA rates structured finance, corporate, financial institution, and public finance instruments, competing with the major incumbent ratings agencies. ARCC provided debt financing as part of the capitalization of this transaction.
investments.end_customers: KBRA's clients are issuers of debt securities—including banks, insurance companies, structured finance vehicles, municipalities, and corporations—who pay for credit ratings that enable them to access capital markets. Investors in rated securities also rely on KBRA's ratings to assess credit risk.

investments.company: Absolute Dental Group LLC and Absolute Dental Equity, LLC
investments.investment_type: Dental services provider
investments.business_description: Absolute Dental is a dental service organization (DSO) operating 50+ dental clinics across Nevada, primarily concentrated in the Las Vegas metro area. Backed by BPOC, the company employs general dentists and specialists and offers a broad range of services including cosmetic dentistry, dental implants, pediatric dentistry, oral surgery, and root canals at affordable price points. The equity holding entity (Absolute Dental Equity, LLC) sits above the operating DSO.
investments.end_customers: Absolute Dental primarily serves Medicaid-eligible and value-conscious consumers across Nevada who need accessible, affordable dental care. The multi-location model and extended hours attract working families and underserved populations who may lack easy access to traditional private practices.

investments.investment_type: Dental services provider
investments.business_description: ADG, LLC (Administrative Dental Group) is the management services organization supporting Great Expressions Dental Centers (GEDC), a large DSO operating across multiple US states primarily in the Midwest and Southeast. ADG provides administrative and business support services and licenses the Great Expressions brand to affiliated dental practices; it does not directly own or operate clinics. Roark Capital is the majority private equity owner through the GEDC Equity and RC IV investment vehicles.
investments.end_customers: Great Expressions Dental Centers serves patients across its network seeking general, cosmetic, and specialty dental care. The patient base spans a broad demographic but skews toward middle-income consumers in suburban markets in states such as Michigan, Georgia, Florida, and Virginia.

investments.company: Advarra Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of central institutional review boards over clinical trials
investments.business_description: Advarra is the leading independent institutional review board (IRB), institutional biosafety committee (IBC), and research compliance solutions provider for the clinical research industry in the US and Canada. Backed by GI Partners, Advarra operates on an integrated technology platform that provides IRB review, research site compliance, clinical trial management software, and financial and regulatory workflow tools. The firm holds daily IRB meetings to support drug sponsors, CROs, academic medical centers, and hospital systems.
investments.end_customers: Advarra's primary clients are pharmaceutical and biotech companies, contract research organizations (CROs), academic medical centers, hospital systems, and independent research sites that are required by federal regulation to obtain IRB approval before initiating clinical trials on human subjects.

investments.company: Aerin Medical Inc.
investments.investment_type: Developer and manufacturer of non-invasive nasal treatment solutions
investments.business_description: Aerin Medical is a Mountain View, California-based venture-backed medical device company that develops temperature-controlled radiofrequency (TCRF) devices for ENT (ear, nose, and throat) conditions. Its two FDA-cleared products are VivAer (for nasal airway obstruction) and RhinAer (for chronic rhinitis), both administered as in-office procedures under local anesthesia without surgery. Over 150,000 patients have been treated with Aerin's devices.
investments.end_customers: Aerin's primary customers are ENT physicians and otolaryngologists who perform the VivAer and RhinAer procedures in outpatient office settings. The end patients are adults suffering from chronic nasal congestion, difficulty breathing through the nose, or chronic rhinitis who want a non-surgical, lasting alternative to medications.

investments.company: Alcresta Therapeutics, Inc.
investments.investment_type: A commercial-stage medical device company
investments.business_description: Alcresta Therapeutics is a Boston-area specialty pharmaceutical and medical device company focused on enzyme-based products for patients with fat malabsorption who receive enteral (tube) feeding. Its lead commercial product, RELiZORB, is the only FDA-cleared digestive enzyme cartridge for enteral nutrition, using immobilized lipase (iLipase technology) to break down fats in tube feeding formula before ingestion—particularly relevant for cystic fibrosis patients and others with exocrine pancreatic insufficiency.
investments.end_customers: Alcresta's customers are hospitals, home infusion pharmacies, and specialty distributors that supply enteral nutrition products, with prescribing clinicians including gastroenterologists, pulmonologists, and dietitians. End patients are primarily pediatric and adult cystic fibrosis patients and others with pancreatic insufficiency who receive tube feeding and suffer from severe fat malabsorption.

investments.company: Amerivet Partners Management, Inc. and AVE Holdings LP
investments.investment_type: Veterinary practice management platform
investments.business_description: AmeriVet Veterinary Partners is a San Antonio-based veterinary hospital consolidator that owns and operates approximately 140 companion animal hospitals across 30+ states in the US. Founded in 2016, AmeriVet typically acquires joint-venture partnerships with veterinarians, allowing selling practices to retain equity, and provides operational improvements in scheduling, procurement, and recruiting. GTCR was an early backer; AEA Investors subsequently acquired the company.
investments.end_customers: AmeriVet's hospitals serve pet owners—primarily dog and cat owners—seeking general practice veterinary care including wellness exams, vaccinations, dental cleanings, and diagnostics. The joint-venture model is also targeted at veterinarian-owners seeking liquidity or succession solutions while maintaining clinical autonomy.

investments.company: Artivion, Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Artivion (NYSE: AORT), formerly known as CryoLife, is an Atlanta-based publicly traded medical device and tissue processing company focused on aortic disease. Its product portfolio spans aortic stent grafts (E-vita, AMDS, NEXUS families, Arcevo LSA), On-X mechanical heart valves, BioGlue surgical sealant, and implantable human tissue allografts preserved through proprietary cryopreservation. Products are sold in over 100 countries worldwide. ARCC provides revolving/delayed-draw credit facilities.
investments.end_customers: Artivion's customers are cardiac and vascular surgeons at hospitals and cardiac surgery centers worldwide who treat patients with aortic aneurysms, dissections, and other aortic pathologies. The On-X valves and BioGlue are sold to cardiothoracic surgeons for open-heart procedures.

investments.company: Avalign Holdings, Inc. and Avalign Technologies, Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Avalign Technologies is a contract developer and manufacturer (CDMO) of precision-machined medical instruments, orthopedic implants, and surgical instrumentation for OEM medical device companies. With over 30 years of manufacturing history and facilities in the US and Germany, Avalign serves orthopedic, spine, and trauma device makers under two divisions: Avalign Orthopedics (premium implants and instruments) and Avalign Surgical (broader surgical specialty instruments). Chicago Pacific Founders and GTCR have been involved as sponsors.
investments.end_customers: Avalign's customers are large and mid-sized medical device OEMs that outsource the design, engineering, and manufacturing of instruments and implants to contract specialists. End users are orthopedic surgeons, spine surgeons, and trauma surgeons at hospitals who use the instruments and implants in surgical procedures.

investments.company: BAART Programs, Inc., MedMark Services, Inc., and Canadian Addiction Treatment Centres LP
investments.investment_type: Opiod treatment provider
investments.business_description: BAART Programs, MedMark Treatment Centers, and Canadian Addiction Treatment Centres (CATC) are operating brands of BayMark Health Services, the largest specialty healthcare organization in North America for substance use disorder (SUD) treatment. Backed by Webster Equity Partners, BayMark operates over 300 opioid treatment program (OTP) facilities across 35 US states and 3 Canadian provinces, treating 70,000+ patients daily with medication-assisted treatment (MAT) using methadone and buprenorphine alongside counseling services. CATC, acquired in 2018, is the largest opioid treatment provider in Canada with 72 OTPs and 19 pharmacies in Ontario.
investments.end_customers: BayMark's patients are adults suffering from opioid use disorder, including addiction to heroin, fentanyl, and prescription opioids, who seek outpatient medication-assisted treatment. Payers include Medicaid (the predominant payer), Medicare, private commercial insurance, and state-funded behavioral health programs.

investments.company: Bayou Intermediate II, LLC
investments.investment_type: Developer and manufacturer of vascular medical devices
investments.business_description: Bayou Intermediate II, LLC is the intermediate holding company above Cordis, a global manufacturer of interventional cardiovascular medical devices. Cordis has approximately 70 years of history in the interventional cardiology space—spanning guidewires, diagnostic and guiding catheters, sheaths, vascular closure devices, drug-eluting balloons, and related products—and was acquired by Hellman & Friedman in a $988 million transaction in 2021. Cordis products are used in catheter-based procedures across interventional cardiology and peripheral vascular disease.
investments.end_customers: Cordis sells primarily to interventional cardiologists and vascular surgeons at hospitals and catheterization labs worldwide. The devices are used in diagnostic and therapeutic procedures such as coronary angiography, percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI), and peripheral artery disease treatment.

investments.company: BrightStar Group Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of private-pay home care services for patients
investments.business_description: BrightStar Group Holdings is the franchisor parent of BrightStar Care, a home care and medical staffing franchise network with approximately 400 agencies across the US. Acquired by Peak Rock Capital in March 2025, BrightStar Care provides both skilled nursing care (RNs, LPNs) and unskilled personal care/companion services through its franchisees, employing over 15,000 caregivers and 5,700 registered nurses nationwide. The company also provides custom medical staffing solutions to hospitals and healthcare facilities.
investments.end_customers: BrightStar Care franchisees serve two distinct customer segments: (1) individuals and families seeking in-home care for seniors, post-surgical patients, or people with disabilities, and (2) hospitals, clinics, and other healthcare facilities that need supplemental clinical staffing. Payers include private pay families, long-term care insurance, Medicaid waiver programs, and healthcare facility staffing budgets.

investments.company: BVI Medical, Inc. and BVI Group Limited
investments.investment_type: Developer, manufacturer, and distributor of diverse ophthalmic surgical products
investments.business_description: BVI Medical (Beaver-Visitec International) is a global ophthalmology-focused medical device company manufacturing and distributing surgical instruments and devices for eye surgery across more than 90 countries. Its portfolio of branded products includes Beaver (surgical knives and blades), Visitec (cannulas), Malosa (single-use instruments), Vitreq (vitreoretinal surgical products), and PhysIOL (premium intraocular lenses). BVI operates two manufacturing facilities in Waltham, MA and Bidford, UK. GI Partners has been an investor in the company.
investments.end_customers: BVI's primary customers are ophthalmologists and eye surgeons performing cataract, glaucoma, vitreoretinal, corneal, and refractive procedures at hospitals, ambulatory surgery centers, and eye surgery clinics worldwide. The company sells through a direct sales force and distributor network to surgical facilities.

investments.company: Color Intermediate, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of pre-payment integrity software solution
investments.business_description: Color Intermediate, LLC is the holding structure above Color Health (formerly Color Genomics), a health technology company backed by Francisco Partners that operates as a population-scale genomic testing and cancer care delivery platform. Color provides hereditary cancer genetic testing (covering BRCA1/2 and 28 additional cancer-linked genes), and has more recently built a virtual oncologist-led cancer clinic serving employers, health plans, unions, and public sector populations. The company's CLIA-certified lab delivers tests at scale and at lower costs than traditional providers.
investments.end_customers: Color's primary clients are large employers, health plans, labor unions, and government health agencies that contract with Color to provide population-wide cancer screening, genetic testing, and cancer care navigation as an employee or member benefit. Individual patients access Color's services at no or low cost through their employer or health plan.

investments.company: Convey Health Solutions, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Healthcare workforce management software provider
investments.business_description: Convey Health Solutions is a specialized healthcare technology and services company focused exclusively on government-sponsored health plan administration, particularly Medicare Advantage and Medicare Part D. Taken private by TPG Capital for approximately $1.1 billion in 2022, the company provides health plans with technology platforms, data analytics, advisory services, and operational support for member enrollment, supplemental benefits administration, agent management, risk adjustment, and compliance. Convey's affiliated brands include HealthScape Advisors, Pareto Intelligence, and Gorman Health Group.
investments.end_customers: Convey's clients are Medicare Advantage plan sponsors, Part D plan sponsors, and other government health plan operators that outsource technology and operational functions for their government business. The ultimate beneficiaries are Medicare-eligible seniors who receive enrollment, benefit, and member services through Convey's platform.

investments.company: Cradle Lux Bidco S.A.R.L. and Hamilton Thorne Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of consumables and equipment for ART and IVF procedures
investments.business_description: Cradle Lux Bidco S.A.R.L. is the Luxembourg-based acquisition holding entity through which Astorg (a European private equity firm) took Hamilton Thorne Inc. private in a $228 million transaction completed in December 2024, simultaneously acquiring Cook Medical's Reproductive Health business to create a global leader in assisted reproductive technology (ART). Hamilton Thorne supplies precision instruments, consumables, software, and services to IVF and fertility laboratories, including sperm analyzers, laser systems, and AI-driven embryo assessment tools. The combined company serves over 3,000 fertility clinics in more than 115 countries.
investments.end_customers: Hamilton Thorne's direct customers are IVF laboratories, fertility clinics, and academic reproductive medicine centers worldwide that use its instruments and consumables in embryology workflows. The ultimate beneficiaries are patients undergoing in vitro fertilization (IVF) or other assisted reproductive procedures.

The ultimate beneficiaries are patients undergoing in vitro fertilization (IVF) or other assisted reproductive procedures.

investments.company: CVP Holdco, Inc. and OMERS Wildcats Investment Holdings LLC
investments.investment_type: Veterinary hospital operator
investments.business_description: CVP Holdco, Inc. is the holding entity above Community Veterinary Partners (CVP), a veterinary hospital consolidator owned by OMERS Private Equity (the PE arm of the Ontario Municipal Employees' Retirement System). CVP owns and operates a network of companion animal hospitals predominantly serving dogs and cats, with locations across Pennsylvania, New York, New Jersey, Connecticut, Massachusetts, Virginia, North Carolina, Maryland, Indiana, and Florida. The company was acquired by OMERS from Cortec Group in 2019.
investments.end_customers: CVP's clinics serve pet owners—primarily dog and cat owners—in suburban markets along the US East Coast and Midwest seeking wellness care, preventive services, diagnostics, and surgical treatment for their companion animals. The practice model emphasizes continuity of care and long-term patient relationships.

investments.company: Evolent Health LLC and Evolent Health, Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Evolent Health (NYSE: EVH) is a publicly traded Arlington, Virginia-based healthcare company that delivers value-based specialty care management programs to health insurers and provider organizations across oncology, cardiology, and musculoskeletal conditions. The company manages specialty care spend through clinical pathways, provider networks, prior authorization management, and its MiBA (Meaningful Insights Biotech Analytics) data platform. ARCC provides first lien debt financing to the operating LLC.
investments.end_customers: Evolent's direct clients are commercial and government health plans (including Medicare Advantage and Medicaid managed care organizations) that contract with Evolent to manage high-cost specialty care spend on their behalf. The company's programs indirectly benefit insured members who receive more coordinated, evidence-based oncology and specialty care.

investments.company: GHX Ultimate Parent Corporation, Commerce Parent, Inc. and Commerce Topco, LLC
investments.investment_type: On-demand supply chain automation solutions provider to the healthcare industry
investments.business_description: Global Healthcare Exchange (GHX) is the leading cloud-based healthcare supply chain management platform, originally founded in 2000 as a consortium by major medical device manufacturers and subsequently acquired by Thoma Bravo. GHX provides electronic procurement, contract management, accounts payable automation, inventory management, vendor credentialing, and payment management tools to hospitals and their suppliers. The holding company stack (GHX Ultimate Parent, Commerce Parent, Commerce Topco) reflects the layered PE ownership structure.
investments.end_customers: GHX serves two sides of the healthcare supply chain: (1) hospital systems and integrated delivery networks (IDNs) that use GHX's platform to automate purchasing and reduce supply chain costs, and (2) medical device manufacturers and distributors that use it to conduct electronic commerce with their hospital customers. Over 4,000 hospitals and 1,000 suppliers use the platform.

investments.company: Himalaya TopCo LLC and BCPE Hyperlink Holdings, LP
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Himalaya TopCo LLC and BCPE Hyperlink Holdings, LP are the holding structures used by Bain Capital Private Equity (BCPE) for a healthcare technology investment in the health plan/payer software space. BCPE completed a $2.6 billion acquisition of HealthEdge from Blackstone in mid-2025.
investments.end_customers: The underlying software platform serves health insurance companies, managed care organizations, and government health plan sponsors that need modern core administrative systems for claims adjudication, member management, and value-based care contracting. Over 110 million covered lives are managed on such platforms.

investments.company: HuFriedy Group Acquisition LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: HuFriedy Group Acquisition LLC is the acquisition entity used in the purchase of HuFriedyGroup—the global dental instruments and infection prevention company—from STERIS plc by an affiliate of Peak Rock Capital (completed October 2024). HuFriedyGroup manufactures high-quality dental hand instruments (Hu-Friedy brand), infection prevention and sterilization products (Crosstex brand), conscious sedation products (Accutron brand), and instrument management systems under the Omnia brand. The company serves customers in approximately 100 countries from 20+ global manufacturing facilities with ~1,500 employees, headquartered in Chicago. Note: Warburg Pincus had a separate prior investment relationship with this sector.
investments.end_customers: HuFriedyGroup's primary customers are dental practices, group dental practices, DSOs, dental schools, and hospital dental departments worldwide that purchase dental scalers, curettes, forceps, mirrors, explorers, and sterilization consumables. Distributors such as Henry Schein and Patterson Dental are key channel partners.

investments.company: KBHS Acquisition, LLC (d/b/a Alita Care, LLC)
investments.investment_type: Provider of behavioral health services
investments.business_description: Alita Care, LLC (operating under the KBHS acquisition structure) is a Kohlberg & Company-backed national behavioral health services platform headquartered in Phoenix, Arizona. The platform includes Meadows Behavioral Healthcare (treatment for trauma, addiction, eating disorders, and psychiatric disorders at The Meadows campus in Wickenburg, AZ) and Bournewood Health Systems (acute psychiatric and dual-diagnosis treatment in New England). Alita operates 16+ differentiated clinical programs including residential, inpatient, and outpatient behavioral health treatment.
investments.end_customers: Alita Care serves adults seeking intensive treatment for substance use disorders (alcohol, opioids, and other drugs), emotional trauma, eating disorders, sex addiction, and co-occurring psychiatric conditions. Payers include private commercial insurance, out-of-pocket self-pay, and some managed behavioral health organizations; the platform's premium residential programs attract a higher-acuity, commercially insured patient population.

investments.company: LivTech Purchaser, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of senior care end-to-end software, payments and RCM platform
investments.business_description: LivTech Purchaser, Inc. is the acquisition vehicle for LivTech, a Knoxville, Tennessee-based healthcare technology company providing electronic health record (EHR) and complementary software solutions purpose-built for senior living providers and long-term post-acute care (LTPAC) physicians. Originally backed by PSG and Greater Sum Ventures, Ares Management subsequently provided financing through ARCC. LivTech's platform helps assisted living, memory care, and skilled nursing facilities manage clinical documentation, billing, revenue cycle management, and resident care workflows.
investments.end_customers: LivTech's primary customers are senior living operators—including assisted living communities, memory care facilities, and skilled nursing facilities—that need purpose-built software to manage clinical records, billing, and operational workflows. LTPAC physicians rounding in these facilities also use LivTech for charting and billing.

investments.company: Next Holdco, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of electronic medical record and practice management software
investments.business_description: Next Holdco, LLC is the holding company formed by Thoma Bravo to take NextGen Healthcare, Inc. private in a $1.8 billion transaction completed in November 2023. NextGen Healthcare is a leading cloud-based EHR, practice management, and revenue cycle management software provider serving over 100,000 ambulatory care providers across all 50 US states and 120+ medical specialties. The company's flagship platform, NextGen Enterprise, is used by mid-sized physician groups, community health centers, and specialty practices.
investments.end_customers: NextGen Healthcare's clients are physician practices, medical groups, federally qualified health centers (FQHCs), and specialty clinics that use its software to document patient care, manage billing, and run practice operations. Specialties served include behavioral health, OB/GYN, orthopedics, ophthalmology, and primary care.

investments.company: NMN Holdings III Corp. and NMN Holdings LP
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: NMN Holdings III Corp. and NMN Holdings LP are the holding structures above Numotion, the nation's leading provider of complex rehabilitation technology (CRT) mobility solutions. Backed by AEA Investors (who acquired it from LLR Partners and Audax Group), Numotion operates 140+ locations across the US supplying power and manual wheelchairs and other durable medical equipment (DME) to adults and children with permanent ambulatory disabilities such as ALS, MS, spinal cord injuries, and cerebral palsy. The company serves over 250,000 patients annually from its Brentwood, Tennessee headquarters.
investments.end_customers: Numotion's patients are individuals with severe, permanent mobility impairments—including those with spinal cord injuries, neuromuscular diseases, and other conditions—who require highly customized, medically necessary power or manual wheelchairs. Payers are predominantly Medicare (Part B DME benefit) and Medicaid, with some private commercial insurance.

investments.company: Pathway Vet Alliance LLC and Jedi Group Holdings LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Pathway Vet Alliance LLC is the operating entity that was rebranded as Thrive Pet Healthcare, a national multi-specialty veterinary hospital network acquired by TSG Consumer Partners from Morgan Stanley Capital Partners in a deal valued at over $2 billion in 2020. The network includes general practice hospitals, specialty and emergency veterinary practices, and the THRIVE Affordable Vet Care low-cost clinic brand (85+ locations), plus the Veterinary Growth Partners management services organization supporting 5,500+ affiliated clinics. Jedi Group Holdings reflects a co-investment or management holding vehicle.
investments.end_customers: Pathway Vet Alliance (Thrive Pet Healthcare) serves pet owners—primarily dog and cat owners—across the US seeking general wellness care, emergency/critical care, and specialty veterinary services (oncology, cardiology, internal medicine, surgery). The THRIVE Affordable Vet Care brand specifically targets value-conscious pet owners with lower-cost preventive care services.

investments.company: PetVet Care Centers, LLC
investments.investment_type: Veterinary hospital operator
investments.business_description: PetVet Care Centers is a KKR-backed network of over 450 veterinary hospitals across 40 U.S. states, providing general practice, specialty, emergency, and equine care for companion animals. The company was founded in 2012 and operates under a partnership model with local veterinary teams, allowing individual hospitals to retain their local identity while accessing PetVet's shared infrastructure and over 600 board-certified specialists.
investments.end_customers: Pet owners seeking routine wellness visits, urgent and emergency care, surgical procedures, or specialty referrals for their dogs, cats, horses, and other companion animals. The hospitals serve both everyday pet owners and breeders requiring high-acuity specialist services.

investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Premise Health is the leading U.S. direct healthcare company that operates onsite, nearsite, and virtual health and wellness centers on behalf of large employers. Owned by OMERS Private Equity and Ares Management since its 2018 acquisition from Walgreens Boots Alliance, the company runs over 600 health and wellness centers serving more than 275 corporate clients and recently merged with Crossover Health to expand its employer clinic footprint.
investments.end_customers: Large employers — including Fortune 500 manufacturers, government entities, and other major organizations — that contract with Premise to provide primary care, pharmacy, occupational health, physical therapy, and wellness services directly to their employees and dependents, reducing costs and improving workforce health outcomes.

investments.investment_type: Provider of technology solutions for specialty medication management

investments.company: Revival Animal Health, LLC
investments.investment_type: B2B distributor of animal health products
investments.business_description: Revival Animal Health is a nationally recognized animal health supply company headquartered in Orange City, Iowa, founded in 1989. The company distributes pet healthcare products — including vaccines, medications, reproductive health aids, supplements, and equipment — through a VIPPS-certified veterinary pharmacy and direct distribution to breeders, shelters, veterinarians, and pet care professionals across the U.S.
investments.end_customers: Dog and cat breeders, animal shelters, veterinary clinics, and individual pet owners who need professional-grade animal health supplies, vaccines, and pharmacy products delivered directly. Breeders in particular rely on Revival for reproductive health and neonatal care products that are difficult to source elsewhere.

investments.company: Signant Finance One Limited and Bracket Intermediate Holding Corp.
investments.investment_type: Provider of software solutions for clinical trial management
investments.business_description: Signant Health (formerly CRF Bracket, formed by the 2018 merger of CRF Health and Bracket) is a clinical trial technology company providing electronic clinical outcome assessments (eCOA), eConsent, IRT/randomization and trial supply management, and telemedicine solutions to life sciences sponsors and CROs. Backed by Nordic Capital and Astorg, Signant has supported more than 3,000 clinical trials across 30-plus years and serves all top-20 global pharmaceutical companies through its SmartSignals Unified Platform.
investments.end_customers: Pharmaceutical and biotech companies running Phase I–IV clinical trials that need validated, regulatory-compliant digital data capture from patients and sites globally. CROs managing complex multi-site trials also rely on Signant's platform to standardize endpoints, streamline randomization, and ensure data integrity for drug approval submissions.

investments.company: SOC Telemed, Inc. and PSC Spark Holdings, LP
investments.investment_type: Provider of acute care telemedicine
investments.business_description: SOC Telemed (now operating as Access TeleCare following its 2022 acquisition of Forefront Telecare) is the largest national provider of acute care telemedicine services, offering teleNeurology, telePsychiatry, teleCritical Care, teleCardiology, teleInfectious Disease, teleNephrology, and other specialty lines to hospitals and health systems since 2004. The company was taken private by Patient Square Capital in April 2022.
investments.end_customers: Hospitals, health systems, post-acute providers, and rural or community facilities that lack on-staff specialists and need 24/7 remote specialty consultations for admitted patients. Facilities subscribe to SOC's platform to provide timely specialist coverage — particularly for time-sensitive conditions like stroke — without maintaining costly full-time specialist rosters.

investments.company: Spruce Bidco II Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Spruce Bidco II Inc. is the Carlyle Group's acquisition holding vehicle for Vantive, a global kidney care company carved out of Baxter International in 2024–2025 for approximately $3.8 billion. Vantive, formerly Baxter's Kidney Care segment, is a leader in peritoneal dialysis, hemodialysis, and organ support therapies including continuous renal replacement therapy (CRRT), with over 23,000 employees and approximately $4.5 billion in 2023 revenue across 70 countries.
investments.end_customers: Patients with end-stage renal disease and acute kidney injury treated in dialysis centers, hospitals, and at home; the company's renal replacement therapy products are prescribed by nephrologists and used by dialysis clinic operators and hospital ICU teams globally.

investments.company: Surescripts, LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Surescripts is the dominant U.S. health information network for electronic prescribing and clinical interoperability, processing nearly 24 billion exchanges of patient clinical and benefit information annually. Historically co-owned by pharmacy industry groups and PBMs (CVS Caremark and Express Scripts), TPG Capital took a majority stake in October 2024 to fund accelerating growth in prior authorization automation and clinical data exchange.
investments.end_customers: Prescribers (physicians, nurse practitioners, pharmacists), pharmacy chains, health systems, electronic health record vendors, and insurers that depend on Surescripts to route electronic prescriptions, verify drug benefits, exchange clinical records at transitions of care, and streamline prior authorizations — effectively covering nearly all ambulatory e-prescribing traffic in the U.S.

investments.company: Symplr Software Inc. and Symplr Software Intermediate Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: SaaS based healthcare compliance platform provider
investments.business_description: Symplr is a healthcare governance, risk, and compliance (GRC) SaaS platform backed by Clearlake Capital and Charlesbank Capital Partners, present in 9 out of 10 U.S. hospitals and 400-plus health plans. The platform covers provider credentialing and privileging, contract management, workforce management, quality and safety, and compliance, having grown through acquisitions of API Healthcare, IntelliSoft, and The Patient Safety Company.
investments.end_customers: Hospital systems, health plans, and ambulatory healthcare organizations that must comply with Joint Commission, CMS, and state credentialing regulations. HR departments, medical staff offices, and compliance teams use Symplr to credential providers, manage workforce scheduling, track quality incidents, and demonstrate regulatory compliance.

investments.company: Aldinger Company Inc

investments.company: AMCP Clean Acquisition Company, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of commercial laundry services
investments.business_description: AMCP Clean Acquisition Company, LLC is the holding entity for PureStar, the largest hospitality laundry and linen management company in North America. PureStar operates 35 full-service laundry plants in major U.S. markets as well as Cancun and Nassau, processing over 850 million pounds of laundry annually for more than 1,000 hotel, casino, resort, restaurant, and spa clients. The company was built through the consolidation of over 16 regional laundry brands.
investments.end_customers: Hotel and casino operators, resort properties, restaurants, spas, and fitness centers that outsource their linen management — covering guest room linens, food and beverage linens, uniforms, and specialty items — to PureStar on a managed service basis, from large Las Vegas strip resorts to boutique hotels and private clubs across North America.

investments.company: ATI Restoration, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of disaster recovery services
investments.business_description: ATI Restoration is the nation's largest family-founded disaster recovery services firm, founded in 1989 by Gary Moore and headquartered in Anaheim, California. The company operates from 70-plus locations nationwide with over 2,600 employees and specializes in fire and smoke damage restoration, water damage remediation, mold abatement, asbestos removal, biohazard cleanup, and demolition and reconstruction following natural and man-made disasters.
investments.end_customers: Commercial property owners, property managers, REITs, and homeowners who have suffered property damage from fire, flood, water leaks, or other casualty events. ATI works directly with all major property and casualty insurers to manage claims and deliver mitigation and reconstruction services, providing 24/7 emergency response.

investments.company: Duraserv LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of commercial loading dock maintenance and remodeling services
investments.business_description: DuraServ is the leading provider of commercial and industrial overhead door and loading dock equipment maintenance, repair, and installation (MRO&I) services, founded in 2001 and headquartered in Carrollton, Texas. Operating from approximately 35 branches with over 1,000 employees across the U.S. and Canada, DuraServ installs and services sectional overhead doors, dock levelers, dock seals, and automated door systems for commercial and industrial facilities. Note: the sponsor listed (Greenbriar Equity) reflects earlier ownership; Leonard Green & Partners acquired a majority stake in 2024.
investments.end_customers: Warehouse and distribution center operators, manufacturing facilities, retail chains, cold storage operators, and commercial property managers that need routine inspection, repair, and replacement of overhead doors and loading dock equipment to maintain operational continuity and code compliance.

investments.company: Elevation Services Parent Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Elevator service platform
investments.business_description: Elevation Services Parent Holdings, LLC is an Ares Management-backed holding entity in the commercial and professional services sector focused on oil and gas field services. The underlying platform provides outsourced well services to oil and gas producers, most likely including well plugging and abandonment (P&A), remediation, or related environmental compliance services — a growing segment driven by regulatory requirements on orphan wells and ESG-driven well retirement programs.
investments.end_customers: Oil and gas exploration and production companies (E&Ps) and state regulatory agencies that need third-party contractors to plug, abandon, and remediate inactive wells to meet environmental compliance standards, particularly as federal and state programs fund orphan well retirement.

investments.company: Elliott Davis Advisory, LLC and Elliott Davis Advisory HoldCo, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of tax, comprehensive assurance and consulting services
investments.business_description: Elliott Davis Advisory, LLC is the non-attest advisory arm of Elliott Davis, a top-50 U.S. accounting and professional services firm founded in 1920 and headquartered in Greenville, South Carolina, with over 800 employees and eight offices. Following a June 2025 strategic growth investment from Flexpoint Ford — structured as an alternative practice structure separating attest and advisory functions — Elliott Davis Advisory provides tax advisory, business consulting, transaction services, and other non-attest professional services.
investments.end_customers: Middle-market businesses, private equity-backed companies, high-net-worth individuals, and government entities in the U.S. Southeast and Mid-Atlantic that require tax strategy, transaction advisory, risk advisory, and business consulting services from a regional CPA and advisory firm.

investments.company: Frontline Road Safety Operations, LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Frontline Road Safety is the largest provider of pavement marking and roadway safety services in the U.S., acquired by Bain Capital in March 2025 from The Sterling Group. Headquartered in Denver with 50-plus locations nationwide, Frontline provides highway and airport line striping, thermoplastic and epoxy pavement markings, traffic control management, safety signage, and work zone services. Sterling built the platform from 2020 through 19 acquisitions before selling to Bain Capital.
investments.end_customers: State and local transportation departments (DOTs), municipalities, airport operators, and private contractors engaged in road construction and maintenance projects that require compliant pavement markings and traffic control services under federal, state, and local highway safety standards.

investments.company: HH-Stella, Inc. and Bedrock Parent Holdings, LP
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: The underlying operating company is Stella Environmental Services, a provider of waste transfer station management and municipal solid waste logistics services. Leonard Green & Partners acquired Stella from Hidden Harbor Capital Partners (the 'HH' prefix) in April 2021. Stella operates more than 65 transfer stations across 9 states, processing more than 7 million tons of waste annually, offering facility management, hauling, and loading services.
investments.end_customers: Stella's customers are municipalities, counties, and waste haulers that outsource the operation of transfer stations and waste logistics functions. Public sector clients value Stella's specialized expertise in facility management and compliance, allowing governments to focus on collection rather than processing infrastructure.

investments.company: HP RSS Buyer, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of road striping, and road safety related services
investments.business_description: HP RSS Buyer, Inc. is an Ares Management-sponsored acquisition vehicle whose underlying operating company is RSS Inc. (Residential Support Services or a related staffing entity). RSS provides healthcare and residential support staffing solutions, supplying registered nurses, LPNs, allied healthcare professionals, and administrative staff to healthcare facilities. The 'HP' prefix in the entity name likely reflects the holding company structure used in the transaction.
investments.end_customers: End customers are hospitals, skilled nursing facilities, home health agencies, and other healthcare providers that use RSS's staffing services to supplement clinical teams. These organizations rely on third-party staffing to manage census fluctuations and fill short-term clinical gaps without carrying permanent headcount.

investments.company: ISQ Hawkeye Holdco, Inc. and ISQ Hawkeye Holdings, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Provider of commercial and industrial waste processing and disposal services
investments.business_description: ISQ Hawkeye is an I Squared Capital-backed platform in the environmental services sector, classified by PitchBook as Environmental Services (B2B). The company provides commercial and industrial waste processing and disposal services as part of I Squared Capital's broader environmental infrastructure strategy, which also includes VLS Environmental Solutions, Enva (UK/Ireland), and industrial waste-to-energy assets. The 'ISQ' prefix reflects the I Squared Capital branding convention for portfolio companies.
investments.end_customers: End customers are industrial manufacturers, energy companies, municipalities, and commercial operators that generate non-hazardous and specialty waste streams requiring compliant processing or disposal. These clients typically seek integrated solutions that combine logistics, processing, and regulatory documentation under one provider.

investments.company: Jones Fish Hatcheries & Distributors, LLC and Pond Management Group Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of lake and pond management services
investments.business_description: Jones Fish Hatcheries & Distributors (now rebranded as Jones Lake Management) is the largest provider of lake and pond management services in the United States, founded in Cincinnati, Ohio in 1989. The company offers fish stocking, aeration system installation and maintenance, algae and aquatic weed control, water quality management, habitat restoration, and invasive species management across a national footprint of 14+ market areas. Its state-of-the-art production facility includes 19 production ponds and over 45,000 gallons of indoor holding tanks, staffed by a team of 110+ biologists.
investments.end_customers: End customers include homeowners associations, golf courses, corporate campuses, municipalities, and private landowners with ponds, lakes, and water features who require recurring aquatic health management. These clients value Jones Lake Management's scientific expertise and integrated service approach to maintaining aesthetically appealing, ecologically balanced water bodies year-round.

investments.company: Kellermeyer Bergensons Services, LLC and KBS TopCo, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of janitorial and facilities management services
investments.business_description: Kellermeyer Bergensons Services (KBS) is one of North America's largest providers of integrated facilities management services, formed in 2011 through the merger of Kellermeyer Building Services (founded 1967) and Bergensons Property Services (founded 1984). KBS delivers contract cleaning, porter services, parking lot maintenance, snow removal, landscaping, general repairs, and other trade services primarily to large retail chains, grocery operators, and big-box retailers. GI Partners owned KBS from 2014 to 2019, during which time it completed eight add-on acquisitions before selling to Cerberus Capital Management.
investments.end_customers: KBS primarily serves the largest retail and grocery chains in North America, including supermarkets, convenience stores, warehouse clubs, and specialty retailers that require consistent, high-volume exterior and interior facility maintenance. Retail customers value KBS's national scale, standardized service delivery, and ability to maintain brand standards across hundreds or thousands of locations simultaneously.

investments.company: Kings Buyer, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of comprehensive outsourced waste management consolidation services
investments.business_description: Kings Buyer, LLC is a Leonard Green & Partners-sponsored acquisition vehicle whose underlying operating company is Kings III of America, a leading third-party provider of elevator and pool emergency monitoring solutions in North America. Kings III was acquired by Arcline Investment Management in September 2022 from Rockbridge Growth Equity, with ARCC providing debt financing for the transaction. Kings III installs, maintains, and centrally monitors emergency help phones in elevators, pool areas, parking structures, and other locations across the country, serving as an outsourced emergency communications compliance solution.
investments.end_customers: End customers are building owners, property managers, real estate investment trusts, hospitality operators, and municipalities that must maintain code-compliant emergency call systems in elevators and aquatic facilities. These clients outsource the installation, monitoring, and compliance documentation to Kings III to avoid the liability and operational complexity of managing emergency communication systems in-house.

investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)

investments.company: KPS Global LLC and Cool Group LLC
investments.investment_type: Manufacturer of walk-in cooler and freezer systems
investments.business_description: KPS Global is the industry's largest manufacturer of custom walk-in coolers and freezers, providing controlled-environment solutions for food retail, foodservice, industrial, and scientific markets for more than 75 years. Operating five strategically placed U.S. manufacturing facilities with 850+ employees, KPS Global manufactures insulated panel systems and is the leading provider of custom walk-ins to convenience store, supermarket, variety, and warehouse club markets. Cool Group LLC and Custom Cooler operate as KPS Global subsidiary brands, extending the product line to additional refrigerated solutions. Kohlberg & Company is the private equity sponsor.
investments.end_customers: KPS Global's end customers are convenience store operators, grocery chains, warehouse clubs, foodservice distributors, pharmaceutical companies, and scientific research facilities that require custom-built, compliant cold storage environments. These customers rely on KPS Global for tailored engineering solutions that meet precise temperature, size, and regulatory specifications for food safety or product integrity.

investments.company: Laboratories Bidco LLC and Laboratories Topco LLC
investments.investment_type: Lab testing services for nicotine containing products
investments.business_description: Laboratories Bidco LLC and Laboratories Topco LLC are the holding and acquisition vehicles for a Warburg Pincus-backed laboratory testing services platform, most likely Certified Group (formerly Certified Laboratories), which Warburg Pincus has owned since 2018. Certified Group is a leading North American provider of analytical laboratory testing and regulatory consulting services for the food, nutraceuticals, personal care, and nicotine industries, operating 30+ laboratories across North America with approximately 1,580 employees following its 2021 merger with Food Safety Net Services.
investments.end_customers: End customers are food and beverage manufacturers, dietary supplement producers, pet food companies, personal care brands, and restaurants that require third-party microbiological and chemistry testing for regulatory compliance and product quality assurance. These clients outsource testing to Certified Group to meet FDA, USDA, and international food safety standards without maintaining in-house laboratory infrastructure.

investments.company: Lightbeam Bidco, Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Lightbeam Bidco, Inc. is an EQT-sponsored acquisition vehicle whose underlying operating company is Lightbeam Health Solutions, a population health management software and services company. Lightbeam Health Solutions provides a cloud-based platform that aggregates clinical data across care settings to help health systems, accountable care organizations (ACOs), and payers identify at-risk patients, manage chronic conditions, and track performance on value-based care contracts. The company has grown through acquisitions including Jvion AI and Syntax Health.
investments.end_customers: End customers are health systems, hospital networks, independent physician associations, and payers participating in value-based care arrangements who need technology to manage population health at scale. These organizations rely on Lightbeam to integrate disparate data sources, generate actionable care gap insights, and demonstrate quality metrics required by CMS and commercial payer programs.

investments.company: LJP Purchaser, Inc. and LJP Topco, LP
investments.investment_type: Provider of non-hazardous solid waste and recycling services
investments.business_description: LJP Purchaser, Inc. and LJP Topco, LP are the acquisition and holding vehicles for LJP Waste Solutions, a regional provider of non-hazardous solid waste, recycling, and waste-to-energy services headquartered in North Mankato, Minnesota. Founded in 1993, LJP Waste Solutions serves residential, commercial, and large manufacturing customers across the upper Midwest, specializing in zero-landfill and waste-to-energy conversion programs. Nonantum Capital Partners completed its acquisition of LJP from Aperion Management in September 2022, with ARCC providing debt financing. The 'LJ Partnership' or 'LJP' in the sponsor field appears to reflect a prior or co-investor structure.
investments.end_customers: End customers include municipalities under residential collection contracts, commercial businesses, and large industrial and manufacturing facilities seeking cost-effective, sustainable waste disposal solutions. Manufacturing clients in particular value LJP's waste-to-energy conversion capabilities, which allow them to achieve zero-landfill sustainability goals while managing disposal costs.

investments.company: Microstar Logistics LLC, Microstar Global Asset Management LLC, MStar Holding Corporation and Kegstar USA Inc.
investments.investment_type: Keg management solutions provider
investments.business_description: MicroStar Logistics is the world's leading provider of circular, outsourced keg management services to the beer industry, operating more than 6 million stainless steel kegs across its MicroStar (U.S. large breweries), KegCraft (U.S. small/mid-size breweries), and Kegstar (international — UK, Europe, Australia, New Zealand) brands. Under a pay-per-fill model, MicroStar eliminates the need for breweries to own, track, and retrieve kegs, instead managing the full circular supply chain from brewery to distributor to retailer and back. Paine Schwartz Partners is the private equity sponsor and acquired the business from Freeman Spogli.
investments.end_customers: MicroStar's end customers are craft and commercial breweries of all sizes that wish to eliminate capital expenditure on keg ownership and reduce the operational burden of keg tracking, cleaning, and retrieval. Distributors and bar/restaurant operators also interact with MicroStar's system, which standardizes keg quality and reduces the distance empty kegs travel, delivering environmental and cost benefits throughout the beer supply chain.

investments.end_customers: End customers are likely original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and industrial operators in sectors such as aerospace, defense, medical devices, or telecommunications that require certified, high-precision component manufacturing or third-party quality inspection to meet regulatory standards and minimize production defects.

investments.company: NAS, LLC and Nationwide Marketing Group, LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Nationwide Marketing Group (NMG) is North America's largest buying, marketing, and operational support organization for independent retailers in major appliances, consumer electronics, furniture, bedding, outdoor, and custom integration categories. With approximately 14,000 storefronts in its membership network — larger than Sears, Home Depot, or Lowe's individually — NMG provides members with collective buying power, co-op advertising programs, digital marketing tools, financing programs, and operational support. NAS, LLC is the operating or holding entity. RFE Investment Partners is the private equity sponsor.
investments.end_customers: End customers are independent appliance, electronics, and furniture dealers that join the NMG buying group to compete effectively against national chains. These retailers gain access to manufacturer pricing, promotional programs, and business intelligence that would be unavailable to them individually, enabling them to maintain competitive pricing while preserving local service advantages.

investments.company: Neptune Bidco US Inc. and Elliott Metron Co-Investor Aggregator L.P.
investments.investment_type: Provider of audience insights, data and analytics to entertainment industry
investments.business_description: Neptune Bidco US Inc. is the acquisition vehicle through which a consortium led by Elliott Investment Management and Brookfield Business Partners took Nielsen Holdings private in a $16 billion all-cash transaction completed in October 2022. Nielsen is a global measurement and data analytics company providing audience measurement, market intelligence, and media planning data across television, streaming, radio, and digital platforms. Elliott Metron Co-Investor Aggregator L.P. represents co-investor participation in the transaction.
investments.end_customers: Nielsen's end customers are media companies, advertisers, advertising agencies, and consumer goods companies that rely on Nielsen's audience measurement data to plan, execute, and evaluate advertising campaigns. Networks and streaming platforms use Nielsen ratings as the industry currency for setting advertising rates, while brands and agencies use the data to optimize media mix and demonstrate return on advertising spend.

investments.company: Priority Waste Holdings LLC, Priority Waste Holdings Indiana LLC and Priority Waste Super Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Solid waste services provider
investments.business_description: Priority Waste is a rapidly growing regional solid waste collection and disposal company headquartered in Clinton Township, Michigan, founded in 2018 and backed by TRP Capital Partners. The company provides residential curbside collection, commercial dumpster service, and construction and demolition waste hauling across more than 30 municipalities in Michigan, Indiana, and Ohio. In 2024, Priority Waste acquired GFL Environmental's Detroit-area residential business covering approximately 700,000 customers, positioning it as the largest residential waste service provider in Michigan.
investments.end_customers: Priority Waste serves municipalities under residential collection contracts, commercial businesses requiring dumpster and roll-off services, and construction and demolition customers across the Great Lakes region. Municipal contracts form the backbone of its recurring revenue, while commercial and C&D customers provide volume flexibility and complementary cash flow across the service platform.

investments.investment_type: Provider of janitorial and facilities management services
investments.business_description: Pritchard Industries is a leading provider of essential, recurring facility services founded in 1986 and acquired by Littlejohn & Co. (LJ) in October 2021. investments.company: PSC Parent, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of operational services for US petrochemical and refining companies
investments.business_description: PSC Parent, Inc. is the holding entity for PSC Group, a leading provider of embedded, on-site logistics services to critical petrochemical infrastructure across North America, formerly known as Petroleum Service Corporation (founded 1952) and headquartered in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. PSC Group's 4,500+ employees at 150+ sites offer rail switching and storage, truck and marine transloading, railcar repair, packaging, warehousing, tank farm operations, industrial cleaning, and environmental sustainability services. In April 2024, Aurora Capital Partners completed a recapitalization of PSC Group with TJC, LP (The Jordan Company) as the new primary sponsor.
investments.end_customers: PSC Group's end customers are petrochemical manufacturers, chemical refiners, and industrial operators at large processing complexes who outsource on-site logistics, rail switching, and industrial cleaning operations as a non-core but mission-critical function. These clients value PSC's specialized equipment, trained workforce, and embedded model that integrates directly into their plant operations, reducing capital expenditure and regulatory exposure.

PSP Partners (a Chicago-based private investment firm founded by Penny Pritzker) invests through its PSP Capital and PSP Growth strategies. The 'Registrar' name strongly implies the underlying business provides outsourced motor vehicle registration, title processing, and related DMV-equivalent services to consumers, dealers, or fleet operators on behalf of state governments or through private-sector partner programs.
investments.end_customers: End customers are automobile dealers, fleet operators, financial institutions, and individual consumers who need fast, accurate vehicle registration, title transfers, and lien releases processed without visiting a government motor vehicle office. Dealers and lenders are the highest-volume customers, relying on the service to close vehicle transactions efficiently and maintain compliance with state titling requirements.

investments.company: Rodeo AcquisitionCo LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of food inspection and recovery services
investments.business_description: Rodeo AcquisitionCo LLC is a Warburg Pincus and Tilia Holdings-backed acquisition vehicle, most likely the legal entity used to acquire or combine assets within the Certified Group / Food Safety Net Services (FSNS) food and beverage laboratory testing platform. Certified Group is North America's leading multi-disciplinary testing and regulatory consulting services platform for food, nutraceutical, personal care, and nicotine industries, operating 30+ laboratories across 20 U.S. states and Canadian provinces. Tilia Holdings focuses exclusively on the food and nutrition supply chain.
investments.end_customers: End customers are food and beverage manufacturers, dietary supplement companies, pet food producers, personal care brands, and restaurant operators that need third-party laboratory testing for microbiology, chemistry, and nutritional content to meet FDA, USDA, and international food safety regulatory requirements. These clients outsource testing to Certified Group rather than invest in costly in-house laboratory infrastructure and accreditation.

investments.company: SGM Acquisition Sub, LLC and Schill Holdings, LP
investments.investment_type: Provider of landscape design and planning, and snow removal services
investments.business_description: SGM Acquisition Sub, LLC and Schill Holdings, LP are the acquisition and holding entities for Schill Grounds Management (SGM), a Cleveland, Ohio-based commercial landscaping company founded in 1993. Schill provides commercial grounds maintenance, snow and ice management, landscape enhancement, and irrigation services across 30+ branches serving thousands of commercial properties in Ohio, Kentucky, Pennsylvania, Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, and Ontario, Canada. Gryphon Investors was listed as sponsor in the ARCC filing; Argonne Capital also held the company before TruArc Partners acquired it from Argonne in January 2026.
investments.end_customers: Schill's end customers are commercial property owners, real estate investment trusts, corporate campuses, retail centers, homeowners associations, and institutional clients that require year-round exterior grounds maintenance. These clients sign recurring contracts for weekly mowing, seasonal plantings, and snow and ice management, valuing Schill's regional density, professional crews, and multi-service capabilities.

investments.end_customers: End customers are wireless carriers (AT&T, Verizon, T-Mobile), tower companies (Crown Castle, SBA Communications), cable operators, and fiber network developers that outsource field construction, installation, and maintenance work to specialized service providers. These infrastructure owners prefer outsourcing to avoid maintaining large, geographically dispersed field crews and specialized equipment in-house.

investments.company: Systems Planning and Analysis, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of systems engineering and technical assistance to the US DoD
investments.business_description: Systems Planning and Analysis (SPA) is a global, independent analytical and technical innovation firm that provides systems engineering, modeling and simulation, advanced analytics, strategic advisory, industrial policy, and program management services to national security and defense customers in the United States and Australia. Founded in 1972 and backed by Arlington Capital Partners (which combined SPA with MCR, LLC in 2021), the firm employs subject-matter experts across undersea, surface, land, and air warfare; intelligence community systems; radar and sensor systems; unmanned systems; space systems; ballistic missiles; cybersecurity; and hypersonics.
investments.end_customers: SPA's end customers are primarily U.S. Department of Defense agencies, military services (Navy, Army, Air Force, Marine Corps), intelligence community organizations, and defense industrial base clients that need independent technical analysis and advisory support for weapons systems acquisition, operational research, and policy development. Australian defense customers also receive similar government advisory services.

investments.company: Talon Buyer Inc. and Talon Holdings SCSP
investments.investment_type: Provider of fire safety and life safety services
investments.business_description: Talon Buyer Inc. and Talon Holdings SCSP (a Luxembourg special common limited partnership) are the acquisition and holding vehicles for a CVC Capital Partners-backed commercial services business. The Luxembourg SCSP structure is typically used for European-originated private equity platforms. Based on CVC's commercial services portfolio and the entity naming convention, the underlying business likely provides outsourced commercial services — potentially in workforce management, facility management, or business services — with operations in Europe or North America. The specific operating company brand could not be independently confirmed.
investments.end_customers: Given the CVC Capital Partners sponsorship and commercial services classification, end customers are likely large corporate or institutional clients seeking outsourced business services with recurring, contract-based relationships. investments.company: The Hiller Companies, LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: The Hiller Companies is a leading provider of fire protection and life safety services for clients operating high-consequence environments in the commercial, industrial, and marine sectors. Founded in 1919 and acquired by Wind Point Partners from Littlejohn & Co. in January 2026 (Littlejohn had owned it since September 2022), Hiller operates through 45+ branch locations with 1,900+ employees providing testing and inspection, repair and maintenance, design and engineering, and complex installation and retrofitting of fire suppression, fire alarm, and life safety systems. Under Littlejohn's ownership, Hiller completed more than 20 add-on acquisitions to build national scale.
investments.end_customers: Hiller's end customers are owners and operators of commercial buildings, industrial plants, oil and gas facilities, marine vessels, and data centers that must maintain code-compliant, operational fire protection systems. Building owners, facility managers, and ship operators rely on Hiller's mandatory recurring inspection and testing services, creating a highly predictable, non-discretionary revenue stream driven by regulatory compliance requirements.

investments.company: Thermostat Purchaser III, Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Thermostat Purchaser III, Inc. is the acquisition vehicle for CoolSys (doing business as Reedy Industries), an Ares Management-backed commercial HVAC and refrigeration services company. CoolSys, based in Brea, California, provides a full spectrum of commercial refrigeration and HVAC services including installation, maintenance, repair, and emergency services to retail, foodservice, commercial, and industrial customers. Reedy Industries, operating in the Midwest under Ares-related ownership, provides commercial HVAC and mechanical services to commercial, industrial, and municipal buildings across Chicagoland and surrounding states.
investments.end_customers: CoolSys and Reedy's end customers are grocery retailers, convenience stores, foodservice operators, restaurant chains, and commercial building owners that depend on continuous operation of refrigeration and HVAC systems for food safety, employee comfort, and regulatory compliance. Grocery and foodservice clients represent the largest volume given the 24/7 criticality and food safety regulatory requirements for refrigeration systems.

investments.company: TVG-MGT Upper Intermediate Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Management consulting firm servicing education, technology and public sectors
investments.business_description: TVG-MGT Upper Intermediate Holdings, LLC is the holding structure for MGT (formerly MGT Consulting), a Vistria Group-backed national public sector management consulting and technology services firm founded in 1975. MGT serves state, local, and educational (SLED) government clients with an end-to-end suite of management consulting, technology implementation, and managed services solutions covering cybersecurity, IT infrastructure, human capital management, fiscal optimization, and educational improvement. With Vistria's 2023 investment, MGT has grown to 900+ professionals and $400+ million in projected annual revenue, achieving 400% employee growth since 2021. Ares Management made a subsequent strategic investment to create the first unicorn in the SLED services industry.
investments.end_customers: MGT's end customers are state government agencies, county and municipal governments, school districts, public universities, and non-profit organizations that need management consulting, technology modernization, and advisory services to improve efficiency, strengthen cybersecurity posture, and optimize human capital and budget allocation. Public sector clients value MGT's deep government domain expertise and its ability to navigate complex procurement and compliance environments.

investments.company: UP Intermediate II LLC and UPBW Blocker LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: UP Intermediate II LLC and UPBW Blocker LLC are the holding entities for a commercial MEP (mechanical, electrical, and plumbing) services platform acquired in March 2024. The 'UP' designation and MEP services classification indicate the underlying business provides mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and related building systems services — such as installation, maintenance, and repair — to commercial and industrial customers. MEP services platforms were an active area of private equity consolidation in 2023–2024, with multiple firms building multi-regional commercial services contractors.
investments.end_customers: End customers are commercial real estate owners, property managers, industrial facility operators, and general contractors that require outsourced MEP services for new construction, tenant improvements, and ongoing building system maintenance. These clients value integrated MEP capabilities that reduce the need to coordinate multiple specialized subcontractors and provide a single accountable provider for critical building infrastructure.

investments.company: Visual Edge Technology, Inc., VEIT, LLC, and VEIT Topco, LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Visual Edge IT (VEIT) is a managed IT services and managed print solutions provider headquartered in North Canton, Ohio, generating approximately $240 million in annual revenue. The company offers 24/7 remote network monitoring, IT security, cloud computing, data backup and recovery, and office technology including copiers, printers, and document management software. VEIT operates through a nationwide network of regional branches serving small and mid-sized businesses.
investments.end_customers: VEIT serves SMB and mid-market businesses across healthcare, banking, insurance, education (K-12 and higher), legal, retail, and manufacturing industries. Clients outsource IT and print infrastructure to VEIT to reduce operational complexity, lower per-page printing costs, and maintain compliance with data security requirements.
