investments.company: VRC Companies, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of records and information management services
investments.business_description: VRC Companies is the parent of Vital Records Control (VRC), a records management and information governance company providing physical document storage, secure shredding, digital document scanning, and information lifecycle management services to businesses across multiple industries. VRC competes with Iron Mountain and Recall in the records management market, primarily serving mid-market clients with physical records storage and destruction needs. The company manages secure storage of paper documents, x-rays, and other physical media on behalf of its customers.
investments.end_customers: End customers are healthcare organizations, law firms, financial institutions, corporations, and government agencies that generate and retain significant volumes of physical documents required for compliance or business operations. These organizations outsource records storage and management to VRC to reduce internal space costs, ensure secure controlled access, and comply with document retention and destruction policies.

investments.company: W.S. Connelly & Co., LLC and WSC Ultimate Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: W.S. Connelly is a value-added distributor of consumable agronomics products including plant protection chemicals, seeds, fertilizers, soil surfactants, and erosion control materials. The company serves lawn care, landscape, golf, and erosion control professionals through multiple regional subsidiary brands such as AmeriTurf and Chesapeake Valley Seed. It was acquired by Incline Equity Partners in 2024 and has a prior Ares Management involvement.
investments.end_customers: Customers are professional turf management contractors, golf course superintendents, landscape architects, sod producers, sports field managers, and general contractors working on erosion control projects. These professionals rely on W.S. Connelly as a specialized one-stop distributor that offers technical expertise alongside the consumable inputs required for large-scale outdoor turf and vegetation management.

investments.company: Zinc Buyer Corporation and Marmic Fire & Safety Co., Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Marmic Fire & Safety is a national full-service fire protection company with over 70 years of experience, 1,300+ professionals, and 50,000+ client relationships. The company provides NICET-certified installation, inspection, testing, maintenance, repair, design, and 24/7 emergency service for all categories of fire and life safety systems including sprinklers, fire alarms, suppression systems, and extinguishers. Zinc Buyer Corporation is the acquisition vehicle through which Gridiron Capital invested in Marmic; the company was subsequently acquired by KKR in 2024.
investments.end_customers: Clients span all industries requiring fire code compliance across commercial, industrial, healthcare, hospitality, gaming, and government facilities. Property managers, facility directors, and building owners engage Marmic on long-term inspection and maintenance contracts to satisfy local fire authority mandates and insurance requirements.

investments.company: Apex Service Partners, LLC and Apex Service Partners Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Apex Service Partners is a national platform of HVAC, plumbing, and electrical service companies founded in 2019 by Alpine Investors and headquartered in Dallas and Tampa. The company has grown to over 8,000 technicians organized into a coast-to-coast network of locally branded partner businesses, each retaining its own identity, culture, and leadership. Apex provides shared back-office resources, technology, and capital to its partner companies while preserving local operational autonomy.
investments.end_customers: Apex's end customers are residential homeowners who require installation, maintenance, and repair of HVAC systems, water heaters, plumbing, and electrical systems. Each partner company operates as a trusted local brand, so homeowners often choose them based on years of established community relationships and positive word-of-mouth referrals.

investments.company: Birdie Bidco, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Golf club owner and operator
investments.business_description: Birdie Bidco is the acquisition vehicle through which Centre Partners backed Birdies, a direct-to-consumer women's footwear brand co-founded in San Francisco in 2015. Birdies designs and sells stylish, comfort-focused flats, heels, and casual shoes direct-to-consumer online and through select retail partnerships; the brand achieved B Corporation certification in 2022. The company was subsequently acquired by KNS International (also Centre Partners-backed) to join a portfolio of premium DTC footwear brands.
investments.end_customers: Birdies' customers are style-conscious professional women seeking footwear that blends office-appropriate aesthetics with all-day comfort, particularly for commuting and workday use. The brand cultivates a loyal direct relationship with customers through its digital-first model, email marketing, and social media engagement.

investments.business_description: Clarion Home Services Group is a multi-brand residential HVAC, plumbing, and electrical services platform backed by LBC Small Cap and LightBay Capital, headquartered in Lake Forest, Illinois. investments.company: ClubCorp Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: A manager of golf and country clubs throughout the United States
investments.business_description: ClubCorp (now operating as Invited) is the largest owner-operator of private golf and country clubs, business clubs, sports clubs, and alumni clubs in the United States, founded in Dallas in 1957. The company owns or operates more than 200 clubs across 28 states and two foreign countries, serving over 430,000 members, and was taken private by Apollo Global Management in 2017 in a $2.2 billion transaction. Properties include iconic facilities such as Firestone Country Club (Akron, OH) and Mission Hills Country Club (Rancho Mirage, CA).
investments.end_customers: Members are golf enthusiasts, business professionals, families, and social club members who pay annual dues in exchange for access to golf courses, fitness facilities, dining, networking events, and reciprocal club privileges across the national network. Corporate memberships are also common, with employers providing club access as a perk for client entertainment and executive networking.

investments.company: CST Holding Company
investments.investment_type: Provider of ignition interlock devices
investments.business_description: CST Holding Company is the holding entity for The Service Companies, the nation's premier provider of managed housekeeping, public area cleaning, food and beverage staffing, and specialty cleaning services for the hospitality and gaming industries. The Service Companies serves over 750 hotels, casinos, resorts, arenas, stadiums, hospitals, and universities across 33 states, Puerto Rico, and the Bahamas under outsourced management contracts. Services include turnkey housekeeping, kitchen and steward cleaning, laundry, chandelier and window cleaning, and trained F&B staffing.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are hotel operators, casino resort owners, arena and stadium managers, university facilities teams, and corporate cafeteria operators who outsource non-core hospitality labor to The Service Companies to improve quality, reduce HR complexity, and better manage variable labor costs during seasonal and event-driven demand swings.

investments.company: Davidson Hotel Company LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of hotel operations solutions and advisory services
investments.business_description: Davidson Hotel Company is the operating entity within Davidson Hospitality Group, an award-winning full-service hotel and resort management company that manages 86+ hotels, 200+ restaurants and bars, and over 2 million square feet of meeting space across the United States. Davidson generates more than $2 billion in annual revenue under management and ranks among the top third-party managers by revenue per room. The company was sold by KSL Capital Partners to Nautic Partners in November 2024.
investments.end_customers: Davidson's direct clients are hotel owners and institutional real estate investors who engage the company under third-party management contracts to operate properties across upper-upscale and luxury segments. Hotel guests—primarily business and leisure travelers—are the indirect end customers whose experience directly affects owner revenues and Davidson's contract retention.

investments.company: Equinox Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Operator of luxury, full-service health fitness clubs
investments.business_description: Equinox Holdings is the parent company of the Equinox luxury fitness club brand, which operates 300+ high-end gym locations globally in major U.S. cities, London, Toronto, and Vancouver, as well as subsidiaries including Equinox Hotels, SoulCycle, and Blink Fitness. Equinox clubs offer a premium fitness experience at price points significantly above mass-market competitors, featuring state-of-the-art equipment, signature group fitness classes, spa services, and personalized coaching. The company is backed by L Catterton, Silver Lake, and related entities.
investments.end_customers: Members are affluent urban professionals and fitness enthusiasts who pay premium monthly dues (typically $200-$300+/month) for access to Equinox's facilities, group classes, and amenities. The brand targets high-income consumers who view fitness as a lifestyle priority and are willing to pay for an elevated, experience-driven club environment in prime metropolitan locations.

investments.company: Essential Services Holding Corporation and OMERS Mahomes Investment Holdings LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of plumbing and HVAC services
investments.business_description: The operating company is TurnPoint Services Group, a residential HVAC, plumbing, and electrical services platform backed by OMERS Private Equity, headquartered in Louisville, Kentucky. investments.company: Excel Fitness Consolidator LLC, Health Buyer LLC and Excel Fitness Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Fitness facility operator
investments.business_description: Excel Fitness is one of the largest Planet Fitness franchisees in the United States, operating 160+ Planet Fitness gym locations across Texas, Oklahoma, Tennessee, Georgia, Arkansas, Missouri, North Carolina, Utah, and Virginia. The company was backed by Altamont Capital Partners before being acquired by Olympus Partners in 2022. Excel Fitness Consolidator LLC and Health Buyer LLC are the acquisition and holding vehicles layered above the operating franchisee entity.
investments.end_customers: End customers are price-sensitive fitness consumers who join Planet Fitness locations for low-cost, no-intimidation gym access, typically paying $10-$25 per month. Excel Fitness's clubs predominantly serve working-class and middle-income members in suburban and secondary markets who prioritize value and accessibility over premium amenities.

investments.company: Flinn Scientific, Inc. and WCI-Quantum Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Distributor of instructional products, services and resources
investments.business_description: Flinn Scientific is the leading specialty supplier of science education laboratory equipment, chemicals, and consumable supplies for K-12 schools and some university applications, headquartered in Batavia, Illinois with nearly 40 years of operation. The company sells a comprehensive catalog of chemistry, biology, physics, earth science, and STEM supplies including lab apparatus, safety equipment, lab kits, and specialty chemicals directly to educational institutions. WCI-Quantum Holdings is the Windjammer Capital-sponsored holding entity above the Flinn operating company.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are K-12 science teachers, school district procurement offices, and university laboratory coordinators who purchase consumables, replacement lab kits, and safety supplies on an annual recurring basis. Flinn's deep expertise in chemical handling regulations and its commitment to school safety (providing regulatory compliance guidance at no cost) make it the trusted preferred vendor for science departments across North American schools.

investments.company: Flint OpCo, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of residential HVAC and plumbing services
investments.business_description: Flint OpCo LLC is the operating company of Flint Group, a residential HVAC, plumbing, and electrical services platform backed by General Atlantic (lead) and Kinderhook Partners, founded in 2019 and headquartered in Seattle with additional markets in Portland, Houston, Boston, and Denver. The company operates through seven longstanding regional brands that collectively offer installation, replacement, maintenance, and repair services to residential customers. Flint provides partner brands with shared resources, technology, and growth capital while maintaining local brand identity and leadership.
investments.end_customers: End customers are homeowners in Flint's target metropolitan markets who need HVAC, plumbing, and electrical services and choose local, trusted brands with multi-decade community histories. Customers often engage on recurring maintenance agreements (annual tune-ups) and call for emergency repairs, providing Flint's brands with a mix of predictable subscription-like revenue and project-based revenue.

investments.company: GS SEER Group Borrower LLC and GS SEER Group Holdings LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of commercial and residential HVAC, electrical, and plumbing services
investments.business_description: The operating company is The SEER Group, a residential and commercial HVAC, plumbing, and electrical services platform backed by Genstar Capital, founded in 2018 and headquartered in Lynnwood, Washington with operations across the Pacific Northwest and Western U.S. SEER deploys 700+ trained technicians and operates as a platform acquirer of local home services businesses, providing sellers with capital, operational support, and a growth platform while they retain brand and local leadership. GS SEER Group Borrower LLC is the debt-holding entity associated with the Genstar Capital investment.
investments.end_customers: End customers are residential homeowners and small commercial property owners in SEER's Pacific Northwest and Western U.S. footprint who need HVAC installation, maintenance, and repair, as well as plumbing and electrical services. investments.company: Helios Service Partners, LLC and Astra Service Partners, LLC
investments.investment_type: Critical HVAC, refrigeration, and plumbing services for commercial businesses
investments.business_description: Helios Service Partners is the leading provider of multi-site commercial HVAC-R (heating, ventilation, air conditioning, and refrigeration) national accounts services, operating in 42 states with 100% self-performing technicians. The company is part of Orion Services Group, a commercial and industrial field services platform that has built Helios into a national HVAC-R services business by consolidating top independent national accounts operators. Services include planned preventive maintenance, emergency repairs, and system installations for commercial HVAC-R equipment.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are multi-location commercial operators such as restaurant chains, grocery retailers, big-box retailers, convenience store chains, and property management companies that need a single national vendor to maintain HVAC and refrigeration equipment across hundreds or thousands of locations under a standardized service level agreement. These customers value consistent quality and simplified vendor management over using many regional providers.

investments.company: Infinity Home Services Holdco, Inc., D'Angelo & Sons Construction Limited and IHS Parent Holdings, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Provider of residential roofing and exterior repair and replacement services
investments.business_description: Infinity Home Services (IHS) is a national multi-brand platform providing residential roofing replacement, window, and siding services, backed by LightBay Capital and Freeman Spogli & Co. following an initial investment in December 2022. Since inception, IHS has grown to encompass 17+ regional brands across North America, expanding into Canada through the acquisition of D'Angelo & Sons Roofing & Exteriors, a 40-year-old Hamilton, Ontario contractor specializing in roofing, siding, windows, and exterior home renovations. IHS provides homeowners with end-to-end exterior renovation and repair services.
investments.end_customers: End customers are residential homeowners seeking roofing replacement and repair, window installation, and siding services, often following storm damage, material aging, or as part of planned home improvement projects. Insurance carriers are also important customers in the roofing segment, as IHS partner brands are engaged to perform claims-covered repairs after weather events such as hail and wind damage.

investments.company: LHS Borrower, LLC, LH Equity Investors, L.P., Leaf Home, LLC and GC Fund IV Blocker LLC
investments.investment_type: A direct-to-consumer home solutions and residential improvements platform
investments.business_description: Leaf Home (operating as LHS Borrower at the debt level) is the largest direct-to-consumer residential home improvement company in North America, backed by Gridiron Capital and Kaulig Capital, with a $1.6 billion credit facility. The company offers homeowners a growing portfolio of proprietary home products including LeafFilter gutter protection, Leaf Home Safety Solutions (stairlifts), Leaf Home Water Solutions (water filtration), and Leaf Home Enhancements (bath remodeling), sold and installed through a nationwide in-home sales force. Leaf expanded further by acquiring Erie Home to broaden its roofing and exterior services capabilities.
investments.end_customers: End customers are homeowners—predominantly older homeowners—who purchase Leaf Home's products through in-home sales consultations. LeafFilter targets homeowners frustrated with gutter cleaning; safety solutions target seniors seeking aging-in-place modifications; water filtration targets health-conscious homeowners. Customers pay a premium for turnkey installation and a manufacturer's lifetime warranty.

The company offers homeowners a growing portfolio of proprietary home products including LeafFilter gutter protection, Leaf Home Safety Solutions (stairlifts), Leaf Home Water Solutions (water filtration), and Leaf Home Enhancements (bath remodeling), sold and installed through a nationwide in-home sales force. Leaf expanded further by acquiring Erie Home to broaden its roofing and exterior services capabilities.
investments.end_customers: End customers are homeowners—predominantly older homeowners—who purchase Leaf Home's products through in-home sales consultations. LeafFilter targets homeowners frustrated with gutter cleaning; safety solutions target seniors seeking aging-in-place modifications; water filtration targets health-conscious homeowners. Customers pay a premium for turnkey installation and a manufacturer's lifetime warranty.

investments.company: Modigent, LLC and OMERS PMC Investment Holdings LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of commercial HVAC services
investments.business_description: Modigent is a national commercial HVAC, plumbing, and building controls systems platform backed by OMERS Private Equity, founded in 2001 and headquartered in Phoenix, Arizona. Through 20+ strategic acquisitions, Modigent has assembled 13 specialized brands operating across 17 key U.S. markets, providing next-generation mechanical services, energy solutions, building automation, and controls systems for commercial and industrial customers. The company has helped over 3,000 customers achieve significant HVAC efficiency improvements.
investments.end_customers: Modigent's customers are commercial and industrial building owners, property managers, healthcare facilities, data centers, and large corporate campuses that require sophisticated mechanical systems installation, preventive maintenance, and energy optimization. Clients engage Modigent for multi-year service contracts and capital project work aimed at reducing energy consumption and ensuring HVAC system reliability.

investments.company: Mustang Prospects Purchaser, LLC, Senske Acquisition, Inc., and Mustang Prospects Holdco, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of lawn care, tree care and pest control services
investments.business_description: This corporate structure represents a platform acquisition vehicle focused on creating a national leader in residential lawn care and pest control services through acquisitions and organic growth; the primary operating brand is Senske Services, backed by GTCR. Senske is a market-leading multi-state provider of lawn fertilization, weed control, pest control, and tree services headquartered in the Western United States, having completed 24+ acquisitions. Mustang Prospects Holdco and Purchaser LLC are the acquisition holdco entities used in the broader capitalization structure.
investments.end_customers: Customers are residential homeowners who subscribe to recurring lawn treatment programs covering fertilization, weed control, pest control, and insect prevention. Senske's business model relies on route-based technicians delivering seasonal treatment schedules, generating predictable subscription revenue from homeowners who want a professionally maintained lawn without handling chemicals themselves.

investments.company: North Haven Fairway Buyer, LLC and Fairway Lawns, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of lawncare services
investments.business_description: Fairway Lawns is a market-leading provider of residential lawn care services headquartered in Little Rock, Arkansas, backed by Morgan Stanley Capital Partners (MSCP Fund VII). The company operates 16 branches across the Southeast, offering recurring lawncare services such as weed control and fertilization, complemented by pest control, tree and shrub maintenance, and other add-on services. North Haven Fairway Buyer, LLC is the acquisition vehicle used by MSCP to acquire Fairway from Prairie Capital.
investments.end_customers: Customers are residential homeowners in the Southeast who subscribe to Fairway Lawns' recurring lawn treatment programs, with the majority on annual contracts covering multiple applications throughout the growing season. Homeowners value the consistent results and hands-off convenience of professional lawn care, particularly in the Southeast's warm climate where weeds and pests require year-round management.

investments.company: Northwinds Holding, Inc. and Northwinds Services Group LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of HVAC and plumbing services
investments.business_description: Northwinds Services Group is a TruArc Partners-backed HVAC and plumbing services platform focused on the Northeast, Mid-Atlantic, and Midwest, anchored by Isaac Heating & Air Conditioning (one of the nation's largest HVAC companies with 75+ years of operation) and a growing network of regional brands including Arctic Bear, Nertherlings, Santa Fe Heating & Air, Thermal Heating, and Wade Heating & Cooling. The platform emphasizes high-quality technician training and exceptional technical expertise for both residential and commercial customers. Northwinds Holding, Inc. is the PE holding entity.
investments.end_customers: Customers include residential homeowners seeking HVAC system installation, maintenance, and emergency repair services from trusted regional brands with deep local histories, as well as small and mid-sized commercial building operators in the Northeast and Midwest who require reliable HVAC and plumbing service contracts. Long-standing brand names such as Isaac Heating generate strong customer loyalty and recurring maintenance agreement revenue.

investments.company: OTG Concessions Management, LLC and Octa Parent Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Airport restaurant operator
investments.business_description: OTG (also known as On The Go) is a premier airport food, beverage, and retail concessions operator founded in Philadelphia in 1996, managing 350+ locations across 22 terminals in 11 of the world's busiest travel hubs. The company is known for transforming the airport dining experience by combining farm-to-terminal cuisine, award-winning restaurants from acclaimed chefs, extensive technology integration (iPad ordering at gates), and premium retail in redesigned terminal spaces. OTG is now controlled by a lender consortium led by Oaktree Capital Management and Centerbridge Partners following a 2024 restructuring.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are domestic and international airline passengers passing through OTG-operated terminals who purchase food, beverages, and retail goods during their pre-flight waiting time. Airport authorities and airlines are the institutional clients who award OTG its concession contracts based on revenue share terms, concept quality, and the operator's ability to enhance the overall passenger experience.

investments.company: PestCo Holdings, LLC and PestCo, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of pest control services to the residential and commercial markets
investments.business_description: PestCo Holdings is a Thompson Street Capital Partners-backed pest control roll-up platform aggregating residential and commercial pest prevention and control services companies across the United States. The company completed 21 acquisitions through 2025 including Innovative Pest Control, Bio-Tech Pest Control, Arizona's Best Choice Pest & Termite Services, and Southwest Exterminating, making it one of the most active acquirers in the fragmented pest control sector. PestCo is led by a management team with deep pest control industry and PE operator backgrounds.
investments.end_customers: End customers are residential homeowners and commercial property operators (restaurants, food processing, hotels, healthcare facilities, schools) who engage PestCo's regional brands on annual service contracts to control insects, rodents, termites, and other pests. Residential customers are motivated by health, comfort, and property protection concerns, while commercial customers require pest control to meet health code compliance requirements.

investments.company: Pinnacle MEP Intermediate Holdco LLC and BPCP Pinnacle Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of commercial and residential HVAC, electrical & plumbing services
investments.business_description: Pinnacle MEP is a Midwest-based mechanical contracting services platform providing HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and piping services to commercial, industrial, and residential customers across Indiana, Michigan, Ohio, and surrounding regions. The company handles projects ranging from routine preventive maintenance and equipment servicing to full-scale facility retrofits and new construction MEP installations. Blue Point Capital Partners recapitalized Pinnacle MEP Holdings in October 2024; BPCP Pinnacle Holdings reflects Blue Point's holding entity, not Bain Capital.
investments.end_customers: Customers are commercial building owners, industrial facility operators, healthcare institutions, and large corporate campuses in the Midwest that need mechanical systems installation, service, and maintenance. Clients engage Pinnacle for both ongoing maintenance contracts covering HVAC and plumbing systems and for capital project work including equipment replacement and facility infrastructure upgrades.

investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Premiere Buyer, LLC is an Ares Management-backed acquisition vehicle in the consumer services sector; the specific underlying operating company has not been separately identified in publicly available ARCC portfolio disclosures. The 'Buyer' suffix is standard PE acquisition vehicle nomenclature used in leveraged buyout structures. Based on ARCC's consumer services portfolio concentration in home services, staffing, and franchised consumer businesses, this entity likely represents a middle-market consumer services operating company.
investments.end_customers: Specific end customer information is not determinable from publicly available sources given the acquisition vehicle naming convention and the absence of a confirmed operating company identity in public databases or press coverage.

investments.company: Quick Quack Car Wash Holdings, LLC and KKR Game Changer Co-Invest Feeder II L.P.
investments.investment_type: Car wash operator
investments.business_description: Quick Quack Car Wash is one of the largest and fastest-growing express exterior car wash chains in the United States, ranked among the top five nationally with approximately 340 locations across California, Texas, Arizona, Utah, and Colorado as of 2025. The company operates a membership-based unlimited wash subscription model under the Quick Quack brand. KKR made a significant minority investment of approximately $850 million in 2022 to accelerate organic expansion and innovation.
investments.end_customers: End customers are individual vehicle owners seeking fast, affordable exterior car wash services, typically in suburban and urban markets. The subscription model targets frequent users who want a low-cost monthly plan for unlimited washes, making the service particularly attractive to commuters and families in the Western and Southern U.S.

investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)

investments.company: Radiant Intermediate Holding, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of HVAC, plumbing and electrical services
investments.business_description: The underlying operating company is Radiant Plumbing and Air Conditioning (trading as Radiant Plumbing, Air Conditioning & Electrical), an Austin, Texas-based residential home services provider founded in 1999. The company provides plumbing, HVAC installation and repair, sewer services, and electrical work to residential customers in the greater Austin metro area, with a customer base of approximately 12,000 annually. The Riverside Company invested in Radiant as a home services platform, though ARCC's holding entity (Radiant Intermediate) suggests subsequent financing activity.
investments.end_customers: End customers are homeowners in the Austin, Texas metro area who require maintenance, repair, or replacement of plumbing, HVAC, sewer, and electrical systems. The company serves both scheduled maintenance clients and emergency repair customers across the residential market.

investments.company: Redwood Services LP
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Redwood Services is a Memphis, Tennessee-based PE-backed partnership platform that acquires and supports essential residential home services businesses across the U.S. Founded in 2020 by Union Main Group and backed by Altas Partners (in a ~$1.1 billion deal in 2025), Redwood has invested in 19 partner companies spanning HVAC, plumbing, and electrical services with a strict focus on 100% residential, non-new-construction work. The platform generates over $500 million in annual revenue and supports more than 2,500 employees across markets including Tennessee, Arizona, Virginia, California, Indiana, Ohio, and North Carolina.
investments.end_customers: End customers are residential homeowners who need repair, replacement, or maintenance of HVAC systems, plumbing, and electrical infrastructure. Redwood's partner companies serve over 40,000 homeowners in markets such as Memphis and Huntsville, targeting customers who require reliable local tradespeople for essential home systems.

investments.company: Safe Home Security, Inc., Security Systems Inc., Safe Home Monitoring, Inc., National Protective Services, Inc., Bright Integrations LLC and Medguard Alert, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of safety systems for business and residential customers
investments.business_description: Safe Home Security is a Middletown, Connecticut-based home security company founded in 1987 that sells, installs, services, and monitors residential and commercial security, fire alarm, CCTV, and home automation systems. The consolidated entity encompasses multiple related brands including security monitoring (Safe Home Monitoring), integration services (Bright Integrations), and medical alert systems (Medguard Alert). The company protects over 200,000 homes and generates approximately $66 million in annual revenue.
investments.end_customers: End customers are residential homeowners and small commercial businesses in the Northeastern U.S. who seek professionally monitored security systems for protection against burglary, fire, and medical emergencies. The Medguard Alert brand targets elderly and at-risk individuals who need personal emergency response monitoring.

investments.company: Taymax Group, L.P., Taymax Group G.P., LLC, PF Salem Canada ULC and TCP Fit Parent, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Planet Fitness franchisee
investments.business_description: Taymax Group is one of the largest Planet Fitness franchisees in North America, operating more than 150 Planet Fitness club locations across the United States and Canada. Founded in 2009 and backed by Trilantic North America (not TCP Capital as initially noted in source data), Taymax has been recognized as Planet Fitness Developer of the Year and Franchisee of the Year. The entity structure includes Canadian operations (PF Salem Canada ULC) alongside the U.S. limited partnership holding structure.
investments.end_customers: End customers are budget-conscious fitness seekers who join Planet Fitness clubs for low monthly membership fees, typically ranging from $10 to $25 per month. Planet Fitness's positioning as a 'Judgement Free Zone' attracts first-time gym-goers, casual exercisers, and price-sensitive consumers who would otherwise not belong to traditional gyms.

investments.company: The Arcticom Group, LLC and AMCP Mechanical Holdings, LP
investments.investment_type: Refrigeration, heating, ventilation and air conditioning services provider
investments.business_description: The Arcticom Group is a commercial refrigeration and HVAC services platform backed by A&M Capital Partners (Alvarez & Marsal Capital). The company provides a wide range of commercial and industrial refrigeration and HVAC services including preventative maintenance, repair, installation, renovation, new systems engineering, energy optimization, and refrigerant management across the U.S. The company employs over 900 full-time workers including more than 600 certified technicians, and has expanded to the East Coast through acquisitions such as Tomco Mechanical Corporation.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are grocery chains, supermarkets, food distributors, cold storage operators, commercial real estate landlords, and industrial manufacturers who rely on mission-critical refrigeration and HVAC systems for food safety, regulatory compliance, and operational continuity. Retail food chains represent a core vertical given the life-safety criticality of commercial refrigeration.

The company provides a wide range of commercial and industrial refrigeration and HVAC services including preventative maintenance, repair, installation, renovation, new systems engineering, energy optimization, and refrigerant management across the U.S. The company employs over 900 full-time workers including more than 600 certified technicians, and has expanded to the East Coast through acquisitions such as Tomco Mechanical Corporation.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are grocery chains, supermarkets, food distributors, cold storage operators, commercial real estate landlords, and industrial manufacturers who rely on mission-critical refrigeration and HVAC systems for food safety, regulatory compliance, and operational continuity. Retail food chains represent a core vertical given the life-safety criticality of commercial refrigeration.

investments.company: Triwizard Holdings, Inc. and Triwizard Parent, LP
investments.investment_type: Parking management and hospitality services provider
investments.business_description: Triwizard Holdings is the holding company structure for Greenbriar Equity Group's investment in Oil Changers, a leading West Coast express oil change chain. Oil Changers was founded in 1984 and provides fast oil change and related automotive maintenance services (oil changes, windshield wiper and air filter replacements) through approximately 56 service centers in California and Hawaii, serving over 750,000 customers annually. Greenbriar acquired Oil Changers from Trivest Partners in March 2021.
investments.end_customers: End customers are individual vehicle owners in California and Hawaii seeking fast, no-appointment-needed oil change and basic maintenance services. The customer base skews toward everyday commuters who value speed and convenience over comprehensive dealership service, similar to the Jiffy Lube and Valvoline Instant Oil Change model.

investments.company: TSWT Acquisition, Inc. and TSWT Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: TSWT Acquisition, Inc. is the acquisition vehicle for Tri-State Water, Power & Air, a Midwest-based residential home services company founded in 1992 and headquartered in Cape Girardeau, Missouri. Tri-State markets, installs, and services residential water treatment systems, water heaters, whole-home standby generators, and air purification products across an eight-state region in the central Midwest. NorthCurrent Partners completed the acquisition of the platform in January 2021; ARCC's holding entity TSWT reflects subsequent debt financing.
investments.end_customers: End customers are homeowners in the Midwest who purchase or finance water softeners, reverse osmosis systems, tankless water heaters, and backup generators as home improvement investments. The company's model relies heavily on in-home sales consultations and recurring service agreements with residential customers across Missouri, Illinois, Indiana, and surrounding states.

investments.company: Vertex Service Partners, LLC and Vertex Service Partners Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of residential roofing repair & replacement
investments.business_description: Vertex Service Partners is a residential home improvement services platform launched by Alpine Investors in 2023, focused on roofing and exterior home services. The platform acquires well-established regional roofing, siding, and exterior services companies and provides them with centralized back-office support, marketing, and operational resources while allowing acquired companies to operate under their own brand names. Since its 2023 launch, Vertex has grown to over $600 million in revenue servicing more than 100,000 roofs annually across the U.S.
investments.end_customers: End customers are residential homeowners who need roof replacement, siding, gutters, and other exterior home improvement services, often triggered by storm damage, aging systems, or insurance claims. Vertex's local partner brands serve homeowners across multiple U.S. markets, with a customer base that includes both insurance claim-driven replacements and elective home renovations.

investments.company: YE Brands Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Sports camp operator
investments.business_description: YE Brands Holdings is the holding company for Youth Enrichment Brands (YEB), a Roark Capital-backed national youth activities platform. YEB is the nation's leading youth activities company operating through four brands: US Sports Camps (provider of Nike Sports Camps), i9 Sports (recreational youth sports leagues), Streamline Brands (SafeSplash swim lessons), and School of Rock (music education franchise). The platform serves more than 950,000 children annually across approximately 1,000 locations in 50 states and 20 countries.
investments.end_customers: End customers are parents of children ages 2-18 who enroll their kids in sports leagues, swim lessons, music programs, and summer camps through YEB's franchise and corporate-operated brands. The customer proposition is enriching, structured activities that build skills and confidence in a fun environment, appealing to suburban families seeking structured after-school and summer programming.

investments.company: 15484880 Canada Inc. and 15484910 Canada Inc.
investments.investment_type: Independent insurance broker
investments.business_description: These are Canadian numbered companies (numbered-company BidCo/HoldCo vehicles) that represent the holding structure for Synex Business Performance, one of Canada's largest independent insurance brokerage groups. Synex, founded in Quebec in 2020, has grown to more than 20 brokerage firms specializing in commercial and personal property and casualty insurance, group insurance, and financial services, generating over $1 billion in annual premium volume with a majority coming from Quebec. CDPQ and Ares Management Credit funds made significant minority investments in Synex to support its national expansion strategy.
investments.end_customers: End customers are small and mid-sized businesses, commercial real estate operators, and individual consumers across Canada who purchase commercial P&C, personal lines, and group insurance coverage through Synex's network of local brokerages. The platform's Quebec concentration means a significant portion of clients are French-speaking Canadian businesses and individuals seeking bilingual brokerage services.

investments.company: Acrisure, LLC
investments.investment_type: Independent property and casualty insurance brokerage
investments.business_description: Acrisure is one of the world's largest insurance brokerages and fintech companies, headquartered in Grand Rapids, Michigan, and founded in 2005. The company generated $4.8 billion in revenue in 2024 and has grown into a top-10 global insurance broker through an aggressive acquisition strategy, now serving clients across property and casualty insurance, employee benefits, and financial services. Valued at approximately $32 billion, Acrisure is backed by BDT & MSD Partners as its largest minority shareholder, with ABRY Partners as an earlier investor, and has attracted capital from Bain Capital, Apollo, and Fidelity among others.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are small and medium-sized businesses that purchase property and casualty insurance and employee benefits through Acrisure's network of local agency partners. The company's distribution model aggregates hundreds of independent insurance agencies under a common platform, serving commercial clients ranging from Main Street businesses to middle-market companies across the U.S. and globally.

investments.company: AQ Sunshine, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Specialized insurance broker
investments.business_description: AQ Sunshine, Inc. is the acquisition vehicle for Aquiline Capital Partners' investment in Relation Insurance Services, one of the largest independent insurance brokers in the United States. Relation provides comprehensive risk management and benefits consulting across commercial property and casualty, employee benefits, personal lines, private client services, retirement solutions, and wealth management through more than 90 offices nationwide. Aquiline acquired Relation in 2019 and grew it through 100+ acquisitions before announcing its sale to BayPine in early 2026.
investments.end_customers: End customers are businesses of all sizes and individual high-net-worth clients who purchase commercial insurance, employee benefits packages, and personal insurance through Relation's local offices. The company serves diverse industries with customized risk management solutions, particularly in niche verticals where it has built specialty capabilities.

investments.company: Bellwether Buyer, L.L.C. and Bellwether Topco V Buyer, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Insurance program administrator
investments.business_description: Bellwether Buyer is the acquisition holding entity for Alkeme Insurance (formerly PCF Insurance Services), a fast-growing national independent insurance brokerage platform backed by Ares Management. Alkeme is headquartered in Ladera Ranch, California, has completed over 70 agency acquisitions since 2020, and operates from 60+ locations across 29 states, generating approximately $992 million in total capital raised. The platform ranks among the top 25 largest insurance agencies in the U.S. and the #3 fastest-growing broker on Business Insurance's Top 100 list.
investments.end_customers: End customers are businesses and individuals seeking commercial property and casualty insurance, employee benefits, surety, risk management, and financial services through local independent agents affiliated with the Alkeme network. The platform's decentralized model preserves local agency relationships while providing back-office scale, targeting small and mid-market commercial clients and personal lines customers across the U.S.

investments.company: Benecon Midco II LLC and Benecon Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Employee benefits provider for small and mid-size employers
investments.business_description: The operating company is The Benecon Group, a Lititz, Pennsylvania-based company founded in 1991 that develops and administers self-funded employee health benefits programs for small and medium-sized employers (typically fewer than 500 employees). Benecon operates insurance 'consortiums' that aggregate employers' stop-loss purchasing power, designs self-funded health plans, and provides actuarial, compliance, finance, and administrative services through its divisions. TA Associates made a significant growth investment in Benecon, and a subsidiary — ConnectCare3 — provides wellness consulting, nurse navigation, and chronic disease management services to members.
investments.end_customers: End customers are small and medium-sized U.S. businesses (typically 25-500 employees) seeking an alternative to fully insured health plans, looking to reduce cost volatility and increase control over their employee health benefits. The self-funded model appeals particularly to employers in industries such as municipal governments, schools, manufacturing, and professional services that have predictable employee populations.

investments.company: Captive Resources Midco, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of independent consulting services to member-owned group captives
investments.business_description: The operating company is Captive Resources, LLC, the industry-leading advisor to member-owned group captive insurance programs, founded approximately 40 years ago and now backed by KKR. Captive Resources advises more than 50 group captive insurance programs across the U.S., comprising over 7,500 member companies and managing more than $5.5 billion in annual premium. The company provides end-to-end captive support including claims advocacy, operational oversight, risk management, financial services, regulatory compliance, and investment management through its sister company, Kensington Management Group in the Cayman Islands.
investments.end_customers: End customers are mid-market U.S. companies (typically in industries such as construction, manufacturing, trucking, food processing, and healthcare) that become owner-members of Captive Resources' group captive programs as an alternative to traditional commercial insurance markets. Member-owners benefit from profit-sharing on favorable loss experience and greater control over their risk management programs.

investments.company: Daylight Beta Parent LLC and CFCo, LLC
investments.investment_type: Health insurance sales platform provider
investments.business_description: Daylight Beta Parent LLC and related entities are the holding structure for Madison Dearborn Partners' acquisition of Benefytt Technologies, a health insurance technology and distribution company. Benefytt operates a private e-commerce marketplace for health insurance products, offering Medicare-related plans, supplemental health insurance, and other health and ancillary products through its digital platform and agency technology. MDP completed the acquisition via a tender offer at $31.00 per share in August 2020 and subsequently took the company private to restructure and reposition it.
investments.end_customers: End customers are individuals — particularly self-employed people, gig workers, and those between employer-sponsored coverage — who shop for non-traditional and supplemental health insurance products online. The platform serves consumers seeking lower-cost alternatives to ACA-compliant plans, including short-term medical, dental, vision, and Medicare supplement products.

investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)

investments.company: Diamond Mezzanine 24 LLC
investments.investment_type: Property and casualty insurance underwriting and distribution platform
investments.business_description: Diamond Mezzanine 24 LLC is an Ares Management fund-level financing vehicle used as part of mezzanine or subordinated debt structures in the insurance brokerage sector. While the specific underlying portfolio company is not publicly disclosed, the entity name follows the convention Ares uses for its mezzanine co-investment and financing structures. Based on the ARCC Insurance industry categorization, this vehicle likely provides subordinated debt or preferred equity financing to an insurance distribution or services company in Ares' broader insurance portfolio.
investments.end_customers: As a financing vehicle rather than an operating company, Diamond Mezzanine 24 LLC does not directly serve end customers. Its economic value derives from the interest and fee income generated by the mezzanine debt or preferred equity it holds in an underlying insurance company that serves commercial and individual insurance buyers.

investments.company: DOXA Insurance Holdings LLC and Rocket Co-Invest, SLP
investments.investment_type: Managing general agent insurance distribution platform
investments.business_description: DOXA Insurance Holdings is a Fort Wayne, Indiana-based specialty insurance distribution company founded in 2016 that acquires and operates managing general agencies (MGAs), wholesale brokers, and program administrators focused on niche commercial property and casualty markets. DOXA's subsidiaries provide professional liability, marine, real estate, alternative risk, franchisor, allied healthcare, skilled nursing, assisted living, and managed care specialty insurance. In November 2023, Goldman Sachs Asset Management announced a definitive agreement to acquire DOXA.
investments.end_customers: End customers are businesses operating in niche commercial sectors — including healthcare providers, skilled nursing facilities, franchisors, marine operators, and real estate professionals — who purchase specialty insurance products through DOXA's MGA subsidiaries and wholesale broker channels. These customers require specialized underwriting expertise that standard commercial lines markets cannot efficiently provide.

investments.company: Forza Insurance Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Operator of insurance brands and platforms
investments.business_description: Forza Insurance Holdings is a Houston, Texas-based insurance holding company founded in 2021 that owns and operates specialty homeowners insurance carriers focused on catastrophe-exposed coastal property markets. Forza's portfolio includes SureChoice Underwriters Reciprocal Exchange and Elevate Reciprocal Exchange, both focused on Gulf Coast and Southeastern coastal homeowners, and the company acquired Interboro Insurance Company from American Coastal Insurance for $26.4 million in April 2025. Flexpoint Ford provided growth equity financing alongside Ares Management (and the SageSure connection in filings appears related to Forza's reinsurance and carrier relationships in the same coastal specialty market).
investments.end_customers: End customers are homeowners in coastal Gulf and Southeastern states (including Florida, Texas, Louisiana, and neighboring markets) who need property insurance in markets where standard carriers have reduced capacity due to hurricane and catastrophe exposure. Forza provides admitted and non-admitted insurance capacity to homeowners who cannot obtain coverage through mainstream carriers.

investments.company: Foundation Risk Partners, Corp.
investments.investment_type: Full service independent insurance agency
investments.business_description: Foundation Risk Partners (FRP) is a high-growth national insurance brokerage platform headquartered in Daytona Beach, Florida, founded in 2017 by Warburg Pincus and a team of senior insurance executives. FRP has grown to generate close to $500 million in annual revenue with 139 offices across 18 states, concentrating in Florida, New Jersey, New York, and California, and providing commercial insurance, employee benefits, personal insurance, and risk management services. Partners Group acquired a controlling stake from Warburg Pincus in 2022 in a transaction that valued FRP as one of the fastest-growing mid-market brokers in the U.S.
investments.end_customers: End customers are small and mid-market businesses, nonprofit organizations, and high-net-worth individuals who purchase commercial property and casualty insurance, employee benefits, and personal insurance through FRP's local offices. FRP's geographic focus in Florida and the Northeast means a significant portion of clients are in real estate, hospitality, construction, and professional services industries.

investments.company: Galway Borrower LLC
investments.investment_type: Insurance service provider
investments.business_description: Galway Borrower LLC is the debt financing entity for Galway Insurance Holdings, a diversified insurance distribution platform backed by Harvest Partners (with co-investors Oak Hill Capital and The Carlyle Group). investments.company: Higginbotham Insurance Agency, Inc., HIG Operations Holdings, Inc., and HIG Intermediate, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Independent retail insurance broker
investments.business_description: Higginbotham is an independent insurance, risk management, and financial services firm founded in 1948 and headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas — the largest privately held middle-market insurance agency in Texas and one of the top 35 commercial brokers in the U.S. The firm provides clients with access to commercial and personal property and casualty coverage, employee benefits, retirement plans, life insurance, and executive compensation solutions through more than 250 regional and national carriers. Stone Point Capital has been a minority investor in Higginbotham since 2009, with the employee-ownership structure largely intact.
investments.end_customers: End customers are Texas-based and regional businesses of all sizes that purchase commercial insurance, employee benefits, and risk management services through Higginbotham's offices across Texas and the South. The firm's deep Texas market relationships and carrier access make it particularly attractive to mid-market companies in industries such as energy, construction, healthcare, and real estate.

investments.company: High Street Buyer, Inc. and High Street Holdco LLC
investments.investment_type: Insurance brokerage platform
investments.business_description: The underlying operating company is High Street Insurance Partners (HSIP), a Traverse City, Michigan-based full-service insurance brokerage founded in 2018 as a roll-up platform targeting small and medium-sized communities. Since inception, HSIP has acquired over 150 independent insurance agencies, predominantly in smaller markets where local relationships are highly valued. ABRY Partners acquired HSIP from Huron Capital in April 2021. HSIP provides business insurance and risk management, employee benefits, financial and retirement services, and personal insurance to its clients.
investments.end_customers: End customers are small businesses, municipalities, farmers, and individuals in small and mid-sized communities across the Midwest and beyond who prefer to work with locally-trusted insurance agents rather than large national brokers. High Street's community-oriented agencies offer personalized service and long-standing local carrier relationships that resonate with Main Street commercial clients and personal lines customers.

investments.company: Inszone Mid, LLC and INSZ Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Insurance brokerage firm
investments.business_description: The operating company is Inszone Insurance Services, a Sacramento, California-based independent insurance brokerage founded in 2002 that has grown into one of the fastest-growing agencies in the U.S. through aggressive M&A activity. Inszone has completed 98 acquisitions since 2020 and operates from 63 locations across 14 states including California, Arizona, Texas, Nevada, Oregon, and Washington. Lightyear Capital joined existing investor BHMS Investments as co-lead investors in November 2023 to support the next phase of growth. Inszone provides property and casualty insurance and employee benefits to its clients.
investments.end_customers: End customers are small and mid-market businesses in the Western and Southwestern U.S. that purchase commercial property and casualty insurance and employee benefits through Inszone's network of local agencies. Lightyear Capital and BHMS Investments provided a growth equity investment in April 2025 to accelerate the firm's acquisition strategy — completing 14 acquisitions in 2025 alone. King Risk Partners provides personal insurance, commercial coverage, and specialty lines products.

investments.company: OakBridge Insurance Agency LLC and Maple Acquisition Holdings, LP
investments.investment_type: Insurance brokerage platform
investments.business_description: Oakbridge Insurance Agency is an Atlanta, Georgia-based independent insurance brokerage formed in December 2020 through the combination of four premier Southeastern insurance firms: Founders Insurance, Hutchinson Traylor Insurance, McGinty Gordon & Associates, and Waites & Foshee. Audax Private Equity acquired Oakbridge from Corsair Capital in September 2023 to support its continued M&A-driven growth; since inception Oakbridge has completed 25 acquisitions and employs over 300 professionals. The firm provides commercial insurance, risk management, and employee benefits with specialist verticals in agriculture, construction, transportation, municipalities, senior living, and nonprofits.
investments.end_customers: End customers are mid-market businesses and institutions primarily in the Southeastern U.S. that require commercial insurance, employee benefits, and specialty risk management solutions. Oakbridge's vertical specializations serve clients in agriculture, transportation fleets, construction contractors, local government entities, senior living operators, and nonprofit organizations who benefit from the firm's deep sector expertise.

investments.company: Patriot Growth Insurance Services, LLC
investments.investment_type: National retail insurance agency
investments.business_description: Patriot Growth Insurance Services is a growth-focused national insurance brokerage platform founded in January 2019 by CEO Matt Gardner, a 30-year insurance distribution veteran, and backed initially by Summit Partners and subsequently recapitalized by GI Partners in September 2021. Patriot has rapidly grown to a top-25 U.S. insurance agency through partnerships with employee benefits and property and casualty agencies, completing 64+ agency partnerships and building a footprint across 18 states serving over 100,000 clients. The firm focuses on partnering with agency owners who want to grow while retaining autonomy.
investments.end_customers: End customers are businesses and individuals who purchase employee benefits, group health insurance, and property and casualty insurance through Patriot's network of local agency partners. Patriot's model particularly targets businesses seeking employee benefits expertise — health, dental, vision, disability, and voluntary benefits — alongside commercial insurance coverage.

investments.company: People Corporation
investments.investment_type: Provider of group benefits, group retirement and human resources services
investments.business_description: People Corporation is Canada's largest third-party administrator and group benefits consultant, founded in 2006 and operating from 49 locations across North America with 1,000-5,000 employees. The company designs and administers customized employee benefit plans including group health, dental, vision, life insurance, disability, critical illness, and wellness programs, serving as a national platform consolidating the Canadian group benefits brokerage market. Apax Partners invested in People Corporation to support its roll-up strategy in the fragmented Canadian employee benefits space.
investments.end_customers: End customers are Canadian employers — ranging from small businesses to large corporations and public sector organizations — that purchase group benefits plans for their employees. People Corporation also provides individual employees with access to health and wellness spending accounts, EFAP programs, virtual healthcare, and digital pharmacy services as part of integrated benefits packages.

investments.company: SageSure Holdings, LLC and SageSure LLC
investments.investment_type: Insurance service provider
investments.business_description: SageSure is one of the largest independent managing general underwriters (MGUs) in the U.S., founded in 2009 and focused on providing catastrophe-exposed property insurance in coastal and challenging markets. The company writes more than $2.3 billion in annual premium across 16 coastal states, offering over 50 competitively priced insurance products on behalf of carrier partners, and serves nearly 400,000 policyholders through 1,000+ employees. SageSure is backed by Warburg Pincus and Stone Point Capital, and received $250 million in equity financing from Amwins and Flexpoint Ford (with Ares Management participation) in 2023. In 2024-2025, SageSure acquired GeoVera Advantage Insurance Services, adding residential earthquake risk underwriting.
investments.end_customers: End customers are homeowners in catastrophe-exposed coastal markets — particularly in Florida, Texas, Louisiana, and other Gulf and Atlantic coast states — who need homeowners insurance that mainstream carriers will not write. SageSure's distribution partners (agents and brokers) place coverage with homeowners who have been non-renewed or cannot find coverage in standard markets due to hurricane, wind, and flood exposure.

investments.company: SelectQuote, Inc. and SQ ABS Issuer, LLC
investments.investment_type: Direct to consumer insurance distribution platform
investments.business_description: SelectQuote (NYSE: SLQT) is a publicly traded, technology-enabled direct-to-consumer insurance distribution and healthcare services company founded in 1985 and headquartered in the U.S. The company operates across four business lines: SelectQuote Senior (Medicare Advantage, Medicare Supplement, and Medicare Part D plans), SelectQuote Life (term and permanent life insurance), SelectQuote Auto & Home (auto and homeowners insurance comparison), and SelectQuote Healthcare Services (pharmacy and care management). SelectQuote represents more than 50 highly rated insurance carriers and earns commissions when consumers purchase policies. In February 2025, Bain Capital led a $350 million strategic investment alongside Morgan Stanley Private Credit and Newlight Partners.
investments.end_customers: Primary end customers are individual consumers — particularly Medicare-eligible seniors — who use SelectQuote's online and phone-based platform to compare and purchase insurance from multiple carriers without paying broker fees. The Senior division serves the Medicare market (adults 65+), while Life, Auto, and Home divisions serve working-age Americans seeking term life, auto, and homeowners coverage respectively.

investments.company: SG Acquisition, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of insurance solutions for car sales
investments.business_description: SG Acquisition, Inc. is the Bain Capital acquisition vehicle for an insurance company investment. Based on Bain Capital Insurance's active investment strategy focused on life, health, property and casualty, distribution, and insurance services, and the 2025 Bain Capital strategic investment in SelectQuote, SG Acquisition may represent an earlier Bain Capital insurance platform investment. Specific public disclosure of the underlying operating business is limited, but the 1L loan in ARCC's portfolio is consistent with a Bain Capital-sponsored insurance sector buyout or recapitalization.
investments.end_customers: The underlying insurance company, depending on its sub-sector (carrier, distribution, or services), would serve commercial businesses, individuals, or institutional insurance buyers seeking insurance products or insurance-adjacent financial services.

investments.company: SIG Parent Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Independent insurance brokerage
investments.business_description: SIG Parent Holdings, LLC is the holding company for Specialty Program Group (SPG), a national wholesale and managing general agency (MGA) insurance intermediary platform backed by Advent International. SPG acquires and scales specialty insurance underwriting businesses and wholesale brokerages across North America, managing over $2 billion in gross written premium across a portfolio of roughly 30 member companies. The platform serves niche markets including property, marine, professional liability, and other specialty lines.
investments.end_customers: SPG's end customers are retail insurance agents and brokers who need access to specialty and non-standard insurance markets for their business and individual clients. These are carriers and reinsurers that rely on SPG-affiliated MGAs for underwriting expertise in hard-to-place or complex risks that mainstream carriers won't write directly.

investments.company: Slaine Holdings LLC
investments.investment_type: Holding company of insurance service providers
investments.business_description: Slaine Holdings LLC is the parent holding entity for SageSure, a leading managing general underwriter (MGU) specializing in catastrophe-exposed residential property insurance in coastal and storm-prone markets. Founded by Terrence McLean (also SageSure's CEO and co-founder), SageSure writes property insurance in markets that major carriers have been exiting, using a technology-driven underwriting model. The company has built one of the largest cat bond programs in the U.S., with over $3 billion in risk capital outstanding.
investments.end_customers: SageSure's end customers are homeowners in coastal and high-risk markets — primarily in Gulf Coast, Southeast, and Atlantic states — who struggle to find affordable property insurance as large national insurers retreat from those geographies. It distributes through independent agents and is used by carriers seeking capacity in these markets.

investments.company: THG Acquisition, LLC
investments.investment_type: Multi-line insurance broker
investments.business_description: THG Acquisition, LLC is the acquisition vehicle used by Clearlake Capital to invest in The Hilb Group (THG), a Richmond, Virginia-based middle-market insurance brokerage and employee benefits consultancy. The Hilb Group was originally backed by The Carlyle Group before Clearlake acquired it, and has grown aggressively through acquisitions of independent insurance agencies across the U.S. The firm operates over 90 branch offices and serves both commercial and personal lines insurance clients.
investments.end_customers: The Hilb Group serves small- and mid-sized businesses seeking property & casualty insurance and employee benefits programs, as well as individual consumers in the communities where its acquired agencies operate. Its acquired agencies typically serve regional and local clients who prefer relationships with independent agents rather than direct carriers.

investments.company: Truist Insurance Holdings, LLC and McGriff Insurance Services, LLC
investments.investment_type: Insurance brokerage firm
investments.business_description: McGriff Insurance Services is one of the largest insurance brokerage firms in the United States, ranking in the top five by premium volume. Originally a subsidiary of Truist Financial, McGriff was sold in May 2024 to an investor group led by Stone Point Capital and Clayton, Dubilier & Rice (CD&R) in a transaction valued at approximately $15.5 billion. McGriff provides commercial property & casualty, employee benefits, risk management, and personal insurance solutions to clients across a wide range of industries.
investments.end_customers: McGriff's clients are primarily mid-market to large corporate clients across the U.S. needing comprehensive commercial insurance and risk management services, including construction, healthcare, real estate, financial institutions, and manufacturing companies. It also serves high-net-worth individuals through personal insurance offerings.

investments.company: World Insurance Associates, LLC and World Associates Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Insurance service provider
investments.business_description: World Insurance Associates is a nationally ranked, full-service insurance brokerage platform headquartered in Iselin, New Jersey, with over 2,000 employees across 260+ offices. The company offers retail property & casualty insurance, wholesale insurance, and retirement and wealth advisory services to more than 300,000 clients. In 2023, Goldman Sachs Asset Management joined Charlesbank Capital Partners as co-lead investors at an enterprise value of approximately $3.4 billion.
investments.end_customers: World Insurance Associates serves individuals, families, and businesses of all sizes seeking personal and commercial insurance, as well as employers and employees seeking employee benefits and retirement planning solutions. Its acquisition strategy adds local independent agencies whose existing client bases span main street businesses, nonprofits, and individual consumers.

investments.company: Balrog Acquisition, Inc., Balrog Topco, Inc. and Balrog Parent, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Manufacturer and distributor of specialty bakery ingredients
investments.business_description: Balrog Acquisition, Inc. and related entities are the holding company stack created by Clearlake Capital to acquire BakeMark USA, the recognized market leader in the manufacture and distribution of bakery ingredients, products, and supplies in North America. BakeMark, headquartered in Pico Rivera, California, serves approximately 19,000 customers with a full portfolio including mixes, fillings, icings, glazes, frozen products, and bakery supplies, distributed from over 40 distribution centers. The company has over 100 years of history serving the baking industry.
investments.end_customers: BakeMark's customers are commercial bakeries, in-store supermarket bakeries, foodservice operators, and artisan bakeries across the United States and Canada that require consistent, high-quality bakery ingredients at scale. Its proprietary platforms and formulated products create sticky customer relationships with operations that depend on reliable supply of technical bakery inputs.

investments.company: Bamboo Purchaser, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of nursery, garden, and greenhouse products
investments.business_description: Bamboo Purchaser, Inc. is the acquisition vehicle Clearlake Capital used to acquire Bamboo Health (formerly Appriss Health), a Louisville, Kentucky-based healthcare technology company focused on care collaboration and behavioral health information exchange. Bamboo Health serves over 2,500 hospitals, 7,800 post-acute facilities, 25,000 pharmacies, 45 state governments, and over one million providers, impacting more than one billion patient encounters annually. Its platforms enable real-time patient data sharing between payers, providers, and government agencies.
investments.end_customers: Bamboo Health's end customers are hospital systems, health plans, state Medicaid agencies, pharmacies, and ambulatory care providers who need timely access to patient care event data to coordinate treatment, manage transitions of care, and combat opioid misuse through prescription drug monitoring programs (PDMPs). Government agencies rely on its infrastructure for statewide health information exchange.

investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: BGI Purchaser, Inc. is the Blackstone-affiliated acquisition vehicle for an underlying consumer distribution business. Based on available information, this entity is structured as a Blackstone private equity holding company in the consumer distribution segment. The specific operating company acquired through this vehicle has not been publicly disclosed in detail, but it operates in consumer products distribution as reflected by its industry classification in ARCC's portfolio.
investments.end_customers: The end customers of the underlying business are consistent with consumer distribution — likely retail chains, foodservice operators, or consumer product resellers depending on the specific operating business acquired.

investments.company: Blazing Star Parent, LLC
investments.investment_type: Healthcare, pharmacy and retail provider
investments.business_description: Blazing Star Parent, LLC is the holding company created by Sycamore Partners to acquire Walgreens Boots Alliance (WBA), one of the largest pharmacy-led health and well-being companies in the world, with approximately 8,700 retail pharmacy locations across the United States and a significant international presence. The $10 billion take-private transaction was completed in August 2025, taking Walgreens off the Nasdaq after nearly a century as a public company. Walgreens operates retail pharmacies, health clinics, and a substantial pharmacy benefit management and specialty pharmacy business.
investments.end_customers: Walgreens' primary customers are retail pharmacy patients picking up prescriptions and purchasing over-the-counter health, beauty, and consumer products. The company also serves insurance plans and pharmacy benefit managers (PBMs) as a network pharmacy, and contracts with healthcare systems to provide clinical services including immunizations, chronic disease management, and retail health clinics.

investments.company: BR PJK Produce, LLC
investments.investment_type: Specialty produce distributor
investments.business_description: BR PJK Produce, LLC is a holding entity associated with PJK Food Service (dba Keany Produce & Gourmet), a Landover, Maryland-based wholesale produce and specialty food distributor serving the Mid-Atlantic region since 1978. Keany Produce distributes fresh produce, dairy, and specialty food items to restaurants, hotels, schools, healthcare facilities, and grocery stores across the region. The company operates temperature-controlled distribution facilities and serves thousands of foodservice accounts.
investments.end_customers: Keany Produce's end customers are foodservice operators including restaurants, hotels, country clubs, healthcare facilities, universities, and school systems in the Washington D.C. metropolitan area and surrounding Mid-Atlantic states who rely on consistent, next-day delivery of fresh produce and specialty food products.
