investments.company: Carrera Bidco Limited
investments.investment_type: Fuel and convenience retailer
investments.business_description: Carrera Bidco Limited is a UK-registered holding company created as part of a CVC Capital Partners private equity transaction in the consumer distribution sector. As a BidCo structure, it sits directly above the operating business in CVC's corporate stack. Based on available information, the underlying operating business is in consumer distribution, with the BidCo structure used to hold the acquisition debt. The specific operating company has not been publicly identified in available sources.
investments.end_customers: Given the consumer distribution industry classification and CVC's focus on that sector, the end customers of the underlying business are likely retailers, wholesale buyers, or end consumers purchasing through distribution channels, consistent with CVC's portfolio of European and North American consumer businesses.

investments.company: City Line Distributors LLC and City Line Investments LLC
investments.investment_type: Specialty food distributor
investments.business_description: City Line Distributors is a third-generation, family-founded foodservice distributor headquartered in West Haven, Connecticut, serving restaurants, hotels, schools, healthcare facilities, and institutional customers across 30 counties in New England. The company is the largest single-facility food distributor in New England and ranks among the top 30 foodservice distributors in the United States by revenue. Backed by Frontenac Company, City Line serves as the foundation for the Navis Food Partners distribution platform, which is building an independent foodservice distribution network in the Northeast through add-on acquisitions.
investments.end_customers: City Line's customers are foodservice operators across New England — including independent restaurants, hotel chains, educational institutions, and healthcare systems — that need consistent, broad-line food distribution with same- or next-day delivery capabilities. Institutional customers value City Line's deep regional expertise and broadline product assortment.

investments.company: DecoPac, Inc. and KCAKE Holdings Inc.
investments.investment_type: Supplier of cake decorating solutions and products to in-store bakeries
investments.business_description: DecoPac is the world's largest supplier of cake decorations and related products to professional cake decorators, in-store bakeries, and retail bakeries, headquartered in Anoka, Minnesota. The company's product portfolio includes edible and non-edible decorations, sprinkles, fondant, pastry bags, and figurines, along with proprietary technology platforms: PhotoCake (which prints custom edible images onto baked goods) and Cakes.com (a consumer-facing cake personalization and ordering platform). Kohlberg & Company acquired DecoPac in 2021 for approximately $550 million from Snow Phipps Group.
investments.end_customers: DecoPac's primary customers are in-store supermarket bakeries and professional cake shops across North America that need a reliable, one-stop source for decorating supplies and technology platforms. Its Cakes.com platform bridges B2B supply to end consumers who customize and order decorated cakes, linking bakeries with retail demand.

investments.company: Display Holding Company, Inc., Saldon Holdings, Inc. and Fastsigns Holdings Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of visual communications solutions
investments.business_description: This holding company stack represents FASTSIGNS International, a leading global franchise system for custom signage, graphics, and visual communications, headquartered in Carrollton, Texas. FASTSIGNS operates over 780 franchise locations across the U.S., Canada, the U.K., Australia, and other countries, and offers comprehensive sign and graphics solutions including banners, vehicle wraps, digital displays, trade show graphics, and architectural signage. Roark Capital, an Atlanta-based private equity firm specializing in franchise and restaurant businesses, is the current owner.
investments.end_customers: FASTSIGNS franchise centers serve small businesses, corporations, event organizers, healthcare facilities, schools, and government agencies that need custom visual communications — from interior branding and wayfinding signage to vehicle graphics and outdoor advertising. Businesses in virtually every sector rely on FASTSIGNS for both recurring signage needs and one-time projects.

investments.end_customers: Based on the consumer distribution industry classification, the underlying business likely serves retail chains, foodservice operators, or institutional buyers through wholesale or distribution channels, consistent with Ares Management's broader consumer sector investment thesis.

investments.end_customers: Sterling Group's consumer distribution investments have historically served retail buyers, food manufacturers, or industrial distributors. investments.company: Hills Distribution, Inc., Hills Intermediate FT Holdings, LLC and GMP Hills, LP
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Hills Distribution is a PE-backed platform formed by Grove Mountain Partners to build a leading HVAC and plumbing distribution network in the Northeastern United States. The platform operates through multiple regional companies including Plimpton & Hills, Coastal Supply Group (founded 1972, Staten Island, NY), and RJ Murray Company (founded 1922, Latham, NY), running approximately 30 distribution branches across five states. Each member company retains its brand identity and management while gaining shared resources through the Hills platform.
investments.end_customers: Hills Distribution's customers are licensed plumbing and HVAC contractors, mechanical engineers, and construction firms across the Northeast who need reliable, quick access to wholesale plumbing fixtures, HVAC equipment, and related supplies. Contractors depend on these distributors for the technical product knowledge, regional parts availability, and credit terms needed to complete residential and commercial projects.

investments.investment_type: Distributor of specialty foods
investments.business_description: LJ Perimeter Buyer, Inc. and LJ Perimeter Co-Invest, L.P. are the acquisition and co-investment vehicles created by Leonard Green & Partners for a consumer distribution investment in the food and beverage sector, as indicated by the company's industry classification in financial databases. investments.company: Marcone Yellowstone Buyer Inc. and Marcone Yellowstone Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Distributor of OEM appliance aftermarket parts
investments.business_description: Marcone Yellowstone represents the Genstar Capital-backed holding structure for Marcone, the leading North American distributor of OEM appliance repair parts and related products. Headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri, Marcone operates over 113 facilities and serves approximately 43,000 technician customers with parts for major brands including Whirlpool, Electrolux, GE, Bosch, Samsung, and LG. The company has expanded beyond appliance parts into HVAC distribution (via the Munch's Supply acquisition) and plumbing (Professional Plumbing Group), positioning as a broad home systems parts and supplies platform.
investments.end_customers: Marcone's core customers are independent appliance repair technicians and extended warranty service providers who need rapid, local access to OEM replacement parts for branded household appliances. As Marcone has expanded into HVAC and plumbing, it also serves HVAC contractors and plumbing professionals through its distribution branches.

investments.company: Mavis Tire Express Services Topco Corp., Metis HoldCo, Inc., and Metis TopCo, LP
investments.investment_type: Auto parts retailer
investments.business_description: Mavis Tire Express Services is one of the largest independent tire and automotive service companies in North America, operating approximately 3,500 owned and franchised retail locations under brands including Mavis Discount Tire, Mavis Tires & Brakes, Town Fair Tire, Tire Kingdom, NTB, Express Oil Change & Tire Engineers, Brakes Plus, Tuffy Tire & Auto Service, and Midas. Backed by KKR, the company offers tire sales and installation, oil changes, brake service, wheel alignments, and other maintenance services. Metis HoldCo and Metis TopCo are the KKR holding company entities for this platform.
investments.end_customers: Mavis serves everyday car owners who need routine automotive maintenance — primarily tire replacement, brake service, and oil changes — at competitively priced, conveniently located service centers. The company targets value-conscious consumers who prefer independent specialty service chains over dealership service departments.

investments.company: Midco Holding, LLC and Nivel Topco, LLC
investments.investment_type: Manufacturer of aftermarket golf cart parts and accessories
investments.business_description: Nivel Topco and Midco Holding represent the holding structure for Nivel, a global manufacturer, distributor, and seller of aftermarket parts and accessories for niche vehicles including personal transportation vehicles (golf carts/PTVs), off-road vehicles (UTVs/ORVs), heavy-duty equipment, and the automotive sector. Nivel maintains a catalog of over 100,000 parts and accessories and serves as a U.S.-based manufacturer while also managing a broad distribution network for both domestic and international markets.
investments.end_customers: Nivel's customers are golf cart dealers, recreational vehicle dealers, and aftermarket specialty retailers who sell and service PTVs and ORVs and need consistent access to a broad catalog of replacement parts and accessories. Fleet operators and municipalities with golf cart or utility vehicle fleets also purchase through Nivel's dealer network.

investments.investment_type: Produce distribution platform
investments.business_description: Mr. Greens (operating through Florida Veg Investments) is a Miami, Florida-based value-added distributor of fresh produce, dairy, and dry goods serving over 3,500 customers including restaurants, hotels, country clubs, caterers, grocery stores, and retail outlets across Florida and Texas. The company was originally backed by Shoreline Equity Partners and subsequently acquired by Sterling Investment Partners. The holding structure — including MRG Texas and Restaurant Produce and Services Blocker — reflects its multi-state operations.
investments.end_customers: Mr. Greens primarily serves foodservice operators — restaurants, hotels, country clubs, and caterers — in Florida and Texas who need daily delivery of fresh produce and specialty ingredients. Its value-added services (pre-cut, cleaned, and portioned produce) differentiate it for upscale foodservice accounts that require labor-saving preparation.

The company was originally backed by Shoreline Equity Partners and subsequently acquired by Sterling Investment Partners. The holding structure — including MRG Texas and Restaurant Produce and Services Blocker — reflects its multi-state operations.
investments.end_customers: Mr. Greens primarily serves foodservice operators — restaurants, hotels, country clubs, and caterers — in Florida and Texas who need daily delivery of fresh produce and specialty ingredients. Its value-added services (pre-cut, cleaned, and portioned produce) differentiate it for upscale foodservice accounts that require labor-saving preparation.

investments.company: Project Cardinal Acquisition, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of outsourced foodservice to K-12 school districts
investments.business_description: Project Cardinal Acquisition, LLC is an Ares Management-sponsored acquisition vehicle for a consumer distribution company. The 'Project Cardinal' code name is a typical deal code used before a transaction closes; the underlying operating company has not been publicly identified in available press sources. Based on the industry classification, the business operates in consumer products distribution.
investments.end_customers: As an Ares consumer distribution investment, the underlying business likely serves retail chains, foodservice operators, or other commercial buyers requiring wholesale distribution of consumer products. The specific end customer profile would depend on the operating company's product category and geography.

investments.company: Quirch Foods Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Specialty food distributor
investments.business_description: Quirch Foods is a Miami-based leading distributor, importer, and exporter of protein and ethnic food products, founded by the Quirch family in 1967 and backed by Palladium Equity Partners since 2018. The company operates through 23 distribution facilities spanning over 2.3 million square feet across the U.S., Puerto Rico, and Latin America, with a fleet of nearly 400 refrigerated trucks. After merging with Colorado Boxed Beef and making several additional acquisitions, Quirch has grown into a national distributor of beef, pork, poultry, seafood, and ethnic food products under brands including Panamei Seafood, Mambo, and High River Angus.
investments.end_customers: Quirch primarily serves independent and ethnic grocery stores, foodservice distributors, and export customers who need competitively priced, broad-assortment proteins and Hispanic specialty food products. Its ethnic food expertise gives it a strong position with Latin American and Caribbean-focused retailers and restaurants across the U.S. and in export markets.

investments.company: Reddy Ice LLC
investments.investment_type: Packaged ice manufacturer and distributor
investments.business_description: Reddy Ice is the largest manufacturer and distributor of packaged ice products in North America, operating under the Reddy Ice brand across 37 states and the District of Columbia, with over 115 manufacturing facilities, distribution centers, and cold storage locations. Owned by Stone Canyon Industries Holdings (SCI Capital Partners), Reddy Ice sells packaged ice through consumer packaged goods channels including grocery and convenience retail, as well as to restaurants, stadiums, and special events. In early 2026, Reddy Ice announced the acquisition of Arctic Glacier from Carlyle, further consolidating the North American packaged ice market.
investments.end_customers: Reddy Ice's end customers are primarily consumers buying bagged ice at grocery stores, convenience stores, and gas stations for home use, parties, and outdoor activities. It also serves commercial customers including restaurants, bars, event venues, seafood processors, and agricultural operations that require large, reliable supplies of packaged ice.

investments.company: Royal Borrower, LLC and Royal Parent, LP
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Royal Borrower, LLC and Royal Parent, LP are the holding entities for Royal Cup Coffee and Tea, a Birmingham, Alabama-based leading importer, roaster, and distributor of premium coffees and teas to foodservice, hospitality, office, and specialty coffee markets since 1896. Royal Cup distributes to restaurants, hotels, airlines, offices, resorts, and convenience stores across North America and the Caribbean. In March 2026, Royal Cup announced it had signed an agreement to acquire Farmer Brothers Coffee, which would further expand its roasting and distribution scale.
investments.end_customers: Royal Cup's primary customers are foodservice and hospitality operators — hotels, restaurants, airlines, office coffee programs, and resorts — that require a reliable premium coffee and tea program with full-service support including equipment, training, and supply management. Corporate offices and specialty retail accounts represent a growing segment as well.

investments.company: SCIH Salt Holdings Inc.
investments.investment_type: Salt and packaged ice melt manufacturer and distributor
investments.business_description: SCIH Salt Holdings Inc. is a holding entity within Stone Canyon Industries Holdings (SCIH), a global industrial holding company that has built the largest salt platform in North America through a series of acquisitions. SCIH's salt operations include Kissner Group (rock salt mines in Detroit, MI and Lyons, KS, and Canadian production facilities) and Morton Salt (acquired as part of the K+S Americas business for $3.2 billion in 2021), producing and distributing bulk deicing salt, packaged consumer salt, and specialty industrial salt. Morton Salt is the iconic U.S. consumer brand for table salt.
investments.end_customers: SCIH's salt businesses serve a broad range of customers: municipalities and road departments (bulk deicing rock salt for winter road treatment), grocery retailers and consumers (Morton table salt and water softener salt), food processors (industrial salt for food manufacturing), water treatment facilities (water softening salt), and chemical producers (industrial salt applications).

investments.company: Shur-Co Acquisition, Inc. and Shur-Co Holdco, Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Shur-Co is a leading manufacturer of innovative cargo covering systems, tarping solutions, and cargo control accessories for the transportation industry, headquartered in Yankton, South Dakota. Backed by Behrman Capital since 2021, Shur-Co produces automated and manual tarp systems, airbags, and straps primarily for commercial trucks, trailers, and agricultural equipment. The company has grown through acquisitions including AAA Tarps (2023), Dawbarn & Sons in the UK (2022), US Tarp (2024), and AB Airbags (2024).
investments.end_customers: Shur-Co's end customers are trucking companies, construction contractors, agricultural haulers, and recycling/refuse operators who need durable cargo covering systems to secure loads on flatbed, dump, and specialty trailers. Its products are also sold through trailer manufacturers as OEM equipment and through aftermarket dealers serving the transportation industry.

investments.company: US Salt Investors, LLC and Emerald Lake Pearl Acquisition-A, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Producer and packager of compressed, household, and packaged salt
investments.business_description: US Salt Investors, LLC and Emerald Lake Pearl Acquisition-A, L.P. are holding entities for US Salt, an evaporated salt producer based in Watkins Glen, New York, with over 100 years of operation. US Salt uses solution mining of underground deposits to produce high-purity evaporated salt for consumer food, food processing, water conditioning, pharmaceutical, and industrial applications. The business was divested by Stone Canyon Industries/Kissner as a DOJ-required remedy and subsequently managed by Emerald Lake Capital Management before a $907.5 million acquisition agreement with ContextLogic Holdings.
investments.end_customers: US Salt sells to retail grocery chains (consumer food-grade salt), food manufacturers, water softener salt dealers, pharmaceutical companies requiring USP-grade salt, and industrial end-users. Its premium purity evaporated salt commands higher margins than rock salt and serves quality-sensitive end markets that require consistent chemical specifications.

investments.company: VRS Buyer, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of on-site mobile and rail re-fueling solutions
investments.business_description: VRS Buyer, Inc. (dba Liquid Tech Solutions) is a Vista Equity Partners-era acquisition vehicle for Liquid Tech Solutions, the nation's largest tech-enabled mobile on-site refueling company, founded in 1998 and headquartered in Stoughton, Massachusetts. Liquid Tech provides on-site diesel fuel delivery, diesel exhaust fluid supply, generator fueling, tank monitoring, and related services to a diverse customer base across all 50 states, operating through a route-based network of company-owned trucks and delivery partners. Lindsay Goldberg ultimately owned LTS before selling it to Velocity Rail Solutions (Wind Point Partners) in July 2025.
investments.end_customers: Liquid Tech Solutions serves fleet operators, construction companies, data center operators, emergency backup power users, and rail operators who need reliable on-site fuel delivery to keep their equipment running without redirecting vehicles to fuel stations. Its managed fueling programs are valued by customers with large diesel fleets that require continuous, uninterrupted operations.

investments.company: Worldwide Produce Acquisition, LLC and REP WWP Coinvest IV, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Fresh and specialty food distributor
investments.business_description: Worldwide Produce is one of the largest produce and dairy distributors in Southern California, serving restaurants, foodservice customers, and grocery stores across the western United States from five climate-controlled distribution facilities. Backed by Ridgemont Equity Partners (with Sole Source Capital retaining a minority stake), Worldwide Produce stocks approximately 6,000 SKUs including fresh fruits, vegetables, dairy products, and specialty foods. The company has grown through add-on acquisitions including Left Coast Food, Valley Food Services, Chef's Choice Produce, Vision Produce, and Avalon Fine Foods.
investments.end_customers: Worldwide Produce's customers are restaurants, hotels, country clubs, grocery stores, and institutional foodservice operators across Southern California, Nevada, and the broader western U.S. that need daily delivery of fresh, high-quality produce and dairy. Its specialty and organic offerings appeal to upscale restaurant accounts that demand premium ingredients.

investments.company: AeriTek Global US Acquisition Inc., AeriTek Global Holdings LLC, and Minus Forty QBD Corp.
investments.investment_type: Manufacturer of commercial refrigeration and foodservice equipment
investments.business_description: AeriTek Global (with Minus Forty QBD Corp., dba Due North, as an acquired subsidiary) is a Mill Point Capital-backed commercial refrigeration and foodservice equipment manufacturer and distributor, operating under the Imbera and Torrey brands globally and the Due North brand in North America. AeriTek originated as a carve-out from FEMSA (the Mexican industrial conglomerate) in late 2024 and has annual production capacity of over one million refrigeration units across 13 sales offices globally, headquartered in Kennesaw, Georgia. Imbera specializes in coolers and merchandising refrigerators, while Torrey produces refrigeration and weighing/labeling equipment for retail and foodservice.
investments.end_customers: AeriTek's end customers are convenience stores, supermarkets, foodservice operators, restaurants, and vending companies that need commercial-grade refrigeration units for product merchandising, food storage, and point-of-sale cooling. Retail chains buying refrigerated display coolers, and food processors needing Torrey weighing and labeling equipment, are core customer segments.

investments.company: AI Aqua Merger Sub, Inc.
investments.investment_type: End to end provider of water solutions to a wide range of customer bases
investments.business_description: AI Aqua Merger Sub, Inc. is the acquisition vehicle for Culligan International, a global leader in water treatment products and services for household, commercial, and industrial applications, backed by Advent International and headquartered in Rosemont, Illinois. Culligan operates in dozens of countries, providing home water softeners, drinking water systems, water filtration, commercial/industrial water treatment, and bottleless water coolers. The company has aggressively expanded through acquisitions including Waterlogic (bottle-free coolers) and numerous regional water service dealers.
investments.end_customers: Culligan serves residential homeowners seeking softer, cleaner water through in-home water softeners and filtration systems, and commercial and industrial customers needing customized water treatment for manufacturing processes, foodservice, healthcare, and office hydration. Its dealer network provides recurring monthly service agreements that create sticky long-term customer relationships.

investments.company: Airx Climate Solutions, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of commercial HVAC equipment and services
investments.business_description: AirX Climate Solutions (ACS) is a Gryphon Investors-backed designer, manufacturer, distributor, and servicer of highly engineered specialty HVAC products for functionally critical applications, headquartered in Yukon, Oklahoma. ACS serves telecommunications infrastructure, data centers, energy storage, industrial, educational, and commercial markets with custom climate control solutions that must operate reliably in demanding environments. The company has grown through add-on acquisitions including Powrmatic (UK) and AboveAir Technologies.
investments.end_customers: AirX's customers are data center operators, telecommunications tower companies, energy storage facilities, industrial manufacturers, schools, and commercial building owners that need specialty HVAC systems engineered for non-standard environments where conventional equipment would fail. These are mission-critical installations where equipment downtime is extremely costly.

investments.company: Allclear Commercial Inc., Allclear Military Inc., Allclear Space Inc., and Allclear Group LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: AllClear Aerospace & Defense (operating through Allclear Commercial, Military, and Space subsidiaries under Allclear Group LLC) is a provider of aerospace aftermarket sustainment solutions for military aviation, headquartered in the U.S. and originally backed by Odyssey Investment Partners before being restructured in June 2025 with senior lenders taking ownership. AllClear distributes OEM spare parts, provides engineering services, and offers managed repair solutions for over 25 major military aircraft platforms under distribution agreements with leading defense OEMs. The Space subsidiary reflects expansion into orbital market services.
investments.end_customers: AllClear's primary customers are the U.S. Department of Defense and allied governments seeking reliable, cost-effective sustainment for aging military aircraft fleets — including parts for helicopters, fixed-wing aircraft, and naval aviation platforms. Commercial MRO (maintenance, repair, and overhaul) operators and government prime contractors also source parts and repair services through AllClear's distribution network.

investments.company: Arrowhead Holdco Company and Arrowhead GS Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Distributor of non-discretionary, mission-critical aftermarket replacement parts
investments.business_description: The operating company is Arrowhead Engineered Products, headquartered in Blaine, Minnesota — a leading global distributor of non-discretionary, proprietary branded aftermarket replacement parts for motorized vehicles and equipment. Arrowhead supplies over 700,000 SKUs including starters, clutches, driveline components, belts, gaskets, and lighting products across its portfolio of brands (Red Hawk, All Balls Racing Group, Western Power Sports, Stens, J&N, TriLink, Bihr, and others). The company operates 10+ distribution centers in North America plus sourcing offices in Asia, and acquired Ariens' aftermarket business as part of its expansion.
investments.end_customers: Arrowhead's primary customers are aftermarket distributors, dealers, service shops, and retailers serving outdoor power equipment (lawn mowers, snow blowers), powersports (ATVs, motorcycles, UTVs), golf (golf carts), marine, and agricultural end markets. Customers buy Arrowhead parts because they are mission-critical replacement parts — equipment operators need these non-discretionary items to keep their machinery operational — and Arrowhead's vast SKU breadth and fast availability across 100,000+ customer accounts makes it the preferred one-stop source.

investments.company: BGIF IV Fearless Utility Services, Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: BGIF IV Fearless Utility Services is a Brookfield-backed platform providing outsourced utility field services, operating under the 'FR Utility Services' parent entity. The company provides contractor services to utility operators including infrastructure inspection, locating, and field support services for gas, electric, and telecom utility systems. Brookfield's infrastructure investment arm has historically built platforms serving utility companies with workforce-intensive field operations.
investments.end_customers: End customers are investor-owned utilities, cooperatives, and municipal utility operators that outsource field operations such as damage prevention, locating, and infrastructure services. These utilities contract out such work to manage labor costs, meet regulatory requirements, and handle peak demand without maintaining large permanent field workforces.

investments.company: CPIG Holdco Inc.
investments.investment_type: Distributor of engineered fluid power and complex machined solutions
investments.business_description: CPIG Holdco Inc. is the holding company for Consolidated Precision Products (CPP), a leading manufacturer of highly-engineered precision investment castings and sub-assemblies for aerospace, defense, and industrial gas turbine markets — backed by Warburg Pincus and Berkshire Partners. CPP produces complex superalloy, titanium, aluminum, magnesium, and steel castings used across commercial aircraft, military platforms, missile systems, helicopters, and regional jets. The company operates multiple facilities and has a long track record as a critical supplier to major airframers and defense primes.
investments.end_customers: CPP's end customers include major aerospace OEMs (Boeing, Airbus, GE Aerospace, Pratt & Whitney, Honeywell) and defense system integrators (Lockheed Martin, Raytheon, Northrop Grumman) that require precision cast components for flight-critical and mission-critical applications. These customers rely on CPP for complex, tight-tolerance castings that cannot be easily sourced elsewhere, making the relationships highly sticky.

investments.company: Dynamic NC Aerospace Holdings, LLC and Dynamic NC Investment Holdings, LP
investments.investment_type: Provider of aerospace technology and equipment
investments.business_description: Dynamic NC, based in Rose Hill, Kansas, is a market-leading aerospace manufacturer specializing in difficult-to-machine aerostructure components and assemblies that serve flight-critical functions. The company focuses on hard metals, large monolithic structures, and complex 5-axis CNC machining for blue-chip OEMs and Tier 1 aerospace suppliers. Dynamic NC is backed by Banbury Private Capital and Battle Investment Group, both specialists in aerospace, defense, and critical infrastructure investment.
investments.end_customers: End customers are major aerospace OEMs (Boeing, Airbus) and Tier 1 aerospace suppliers who require precision-machined, flight-critical structural components for commercial and military aircraft. These customers rely on Dynamic NC for technically complex work that requires sophisticated multi-axis machining capabilities and rigorous quality control, creating high switching costs.

investments.company: EIS Legacy Holdco, LLC
investments.investment_type: Distributor of electric applicator components
investments.business_description: EIS Legacy Holdco is the holding company for a PE-backed electronics components manufacturing and distribution business. Bloomberg identifies the operating entity as providing electronics components manufacturing services, and the 'Legacy' designation suggests it may represent legacy product lines or an older portfolio of insurance-related technology or electronics assets. The company appears to be a niche industrial manufacturer or distributor of specialty electronic components.
investments.end_customers: End customers are manufacturers and industrial operators who require specialty electronic components, sub-assemblies, or related manufactured products. Buyers are typically procurement teams at OEMs or industrial companies needing reliable supply of engineered electronic parts.

investments.company: Endurance PT Technology Buyer Corporation and Endurance PT Technology Holdings LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Endurance PT Technology is the Thoma Bravo acquisition vehicle for a physical therapy technology platform. Given Thoma Bravo's exclusive focus on enterprise software and the 'PT Technology' designation, this entity represents a software business serving the physical therapy and rehabilitation therapy sector — likely a practice management, EMR, billing, or clinical workflow software platform. Thoma Bravo is one of the most active buyers of vertical market software companies in healthcare services.
investments.end_customers: End customers are physical therapy clinics, outpatient rehabilitation therapy practices, hospital therapy departments, and multi-site PT chains that use the software for patient scheduling, clinical documentation (EMR), billing/revenue cycle management, and outcomes tracking. Clinics adopt such platforms to improve operational efficiency, ensure regulatory compliance, and reduce manual administrative burden.

investments.company: Generator US Buyer, Inc. and Total Power Limited
investments.investment_type: Provider of generator-based power solutions
investments.business_description: The operating company is Total Power Limited, a Canadian-based full-service provider of power generation solutions — including standby, mobile, and prime power generator systems — with Apax Partners as the PE sponsor for the US acquisition. Total Power has been in the power generation business since 1959, operating across Canada, providing maintenance and repair services, generator rentals, and new equipment distribution for commercial and industrial generators. Generator US Buyer is the US acquisition vehicle used in connection with Total Power's broader platform.
investments.end_customers: Total Power's end customers are commercial, industrial, healthcare, and data center operators who rely on standby and backup generator systems for power continuity. Customers purchase generators and service contracts to protect critical operations from power outages, with healthcare facilities, data centers, and industrial plants representing the most mission-critical segments.

investments.company: Generator US Buyer, Inc. and Total Power Limited
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: The operating company is Total Power Limited, a Canadian-based full-service provider of power generation solutions — including standby, mobile, and prime power generator systems — with Apax Partners as the PE sponsor for the US acquisition. investments.company: Ground Penetrating Radar Systems, LLC and RC VI Buckeye Holdings LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of underground utility locating and concrete scanning
investments.business_description: The operating company is GPRS (Ground Penetrating Radar Systems), the nation's largest company specializing in underground utility detection and concrete structure scanning, backed by Roark Capital. GPRS provides subsurface damage prevention and infrastructure visualization services using ground-penetrating radar (GPR), video pipe inspection, 3D laser scanning, and its proprietary SiteMap GIS data platform across every major market in the United States. The company serves utilities, contractors, engineers, and businesses to prevent utility strikes and provide existing conditions documentation.
investments.end_customers: GPRS customers are construction contractors, civil engineers, utilities, facility managers, and municipalities that need to understand what is underground or inside concrete structures before excavating, coring, or cutting. The service is critical for safety (preventing utility strikes, electrocution, gas leaks) and for project planning, making GPRS's work non-discretionary whenever digging or structural modification occurs.

investments.company: Imaging Business Machines, L.L.C. and Scanner Holdings Corporation
investments.investment_type: Provider of high-speed intelligent document scanning hardware and software
investments.business_description: The operating company is ibml (Imaging Business Machines), founded in 1992 and headquartered in Irondale, Alabama — a leading designer and manufacturer of high-speed document scanners and intelligent document processing (IDP) solutions, in which Ares Management acquired a majority stake in 2007. ibml builds the ImageTrac family of high-speed, high-volume document capture scanners widely used in mail processing, financial services, and government document management, and provides end-to-end scanning, software, and services for large-scale information capture operations. The company serves customers that need to process millions of physical documents rapidly and convert them to structured digital data.
investments.end_customers: ibml's customers are large enterprises and government agencies with high-volume document processing needs, including banks and financial institutions (check processing, loan document capture), insurance companies, healthcare payers, the airline industry (ticket/document capture), and government mail-handling operations. The 'OPH' entity is likely a special purpose vehicle in the capital structure used to hold or finance specific assets.
investments.end_customers: Contracted off-takers of NextEra Energy Partners' renewable power generation are utilities and large commercial/industrial power purchasers who have entered long-term power purchase agreements (PPAs), typically 15-25 years. These counterparties buy clean energy to meet renewable portfolio standards, sustainability commitments, or hedge against fossil fuel price volatility.

investments.company: Paris US Holdco, Inc. & 1001028292 Ontario Inc.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Paris US Holdco and its Ontario-registered affiliate are the acquisition vehicles through which Centerbridge Partners acquired Precinmac, a leading supplier of complex precision machined components for mission-critical applications in aerospace, defense, space, and semiconductor markets. Precinmac operates through nine facilities across the US and Canada (including the Ontario entity), manufacturing high-complexity precision components for flight-critical and mission-critical applications. The company was acquired by Centerbridge from Pine Island Capital Partners and Bain Capital's Private Credit Group in late 2024.
investments.end_customers: Precinmac's customers are aerospace OEMs and Tier 1 suppliers (for aircraft and defense platforms), space launch vehicle manufacturers, semiconductor equipment makers, and defense system integrators that require extremely precise, complex machined parts. These customers source from Precinmac because of its demonstrated capability in high-requirement materials and tight-tolerance applications where quality failures are not acceptable.

investments.company: Pave America Holding, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of high-quality asphalt and concrete services for commercial properties
investments.business_description: Pave America is the largest US provider of commercial paving maintenance services, acquired by AEA Investors and British Columbia Investment Management Corporation (BCI) from Trivest Partners in September 2025. Headquartered in Virginia with 27 branches and self-perform capabilities in 43 states, Pave America provides asphalt and concrete maintenance, repair, milling, striping, and sealcoating services to commercial, industrial, HOA, utility, and municipal customers with 1,600+ employees. The company was formed in 2023 through the merger of Pavement Partners and Brothers National, building a national platform through an active acquisition strategy.
investments.end_customers: Pave America serves commercial property managers, HOAs, industrial facility owners, utilities, and municipalities that need regular asphalt and concrete maintenance for parking lots, roadways, and paved surfaces. Pavement maintenance is a recurring, non-discretionary expenditure for property owners — neglecting it leads to exponentially more expensive repairs — making Pave America's services a predictable recurring revenue stream.

investments.company: Radius Aerospace, Inc. and Radius Aerospace Europe Limited
investments.investment_type: Metal fabricator in the aerospace industry
investments.business_description: Radius Aerospace, formed by Arlington Capital Partners from the acquisition of Triumph Group's forming and fabrication facilities in 2019, is a leading provider of complex aerostructure fabrications and vertically integrated products for aerospace and defense OEMs and Tier 1 customers. Radius specializes in advanced forming methods including titanium super-plastic forming (SPF), stretch forming, hydroforming, chemical milling, metal bonding, and composite bonding across five US facilities (Hot Springs AR, Shelbyville IN, Fort Worth TX, Phoenix AZ, San Diego CA) and UK facilities acquired from Doncasters. The company serves the most challenging fabrication requirements across commercial and military aircraft programs.
investments.end_customers: Radius Aerospace's customers are large aerospace OEMs (Boeing, Airbus, Northrop Grumman, Lockheed Martin) and Tier 1 aerospace suppliers that need complex formed metallic aerostructures including skins, frames, bulkheads, and other flight-critical structural components. These customers rely on Radius for specialized capabilities — particularly titanium SPF and large-scale stretch forming — that require significant capital investment and expertise to perform.

investments.company: Radwell Parent, LLC
investments.investment_type: Distributor of maintenance, repair, and operations parts
investments.business_description: Radwell International, established in 1979 and backed by Bain Capital, is a global leader in industrial automation parts distribution and repair services, providing new, surplus, refurbished, and obsolete automation components — including PLCs, drives, motors, sensors, and industrial electronics — to manufacturing facilities worldwide. Radwell stocks industrial automation, MRO, pneumatic, motion, electronic, hydraulic, HVAC, and electrical control equipment with same-day shipping capabilities, and also offers component-level repair services for industrial electronic equipment. The company serves as a critical partner for plants experiencing unplanned downtime who need hard-to-find or obsolete parts immediately.
investments.end_customers: Radwell's customers are plant maintenance managers, MRO purchasing departments, and operations teams at manufacturing facilities, process plants, and industrial operations across all industries who need fast access to automation and electrical parts — particularly when OEM lead times are too long for production-critical applications. The company serves over 50,000 customers and its value proposition is speed and availability of difficult-to-source parts.

investments.company: Sunk Rock Foundry Partners LP, Hatteras Electrical Manufacturing Holding Company and Sigma Electric Manufacturing Corporation
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: The operating company is Sigma Electric Manufacturing Corporation, a leading manufacturer of electrical conduit bodies, conduit fittings, weatherproof electrical boxes, and related electrical raceway products sold under the Sigma and ProConnex brands through electrical distributors and big-box retailers nationwide. Sigma's die-cast aluminum and steel products include conduit bodies (LB, LL, LR, T, C), hangers, straps, box accessories, extension rings, metallic outlet boxes, and gang boxes used in commercial, industrial, and residential electrical construction. The Sunk Rock/Hatteras holding structure represents PE ownership of Sigma's manufacturing platform.
investments.end_customers: Sigma's products are purchased by electrical contractors performing commercial, industrial, and residential electrical construction, through electrical wholesale distributors and retailers including Home Depot and Lowe's. Electricians and contractors specify these products for every conduit installation — Sigma's boxes and fittings are essential, low-cost, non-discretionary components in any electrical wiring project.

investments.company: Sunvair Aerospace Group, Inc. and GB Helios Holdings, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Provider of aircraft component maintenance, repair, and overhaul services
investments.business_description: Sunvair Aerospace Group, founded in 1955 and acquired by Greenbriar Equity Group from Blue Sea Capital in June 2024, is a global provider of aircraft component MRO (maintenance, repair, and overhaul) services specializing in landing gear overhaul, avionics repair, flight instruments, sensors, accessories, and cargo loading systems. Sunvair operates state-of-the-art repair facilities in Valencia, California and Owasso (Tulsa), Oklahoma, and is a recognized world leader in landing gear, avionics, and specialty plating for commercial and military aerospace customers. GB Helios Holdings is Greenbriar's holding vehicle for the investment.
investments.end_customers: Sunvair's customers are commercial airlines, business aviation operators, military branches, and defense logistics commands that need FAA-/EASA-certified repair of aircraft components to return them to airworthy condition. Airlines and operators send components to Sunvair rather than purchasing new units because overhaul costs are a fraction of new part prices, and regulatory airworthiness standards require certified repair for many components.

investments.company: Two Six Labs, LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Two Six Labs (now operating as Two Six Technologies after its merger with IST Research under Carlyle Group ownership) is a leading provider of cybersecurity R&D, advanced technology solutions, and operationally deployed products for the U.S. Intelligence Community, Department of Defense, and national security customers. The company delivers innovation in cyber operations, data science, mobile security, microelectronics, and information operations, with approximately 900 staff and $300 million in annual revenue. Key products include the Pulse, IKE, SIGMA, and 8Wire platforms; major customers include DARPA, U.S. Cyber Command, and the Department of State. Arlington Capital Partners was the original PE backer before the Carlyle combination.
investments.end_customers: Two Six's customers are U.S. government agencies — primarily the Intelligence Community, Cyber Command, DARPA, DHS, and Department of State — that require advanced R&D, prototype technology development, and deployed cybersecurity tools for national security missions. These agencies contract with Two Six for specialized technical expertise and innovative solutions that cannot be built in-house.

investments.company: 3 Step Sports LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of integrated youth sports solutions
investments.business_description: 3STEP Sports, founded by David Geaslen and headquartered in the northeastern US, is the nation's largest youth sports club and event organization, operating 2,500+ programs across 120+ brands in nine sports (soccer, basketball, lacrosse, volleyball, football, baseball, softball, and others). The company operates 37 multi-sport facilities across the US and runs nearly 1,500 youth sports events and leagues serving over 2 million athletes, with its Run 4 The Roses basketball event being the largest in the world (25,000+ teams). 3STEP also operates its proprietary PlayerFirst athlete development platform.

investments.business_description: Bad Vibes Forever, LLC is the record label and brand entity of the estate of deceased rapper XXXTentacion (Jahseh Onfroy), managing his posthumous music catalog and associated merchandise and apparel business. The LLC holds rights to his recorded music and publishing, distributing releases through Empire Distribution. In mid-2025, the estate secured a $20 million private credit loan from Ares Capital Corp (priced at SOFR+550, maturing 2032) to fund ongoing brand development, posthumous releases, and a clothing line expansion.
investments.end_customers: Music fans of XXXTentacion—primarily Gen Z listeners who consume rap and emo-influenced hip-hop—purchase music streams, physical albums, and branded merchandise. Streaming platforms (Spotify, Apple Music) and retail partners are intermediaries, while direct-to-consumer apparel sales target the artist's core fanbase.

investments.company: Eagle Football Holdings BidCo Limited and Eagle Football Holdings Limited
investments.investment_type: Multi-club sports platform
investments.business_description: Eagle Football Holdings (now Eagle Football Group) is the multi-club football ownership vehicle of American entrepreneur John Textor, consolidating stakes in professional soccer clubs across multiple continents. Its portfolio has included Botafogo (Brazil, 2022 Copa Libertadores champions), Olympique Lyonnais (France, Ligue 1), RWD Molenbeek (Belgium), and a previously held minority stake in Crystal Palace (England, Premier League). The group operates a shared scouting, player development, analytics, and media infrastructure across all clubs to generate cross-club synergies.
investments.end_customers: Fans attending matches at each club are the primary consumers, purchasing tickets, merchandise, and broadcast subscriptions. Broadcasting partners (Canal+, beIN Sports, Globo) pay media rights fees, and corporate sponsors pay for brand association across the club portfolio.

investments.company: Fever Labs, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Technology led marketing and ticketing platform for live events
investments.business_description: Fever Labs is the world's leading independent technology platform for live entertainment discovery and ticketing, operating in more than 40 countries and reaching over 200 million people monthly. The platform uses data and personalization algorithms to match users with curated local experiences including immersive exhibitions (Van Gogh, Candlelight concerts), interactive theatre, festivals, and pop-up events. Fever has raised over $527 million in total funding from Goldman Sachs, L Catterton, Point72, and others, achieving 20x revenue growth since pre-pandemic levels and full-year EBITDA profitability in 2024.
investments.end_customers: Urban consumers—primarily millennials and Gen Z—seeking unique out-of-home experiences use the Fever app to discover and book events. Brands and event producers use Fever as a distribution and marketing channel to sell tickets and build audiences, paying Fever a commission or platform fee per transaction.

investments.company: Global Music Rights, LLC
investments.investment_type: Music right management company
investments.business_description: Global Music Rights (GMR) is a for-profit performing rights organization (PRO) founded in 2013 by veteran music executive Irving Azoff, representing a curated, invitation-only roster of top-tier songwriters and composers. GMR collects public performance royalties from radio stations, streaming services, venues, and other music users on behalf of artists including Bruce Springsteen, Bruno Mars, the Eagles, and John Lennon's catalog, generating $450–500 million in annual royalty revenue. Hellman & Friedman acquired the majority stake from TPG, with Irving Azoff retaining a position and Ares Capital providing senior debt financing.
investments.end_customers: Radio broadcasters, digital streaming platforms (Spotify, Pandora), concert venues, bars, restaurants, and any other business that publicly performs music must license GMR's repertoire. The ultimate end consumers are listeners and audiences who enjoy live or recorded music at these venues and platforms.

investments.company: Legends Hospitality Holding Company, LLC, ASM Buyer, Inc., Legends ASM Holdco I, LLC, and Stadium Coinvest (B)-III, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Hospitality platform provider of premium experiential services
investments.business_description: Legends Global (formerly Legends Hospitality) is the world's largest integrated venue services and sports business solutions company, owned by Sixth Street Partners, the New York Yankees, and the Dallas Cowboys. Following the 2024 acquisition of ASM Global—manager of over 300 arenas, stadiums, convention centers, and theaters worldwide (including NRG Stadium, OVO Arena Wembley, and Accor Stadium Sydney)—the combined entity operates over 450 venues, hosts 20,000 events annually, and entertains 165 million guests. Its services span premium hospitality and food/beverage, planning and development consulting, global sales, and fan engagement technology.
investments.end_customers: Sports franchises, concert promoters, convention organizers, and municipal venue owners engage Legends Global to manage and operate their facilities, including food service, premium experiences, and corporate hospitality. Ultimately, sports fans, concertgoers, and convention attendees are the end consumers of Legends' services at each venue.

investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)

investments.company: Mari Events Midco LLC and AE EventsCo Holdings LLC
investments.investment_type: Sports and entertainment company
investments.business_description: MARI (Mari Group) is a global live events and experiences holding company founded by Ari Emanuel (former WME CEO) and Mark Shapiro, backed by Ares Management, Apollo, RedBird Capital, and the Qatar Investment Authority. MARI's portfolio—assembled in 2025—includes the Miami Open and Madrid Open ATP/WTA tennis Masters 1000 events (acquired from IMG), the international art fair Frieze, and the premium collector car auction brand Barrett-Jackson. AE EventsCo Holdings LLC is the trademark and holding structure for the MARI brand, while Mari Events Midco LLC is the intermediate holding entity.
investments.end_customers: Tennis fans worldwide watch and attend the Miami and Madrid Open tournaments, while sponsors and broadcast partners (ESPN, beIN) pay rights fees for premium audience access. Art collectors and galleries are customers of Frieze events, and automotive enthusiasts and collectors are customers of Barrett-Jackson auctions.

investments.company: Mari Miami II LLC and South Florida Tennis, LLC
investments.investment_type: Professional sporting event
investments.business_description: Mari Miami II LLC and South Florida Tennis, LLC are the operating entities for the Miami Open tennis tournament, one of the most prestigious ATP Masters 1000 and WTA 1000 events in professional tennis, held annually at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, Florida. The event—owned by MARI Group (Ari Emanuel) and co-invested by Ares Management funds, Stephen Ross, and other Hard Rock Stadium partners—attracts elite players and approximately 400,000 fans per year. South Florida Tennis, LLC holds the tournament operating license and related commercial rights.
investments.end_customers: Tennis fans from around the world purchase tickets to the Miami Open, with premium hospitality packages sold to corporations and high-net-worth individuals. Sponsors (e.g., Itaú Bank as title sponsor) pay for brand visibility at one of the world's largest tennis events, and broadcast partners pay media rights fees.

investments.company: Production Resource Group, L.L.C. and PRG III, LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Production Resource Group (PRG) is the world's leading provider of production services and technical equipment—audio, video, lighting, rigging, staging, automation, and broadcast systems—for live entertainment and events. Founded in 1995 and headquartered in Armonk, NY, PRG operates across 154 locations in North America, South America, Europe, the Middle East, Asia, and Australia, employing 5,000–10,000 people and holding over 170 patents and 70 trademarks. It serves live music tours, Broadway productions, TV and film productions, corporate events, sports broadcasts, and gaming/esports events. PRG III, LLC is a co-borrower entity within the corporate debt structure.
investments.end_customers: Concert promoters (Live Nation, AEG), Broadway producers, TV networks, film studios, sports leagues, and Fortune 500 corporations engage PRG for technical production services at their events and productions. Ultimately, the audiences attending concerts, theatrical performances, sporting events, and corporate conferences are the consumers of the experiences PRG helps produce.

investments.company: Professional Fighters League, LLC and PFL MMA, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Mixed martial arts league
investments.business_description: Professional Fighters League (PFL) is the second-largest MMA promotion in the world, distinguished by its sports-league format where fighters compete in a regular season, playoffs, and championship—rather than the event-by-event model of other promotions. Founded in 2017 (as a restructuring of World Series of Fighting), PFL broadcasts on ESPN2, ESPN+, and is distributed to 160 countries. In 2023, PFL acquired Bellator MMA from Paramount and sold a minority stake to Saudi Arabia's SRJ Sports Investments, signing Francis Ngannou and expanding to Africa and the Middle East.
investments.end_customers: MMA fans worldwide watch PFL events on ESPN, ESPN+, and international broadcast partners, while attending live events at arenas. Brands targeting combat sports audiences—sports betting companies, nutritional supplement brands, and apparel companies—pay for sponsorship, and pay-per-view purchasers subscribe for premium bouts.

investments.company: Shout! Factory, LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Shout! Factory (now operating as Shout! Studios) is an American independent home entertainment distributor and rights acquirer founded in 2002, specializing in DVD/Blu-ray releases and digital distribution of classic and contemporary films, television series, animation, comedy specials, and live music. Its library includes The Carol Burnett Show, Mystery Science Theater 3000, Sesame Street, content from ITV Studios, Morgan Creek Productions, and GKIDS animation. The company operates sub-labels including Shout! Select, Scream Factory (horror), and Westchester Films (classic cinema).
investments.end_customers: Home video collectors, nostalgia-driven audiences, and classic TV fans purchase Shout!'s premium physical media releases (often featuring restored prints and special features). Streaming platforms and digital retailers license Shout!'s catalog, and libraries and educational institutions acquire its documentary and educational content.

investments.company: Storm Investment S.a.r.l. and Atletico Holdco, S.L.
investments.investment_type: Spanish soccer club
investments.business_description: Storm Investment S.a.r.l. is a Luxembourg-registered holding entity, and Atletico Holdco, S.L. is the Spanish holding structure, together comprising the intermediate investment vehicle through which Ares Management held an equity stake in Atlético de Madrid, one of Spain's two dominant LaLiga clubs. In November 2025, Apollo Global Management's Apollo Sports Capital agreed to acquire a 55% majority stake in the club (valuing it at approximately €2.2 billion), with existing shareholders including Ares, Miguel Ángel Gil, and Enrique Cerezo selling or diluting their positions. The transaction completed in March 2026.
investments.end_customers: Atlético de Madrid's 55,000-capacity Cívitas Metropolitano Stadium is filled by season-ticket holders and match-day fans purchasing tickets to LaLiga, Champions League, and Copa del Rey matches. Broadcast partners (LaLiga, UEFA rights holders), global sponsors, and merchandising customers worldwide generate the club's commercial revenue.

investments.company: WRE Sports Investments LLC
investments.investment_type: Professional sports club
investments.business_description: WRE Sports Investments LLC is a sports and entertainment holding company with approximately $31.5 million in disclosed funding, operating as an investment platform focused on professional sports and related activities. The 'WRE' designation and ARCC debt position suggest it is a relatively early-stage or niche sports ownership vehicle, potentially holding a minority stake in a sports franchise, sports media property, or sports-adjacent business. Specific operating details are not prominently disclosed in public sources.
investments.end_customers: As a holding and investment entity, WRE Sports Investments' end consumers depend on the underlying sports or entertainment asset. Revenue would flow from fans purchasing tickets and merchandise, broadcasters paying media rights fees, and sponsors investing in brand association with the property.

investments.company: Senior Direct Lending Program, LLC
investments.investment_type: Co-investment vehicle
investments.business_description: The Senior Direct Lending Program, LLC (SDLP) is a joint venture between Ares Capital Corporation (ARCC) and Varagon Capital Partners (backed by AIG) that provides first-lien senior secured loans—including stretch senior and unitranche structures—to U.S. middle-market companies. Established in 2015, the SDLP had $6.2 billion in committed capital from Ares and Varagon as of December 31, 2023, with approximately $5.4 billion funded and loans outstanding to 39 different borrowers, with individual hold sizes up to $300 million. ARCC holds a subordinated interest in the SDLP, participating in the economics of the joint venture.
investments.end_customers: The SDLP's customers are U.S. middle-market companies—typically PE-backed businesses with $10–150 million in EBITDA—that borrow from the SDLP to finance leveraged buyouts, acquisitions, or recapitalizations. These companies in turn serve end consumers or business customers across a diverse range of industries.

investments.company: Alcami Corporation and ACM Note Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Outsourced drug development services provider
investments.business_description: Alcami Corporation is a U.S.-based contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) with over 45 years of experience, operating five campuses across the United States. Its services span sterile fill-finish and oral solid dose drug product manufacturing, pharmaceutical formulation development, laboratory analytical services, and cGMP biostorage of temperature-sensitive biologics and pharmaceuticals. GHO Capital and The Vistria Group acquired controlling interests in Alcami in January 2023 from Madison Dearborn Partners and Ampersand Capital Partners. ACM Note Holdings, LLC is the co-borrower note holding entity in Alcami's debt structure.
investments.end_customers: Global pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies—ranging from large multinational pharma firms to clinical-stage biotechs—engage Alcami to develop and manufacture their drug products under FDA-compliant cGMP conditions. The drugs manufactured by Alcami are ultimately dispensed to patients through pharmacies and healthcare systems.

investments.company: Artemis BidCo 2 LLC
investments.investment_type: Developer and manufacturer of in vitro diagnostic immunoassays
investments.business_description: Artemis BidCo 2 LLC is a BidCo acquisition vehicle backed by Apollo Global Management, used to finance or hold a pharmaceutical or life sciences business. The 'BidCo 2' nomenclature indicates a second or parallel acquisition entity within an Apollo-structured buyout, consistent with Apollo's active life sciences and specialty pharma investment strategy during 2021–2023. Based on ARCC's first-lien position and Apollo as sponsor, the underlying operating company likely provides pharmaceutical services, specialty chemicals, or drug development and manufacturing services.
investments.end_customers: The end customers of the underlying operating company depend on its specific life sciences activity, but would likely include biopharmaceutical companies sourcing specialty chemical intermediates, contract R&D, or manufacturing services—which in turn produce drugs consumed by patients globally.

investments.company: Bamboo US BidCo LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Bamboo US BidCo LLC is the U.S. acquisition vehicle used by Advent International and Warburg Pincus in their $4.25 billion buyout of Baxter International's BioPharma Solutions business in October 2023, which was rebranded as Simtra BioPharma Solutions. Simtra is a standalone sterile fill-finish CDMO with approximately 1,700 employees operating manufacturing sites in Bloomington, Indiana and Halle, Germany, providing fill-finish services for injectable drugs and biologics from clinical development through commercial scale. ARCC holds a first-lien position through Bamboo US BidCo LLC.
investments.end_customers: Global pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies engage Simtra to fill and finish injectable drugs—including vials, syringes, and lyophilized products—under FDA/EMA-compliant manufacturing. The finished injectable products are ultimately administered to patients worldwide, including treatments for oncology, neurology, and infectious disease.

investments.company: CoreRx, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Small molecule contract development and manufacturing provider
investments.business_description: CoreRx is a contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) headquartered in Clearwater, Florida, providing drug product development, formulation, and cGMP manufacturing services to pharmaceutical and biotech clients. The company operates a multi-site campus offering services across oral solid, oral liquid, and topical dosage forms from clinical scale through commercial production. CoreRx was owned by NovaQuest Private Equity before its current ownership.
investments.end_customers: CoreRx's clients are pharmaceutical companies and biotech firms that outsource drug formulation and manufacturing rather than build in-house capabilities. These include both emerging biopharma companies advancing programs through clinical trials and established pharma companies seeking flexible, validated manufacturing capacity.

investments.company: Creek Parent, Inc. and Creek Feeder, L.P.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Creek Parent, Inc. was the acquisition vehicle used by Novo Holdings to acquire Catalent, Inc., one of the world's largest contract development and manufacturing organizations (CDMOs), in a transaction valued at approximately $16.5 billion that closed in 2024. Catalent operates more than 50 facilities globally and provides development, drug delivery technology, and manufacturing services for small molecules, biologics, and consumer health products. Post-acquisition, Catalent continues to operate as the underlying business.
investments.end_customers: Catalent's customers are pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies — from large multinationals to emerging innovators — that rely on Catalent to develop formulations, conduct clinical supply, and manufacture commercial-scale biologics, gene therapies, and finished drug products. Customers outsource to Catalent to access specialized facilities, drug delivery technologies, and global regulatory expertise.

investments.company: Moderna, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Biotechnology company
investments.business_description: Moderna is a publicly traded (NASDAQ: MRNA) clinical-stage and commercial-stage biotechnology company pioneering messenger RNA (mRNA) therapeutics and vaccines. The company developed one of the leading COVID-19 vaccines (Spikevax) and has an extensive pipeline spanning infectious diseases, oncology, cardiovascular, and rare disease indications. Moderna operates manufacturing facilities in the U.S. and internationally and has entered into large government supply agreements.
investments.end_customers: Moderna's primary customers are governments, national health agencies, and healthcare systems that procure its vaccines at the population level through advance purchase agreements and public health programs. At the individual level, patients and healthcare providers administer Moderna's approved products; the company also sells to commercial pharmacy channels for broader consumer access.

investments.company: NMC Skincare Intermediate Holdings II, LLC
investments.investment_type: Developer, manufacturer and marketer of skincare products
investments.business_description: NMC Skincare Intermediate Holdings II, LLC is the holding company for Topix Pharmaceuticals (also known as Topix Brands), a developer, manufacturer, and marketer of professional and consumer skincare products backed by New Mountain Capital. Topix operates through multiple brands including ClarityRx (professional spa and dermatology channels) and DermaE and Think (natural consumer brands sold through retail). The company is headquartered in Amityville, New York, and produces clinically-positioned topical skincare formulations.
investments.end_customers: Topix sells professional-grade products to dermatologists, medical spas, and estheticians who resell or apply them as part of treatment protocols. Consumer brands (DermaE, Think) are sold through natural/specialty retail and e-commerce channels to health-conscious consumers seeking natural or therapeutic skincare products.

investments.company: North American Science Associates, LLC, Cardinal Purchaser LLC and Cardinal Topco Holdings, L.P.
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: North American Science Associates (NAMSA) is the world's leading contract research organization (CRO) focused exclusively on medical device and medtech testing, preclinical research, and clinical trials, owned by ArchiMed Group since 2020. Founded in 1967 and headquartered in Toledo, Ohio, NAMSA tests more than 100,000 medical devices annually and provides end-to-end services from product conception through global regulatory approval and commercial launch. The company has grown through at least nine acquisitions under ArchiMed, including the U.S. medical device testing business of WuXi AppTec.
investments.end_customers: NAMSA's clients are medical device manufacturers — from large OEMs to early-stage startups — that require biocompatibility testing, sterilization validation, clinical trial management, and regulatory consulting to bring devices to market. Customers engage NAMSA to navigate FDA and global regulatory requirements without building extensive internal testing infrastructure.

investments.company: Adonis Acquisition Holdings LLC and Adonis Acquisition Holdings Parent LLC
investments.investment_type: Producer and filler of aluminum beverage cans
investments.business_description: Adonis Acquisition Holdings is the holding structure for a CVC Capital Partners-backed materials company in the specialty industrial sector. Based on the CVC sponsorship and the Materials industry classification in ARCC's portfolio, this entity likely represents an acquisition in specialty resins, composites, or industrial materials — consistent with CVC's historical activity in the specialty chemicals and performance materials space (including prior investments in AOC Resins and other composites businesses). The specific underlying operating company could not be confirmed through public sources.
investments.end_customers: End customers would depend on the specific materials business, but CVC's materials investments have historically targeted manufacturers of transportation, construction, and consumer goods products that use specialty resins, composites, or performance chemicals as input materials.

investments.company: AP Adhesives Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Distributor of industrial adhesives and equipment
investments.business_description: AP Adhesives Holdings is the holding company for APPLIED Adhesives, a comprehensive value-added distributor of hot melt, water-based, and structural adhesives as well as adhesive dispensing equipment, backed by Arsenal Capital Partners starting in 2021. Headquartered in Minnetonka, Minnesota, APPLIED Adhesives was founded in 1971 and serves the packaging, product assembly, paper converting, and automotive markets. Under Arsenal's ownership, the company completed 17 add-on acquisitions nearly tripling revenues before being sold to Bertram Capital in April 2025.
investments.end_customers: APPLIED Adhesives' customers are industrial manufacturers — in packaging, converting, assembly, and automotive production — that require adhesive solutions and technical dispensing expertise. The company serves as a value-added partner offering product selection, application engineering, and equipment service rather than acting as a pure commodity distributor.

investments.company: ASP-r-pac Acquisition CO LLC and ASP-r-pac Holdings LP
investments.investment_type: Manufacturer and supplier of printed packaging and trimmings
investments.business_description: ASP-r-pac is the acquisition holding structure for r-pac International, a leading global provider of branding and packaging solutions to the retail apparel supply chain, with backing from Ares Management (via American Securities as co-investor). r-pac International, founded in 1987 and headquartered in New York City, supplies RFID-enabled tags, labels, hangtags, belly bands, and supply chain solutions to retailers and brand owners, operating across 30 worldwide locations. The company is a leader in item-level RFID tagging for apparel from source-to-store.
investments.end_customers: r-pac's customers are apparel brands, fashion retailers, and their sourcing/manufacturing partners who need branded packaging, labeling, and RFID-enabled inventory tracking solutions across global supply chains. Retailers and brands use r-pac's tags and labels to achieve near 100% in-store inventory accuracy and full SKU-level supply chain visibility.

investments.company: Bulab Holdings, Inc. and Buckman PPC Co-Invest LP
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Bulab Holdings, Inc. is the holding company for Buckman, a Memphis, Tennessee-based global specialty chemicals company founded in 1945 that manufactures and supplies specialty chemicals for the pulp and paper, tissue, water treatment, and leather industries. Buckman was a family-owned business for its entire history before Pritzker Private Capital completed its acquisition in 2025. The company operates six manufacturing hubs with more than 1,300 employees serving customers in more than 90 countries, and is known for its digital dosing and monitoring tools alongside its chemical products.
investments.end_customers: Buckman's primary customers are pulp and paper mills, tissue manufacturers, water treatment facilities, and leather processors that purchase specialty microbial control agents, water clarifiers, dewatering aids, and processing chemicals. Customers depend on Buckman's chemicals and real-time digital monitoring systems to optimize process efficiency, reduce waste, and meet environmental compliance requirements.
