investments.company: Meyer Laboratory, LLC and Meyer Parent, LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Meyer Laboratory is a Kansas City-area manufacturer and distributor of industrial and institutional cleaning chemicals and application systems, founded in 1978 and acquired by TruArc Partners in March 2024. The company produces water-based cleaning products including industrial degreasers, disinfectants, sanitizers, hand cleaners, floor cleaners, and deodorizers, and distributes them through a nationwide network of sales representatives with local depot delivery. Meyer Lab is headquartered in Blue Springs, Missouri, with additional facilities in Lexington, North Carolina, and Winnipeg, Manitoba.
investments.end_customers: Meyer Lab serves industrial and manufacturing facilities, food processing plants, healthcare facilities, and other commercial accounts that require reliable, routinely delivered cleaning and sanitation chemicals. The company's distributor-direct model provides customers with scheduled delivery and on-site service, making it a preferred partner for facilities with ongoing, high-volume cleaning needs.

investments.company: MP Midco Holdings, LLC and MP Topco Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Food contract manufacturer
investments.business_description: MP Midco Holdings and MP Topco Holdings are the holding company entities for MP Global Products, a Norfolk, Nebraska-based manufacturer of eco-friendly flooring underlayment, acoustic solutions, and related specialty products, backed by Genstar Capital. MP Global Products manufactures its proprietary QuietWalk and SoundStep underlayment lines from recycled synthetic fibers, providing sound insulation, moisture protection, and thermal performance for laminate, luxury vinyl, and hardwood flooring installations. The company's products are sold nationwide through major home improvement retailers and flooring distributors.
investments.end_customers: MP Global's primary end customers are homeowners, contractors, and flooring installers purchasing underlayment for residential and light commercial flooring projects. The company's products are distributed through Home Depot, Menards, and other major retail and trade channels, reaching both DIY consumers and professional flooring installers.

investments.company: NCP-MSI Buyer, Inc. and NCP MSI Co-Invest, LP
investments.investment_type: Manufacturing and packaging company for major brands
investments.business_description: NCP-MSI Buyer is the acquisition vehicle through which Nonantum Capital Partners acquired MSI Express in March 2025 from HCI Equity Partners. MSI Express is one of the largest food and beverage co-manufacturers and co-packagers in the United States, offering blending, filling, packaging, and contract manufacturing across dry and liquid formats. The company operates 15 facilities across six states, headquartered in Indiana, and provides services including pouching, stand-up bagging, lidding, canister sealing, flow wrapping, and blister carding.
investments.end_customers: MSI Express's customers are branded food and beverage companies that outsource production to a contract manufacturer for operational flexibility, capacity, or cost efficiency. Customers include consumer packaged goods brands across snacks, beverages, nutritional products, and other food categories that need a reliable co-packer with nationwide scale and specialized processing capabilities.

investments.company: Nelipak Holding Company, Nelipak European Holdings Cooperatief U.A., KNPAK Holdings, LP and PAKNK Netherlands Treasury B.V.
investments.investment_type: Manufacturer of thermoformed packaging for medical devices
investments.business_description: Nelipak Healthcare Packaging, owned by Kohlberg & Company since 2019, is the leading global manufacturer of custom-designed rigid thermoformed packaging for Class II and Class III medical devices and pharmaceutical drug delivery products. The company designs and produces medical trays, blisters, surgical procedure trays, and pharmaceutical handling trays at facilities in the U.S. (Rhode Island, Pennsylvania, Arizona), the Netherlands, Ireland, Puerto Rico, and Costa Rica, with more than 800 employees worldwide.
investments.end_customers: Nelipak's customers are medical device OEMs and pharmaceutical manufacturers requiring sterile, regulatory-compliant rigid packaging for implants, surgical instruments, drug delivery devices, and other healthcare products. Customers depend on Nelipak's custom design and validation expertise to achieve sterile barrier certification and meet FDA and EU MDR packaging requirements.

investments.company: Novipax Buyer, L.L.C. and Novipax Parent Holding Company, L.L.C.
investments.investment_type: Developer and manufacturer of absorbent pads for food products
investments.business_description: Novipax is a leading U.S. manufacturer of absorbent pads and expanded polystyrene (EPS) foam trays used in the fresh meat, poultry, and seafood packaging industry, originally carved out from Sealed Air Corporation by Atlas Holdings in 2015. The company operates manufacturing facilities producing absorbent pads (which absorb purge from packaged fresh proteins to maintain presentation) and foam trays used in grocery and food service protein packaging. ACON Investments acquired the absorbent pad business from Atlas in 2020, with the tray business separately continuing as NPX ONE.
investments.end_customers: Novipax's customers are meat processors, poultry producers, and grocery retailers that package fresh proteins in foam-tray and absorbent-pad formats for retail display cases. The company's absorbent pads and trays are an essential component of the retail fresh protein supply chain, used in store-branded and brand-name fresh meat and poultry products nationwide.

investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)

investments.company: Plaskolite PPC Intermediate II LLC and Plaskolite PPC Blocker LLC
investments.investment_type: Manufacturer of specialized acrylic and polycarbonate sheets
investments.business_description: Plaskolite, owned by Pritzker Private Capital in partnership with the founding Dunn family, is North America's largest manufacturer of transparent thermoplastic sheet products including acrylic (PMMA), polycarbonate, ABS, PVC, HDPE, and PETG sheet, extruded profiles, and PMMA polymers. Founded in 1950 in Columbus, Ohio, Plaskolite produces customized sheet products used in windows, doors, lighting fixtures, signage, point-of-purchase displays, transportation glazing, security glazing, and bath products. The company has expanded through acquisitions including the assets of Plazit-Polygal and a cell-cast acrylic manufacturing facility from Trinseo.
investments.end_customers: Plaskolite's customers are fabricators, distributors, glaziers, sign manufacturers, and OEMs across construction, retail, transportation, and industrial end markets who convert thermoplastic sheet into finished glazing, display, and structural components. The company supplies both the wholesale distribution channel and direct OEM accounts across North America and internationally.

investments.company: Precision Concepts Parent Inc., Precision Concepts International LLC, and Precision Concepts Canada Corporation
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Precision Concepts International (PCI), headquartered in Huntersville, North Carolina, is a designer and manufacturer of specialty rigid consumer packaging solutions including sticks, jars, vials, closures, spouts, bottles, and tubes. The company operates five manufacturing facilities in the U.S. plus locations in Canada and Costa Rica, and was sold by ONCAP (Onex Corporation's lower mid-market platform) to The Sterling Group in 2025 while ONCAP retained a minority stake. PCI serves small to mid-size brands in personal care, household products, and food and beverage end markets.
investments.end_customers: PCI's customers are consumer product brands — in personal care, household cleaning, and food and beverage categories — that need custom-engineered plastic packaging for their retail products. The company targets small to mid-size brands that require flexible minimum order quantities and collaborative design support rather than the scale demanded by larger commodity packaging manufacturers.

investments.company: Reagent Chemical & Research, LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Reagent Chemical & Research is the largest marketer and distributor of hydrochloric acid (HCl) and sodium hydroxide (caustic soda / lye) in North America, sourcing HCl from by-product and burner producers across the U.S. and distributing it via a logistics network to industrial end users. The company is backed by Wynnchurch Capital, which acquired Reagent around 2024 and positioned it as a platform in industrial acids distribution. Hydrochloric acid is a commodity chemical used across steel pickling, food processing, water treatment, oil and gas well stimulation, and chemical synthesis.
investments.end_customers: Reagent's customers are industrial manufacturers and processors in steel, food processing, water treatment, and oil and gas sectors that consume large volumes of hydrochloric acid and caustic soda as process chemicals. The company serves as an intermediary between chemical producers (who generate HCl as a by-product) and industrial consumers who require reliable, cost-effective bulk acid supply.

investments.company: BNZ TopCo B.V.
investments.investment_type: Developer and operator of solar photovoltaic plants
investments.industry: Independent Power and Renewable Electricity Producers
investments.business_description: BNZ TopCo B.V. is the holding entity for BNZ (BNetZero), an independent renewable energy developer and independent power producer headquartered in Europe that develops, constructs, and operates utility-scale solar PV and multi-technology hybrid renewable energy projects in Southern Europe — primarily Spain, Italy, and Portugal. BNZ was founded in 2020 and is backed by Nuveen Infrastructure through its Europe Clean Energy Fund III. The company has a development pipeline of more than 2 GW across its target markets and secured a €166 million green loan from the European Investment Bank in 2024 to fund project deployment.
investments.end_customers: BNZ's revenue derives from long-term power purchase agreements and merchant electricity sales to grid operators, utilities, and corporate offtakers in Spain, Italy, and Portugal. The ultimate electricity consumers are residential and commercial utility customers served by the national grid networks into which BNZ's solar and wind generation is fed.

investments.company: Sunrun Atlas Depositor 2019-2, LLC and Sunrun Atlas Holdings 2019-2, LLC
investments.investment_type: Residential solar energy provider
investments.industry: Independent Power and Renewable Electricity Producers
investments.business_description: Sunrun Atlas Depositor 2019-2 and its parent Atlas Holdings 2019-2 are special-purpose entities (SPEs) used by Sunrun (NASDAQ: RUN) in its 2019 asset-backed securitization (ABS) program to finance a portfolio of residential solar lease and power purchase agreement (PPA) contracts originated by Sunrun. Sunrun, the largest U.S. residential solar company, regularly pools its contracted customer cash flows into ABS structures to access capital markets funding. These SPEs issue notes backed by discounted future cash flows from residential customers paying monthly for solar power generated by Sunrun-owned rooftop systems.
investments.end_customers: The underlying obligors are residential homeowners with rooftop solar systems under Sunrun leases or PPAs who make fixed monthly payments for the duration of their 20-25 year contracts. ARCC's investment as a noteholder represents a claim on the aggregated cash flows from this diversified pool of residential solar customers across multiple states.

investments.company: Sunrun Atlas Depositor 2019-2, LLC and Sunrun Atlas Holdings 2019-2, LLC
investments.industry: Independent Power and Renewable Electricity Producers
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)

investments.company: Sunrun Luna Holdco 2021, LLC
investments.investment_type: Residential solar energy provider
investments.industry: Independent Power and Renewable Electricity Producers
investments.business_description: Sunrun Luna Holdco 2021 is a special-purpose holding entity in Sunrun's 2021 residential solar securitization program, used to hold and finance a pool of solar leases, PPAs, and battery storage contracts originated by Sunrun (NASDAQ: RUN). As part of Sunrun's structured finance program, these SPEs pool contracted residential solar cash flows and issue subordinated (sub) notes alongside senior notes to finance the company's growth in home solar and battery installations. ARCC holds a subordinated position, meaning it sits junior to senior noteholders in the capital structure.
investments.end_customers: The end obligors are residential homeowners under 20-25 year Sunrun solar lease or PPA agreements who pay monthly for clean electricity generated by Sunrun-owned systems installed on their rooftops. These customers receive predictable energy cost savings while Sunrun retains system ownership, maintenance responsibility, and the associated tax credits.

investments.company: Sunrun Xanadu Issuer 2019-1, LLC and Sunrun Xanadu Holdings 2019-1, LLC
investments.investment_type: Residential solar energy provider
investments.industry: Independent Power and Renewable Electricity Producers
investments.business_description: Sunrun Xanadu Issuer 2019-1 and its associated holdings SPE are structured finance entities used by Sunrun (NASDAQ: RUN) in its Xanadu 2019-1 residential solar ABS transaction, which raised approximately $204 million in notes rated A- by Kroll Bond Rating Agency. These entities hold a portfolio of residential solar lease and PPA contracts from Sunrun's customer base, with the notes backed by the discounted present value of future contracted customer payments. The transaction is part of Sunrun's recurring ABS program that finances its residential solar fleet at the capital markets level.
investments.end_customers: Underlying customers are homeowners across multiple U.S. states with Sunrun solar lease or PPA contracts, typically with FICO scores around 738, who make regular monthly payments under 20-25 year agreements. ARCC participates as a noteholder with a senior claim on the cash flows generated by this pool of residential solar customers.

investments.company: Centric Brands LLC, Centric Brands TopCo, LLC, and Centric Brands L.P.
investments.investment_type: Designer, marketer and distributor of licensed and owned apparel
investments.business_description: Centric Brands is a global apparel and accessories licensing and design company that designs, markets, and distributes products under more than 100 licensed brand names across kids, men's, women's, and accessories categories. The company's licensed portfolio includes Calvin Klein, Tommy Hilfiger, Nautica, Under Armour, Kate Spade, Michael Kors, Disney, Marvel, and many others. Centric Brands emerged from Chapter 11 bankruptcy in October 2020 with Blackstone, Ares Management, and HPS Investment Partners as its new majority owners, having eliminated approximately $700 million of funded debt.
investments.end_customers: Centric Brands sells to major department stores, specialty retailers, mass market chains, and e-commerce platforms that carry licensed apparel and accessories — including children's clothing, denim, accessories, and entertainment-themed products. The ultimate end consumer is the retail shopper purchasing branded products at stores like Macy's, Kohl's, JCPenney, and similar retailers.

investments.company: DRS Holdings III, Inc. and DRS Holdings I, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Footwear and orthopedic foot-care brand
investments.business_description: DRS Holdings III, Inc. and DRS Holdings I, Inc. are holding company entities backed by Greenbriar Equity Group associated with a consumer durables or apparel business in ARCC's portfolio. Greenbriar Equity Group, headquartered in Greenwich, Connecticut, focuses on investments in manufacturing, services, and distribution businesses, and the DRS entities represent Greenbriar's structured acquisition holding stack for this investment. The specific underlying operating company could not be confirmed through public sources, though Greenbriar filed a Form D for DRS Holdings I in 2019.
investments.end_customers: End customers would depend on the specific operating business, but Greenbriar's portfolio history in consumer and manufacturing sectors suggests the company likely serves retail or commercial customers through product distribution or direct-to-consumer channels.

investments.company: Fossil Group, Inc., Fossil Partners, L.P., and Fossil Canada Inc.
investments.investment_type: Designer and manufacturer of consumer fashion accessories
investments.business_description: Fossil Group is a publicly traded (NASDAQ: FOSL) global design, marketing, and distribution company of consumer fashion accessories, primarily watches, under its own brands (Fossil, Relic, Zodiac, Skagen, Misfit) and licensed brands (Michael Kors, Emporio Armani, DKNY, Diesel, Kate Spade, Puma, Tory Burch). The company operates worldwide with design studios, manufacturing relationships in Asia, and a global retail and wholesale distribution network across department stores, specialty retailers, and branded retail locations. ARCC's first-lien revolving credit facility provides Fossil with working capital liquidity.
investments.end_customers: Fossil Group's end customers are fashion-oriented consumers purchasing watches and accessories at mid-range to premium price points through department stores (Macy's, Nordstrom), specialty jewelry retailers, and Fossil's own retail stores and e-commerce platform. Licensed brand customers include buyers seeking designer-branded accessories at accessible price points relative to full luxury.

investments.company: Implus Footcare, LLC, Implus Holdings, LLC, and Implus Topco, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of footwear and other accessories
investments.business_description: Implus is an innovative designer and distributor of footwear accessories and fitness products headquartered in Durham, North Carolina, owned by Berkshire Partners. The company manages a portfolio of more than 20 proprietary brands including Balega (performance socks), FuelBelt (hydration accessories), Harbinger (fitness support gear), Sof Sole (insoles and shoe care), and TriggerPoint (recovery tools), distributed to over 75,000 retail outlets across North America and in more than 70 countries worldwide. The company acquired Spenco (insoles and 2nd Skin blister care) and other brands to build out its portfolio.
investments.end_customers: Implus sells primarily through sporting goods retailers, outdoor chains, mass market retailers, and specialty footwear stores to active consumers and athletes seeking insoles, performance socks, fitness accessories, and foot care products. Retail partners include major chains where products are merchandised in footwear, running, fitness, and outdoor sections for DIY consumer purchase.

investments.company: Johnnie-O Inc. and Johnnie-O Holdings Inc.
investments.investment_type: Apparel retailer
investments.business_description: Johnnie-O is a premium lifestyle apparel brand founded in 2005 by John O'Donnell, offering golf shirts, button-downs, polos, shorts, pants, and outerwear that blend West Coast casual style with East Coast preppy aesthetics — a look the brand calls 'West Coast Prep.' The company is backed by Brentwood Associates (a Los Angeles-based consumer-focused private equity firm) and has grown from a niche golf apparel brand into a broader men's lifestyle label with meaningful e-commerce, own-retail, and wholesale distribution. PGA Tour professionals have worn the brand on-course, elevating its golf-market profile.
investments.end_customers: Johnnie-O's customers are affluent men (and increasingly youth) seeking premium casual and golf-lifestyle apparel at prices between fast fashion and full luxury. The brand sells through its own e-commerce site and retail stores, specialty golf and resort shops, and upscale department stores, targeting golfers, outdoor enthusiasts, and lifestyle consumers who value California-inspired casual styling.

investments.company: Lew's Intermediate Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Outdoor brand holding company
investments.business_description: Lew's Intermediate Holdings is part of the holding structure for Rather Outdoors (formerly Lew's Holdings Corporation), a portfolio of iconic fishing and outdoor brands backed by BDT Capital Partners (previously Peak Rock Capital). The parent platform owns Lew's Fishing (premium baitcasting and spinning rods and reels), Strike King Lures (soft plastic and hard baits), Hunters Specialties (hunting accessories), and other brands. The company is headquartered in Springfield, Missouri, and sells products through fishing specialty retailers, mass market chains, and e-commerce platforms. The sponsor field lists Sycamore Partners, suggesting a possible subsequent ownership transfer.
investments.end_customers: Rather Outdoors' end customers are recreational and competitive freshwater anglers — particularly bass fishermen — who purchase Lew's high-performance reels and rods and Strike King lures at specialty fishing retailers, sporting goods stores (Bass Pro Shops, Cabela's, Academy), and online. The brands command premium prices among tournament-level and enthusiast anglers who prioritize performance.

investments.company: Pelican Products, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Flashlights manufacturer
investments.business_description: Pelican Products is the global leader in the design and manufacture of high-performance protective cases, advanced portable lighting systems, temperature-controlled packaging, and rugged consumer gear, headquartered in Torrance, California and founded in 1976. The company's injection-molded cases are engineered to be crushproof, dustproof, and waterproof, meeting military MIL-SPEC standards, and are used by defense, aerospace, law enforcement, fire safety, life sciences, entertainment, and outdoor recreation markets worldwide. Behrman Capital acquired Pelican in 2004 for approximately $200 million, and the company has since expanded its product lines significantly.
investments.end_customers: Pelican's end customers span professional and consumer markets: defense and law enforcement agencies purchasing cases and lighting for field use, aerospace and life sciences companies transporting sensitive equipment and specimens, film production crews protecting camera gear, and outdoor enthusiasts protecting cameras, electronics, and valuables during recreation. Products are sold through specialty distributors, government procurement channels, and consumer retail.

investments.company: Rawlings Sporting Goods Company, Inc. and SEP Diamond Fund, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Sports equipment manufacturing company
investments.business_description: Rawlings Sporting Goods is one of America's most iconic sporting goods brands, founded in 1887 and headquartered in Maryland Heights, Missouri, specializing in baseball and softball equipment including baseballs, bats, gloves, helmets, protective gear, uniforms, and bags. Rawlings has been the official baseball supplier to Major League Baseball since 2000 and manufactures the official game ball used in every MLB game. Seidler Equity Partners and MLB Properties jointly acquired Rawlings from Newell Brands for $395 million in 2018; Rawlings has since acquired Easton Diamond's baseball and softball business.
investments.end_customers: Rawlings sells to MLB and minor league teams (official game balls, uniforms, protective gear), youth and amateur leagues and players (bats, gloves, helmets), and retail consumers purchasing baseball and softball equipment at sporting goods stores and online. The brand's MLB official status drives strong recognition and demand at all levels of the sport.

investments.company: Reef Lifestyle, LLC
investments.investment_type: Apparel retailer
investments.business_description: Reef is a surf-inspired lifestyle footwear brand founded in 1984 by brothers Fernando and Santiago Aguerre, specializing in men's and women's sandals, shoes, boots, and apparel that bridge beach and street style. Acquired by Charlesbank Capital Partners from VF Corporation in 2018, Reef has more than doubled its business under Charlesbank's ownership and as of 2023 holds a 66% market share in women's sandals and 58% overall in the surf/action specialty channel. The brand is headquartered in the U.S. and distributes globally through surf specialty retailers, major sporting goods chains, department stores, and its own e-commerce platform. The sponsor field notes 'Reef Capital Partners (self-sponsored)' but the actual PE owner is Charlesbank Capital Partners.
investments.end_customers: Reef's end customers are beach-lifestyle consumers, surfers, and outdoor enthusiasts who purchase sandals and casual footwear at surf shops, sporting goods retailers (REI, Dick's Sporting Goods), and department stores. The brand targets men and women seeking comfortable, durable, and stylish sandals for both water-adjacent and everyday casual wear, with particular strength in coastal markets and among the surf community.

The brand is headquartered in the U.S. and distributes globally through surf specialty retailers, major sporting goods chains, department stores, and its own e-commerce platform. The sponsor field notes 'Reef Capital Partners (self-sponsored)' but the actual PE owner is Charlesbank Capital Partners.
investments.end_customers: Reef's end customers are beach-lifestyle consumers, surfers, and outdoor enthusiasts who purchase sandals and casual footwear at surf shops, sporting goods retailers (REI, Dick's Sporting Goods), and department stores. The brand targets men and women seeking comfortable, durable, and stylish sandals for both water-adjacent and everyday casual wear, with particular strength in coastal markets and among the surf community.

investments.company: Shoes For Crews Global, LLC and Shoes For Crews Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Manufacturer and distributor of slip resistant footwear
investments.business_description: Shoes For Crews is the global leader in slip-resistant footwear, supplying work shoes and boots engineered with a proprietary rubber outsole compound that reduces slip-and-fall accidents in wet or greasy environments. Founded in 1984 and headquartered in West Palm Beach, Florida, the company distributes through both direct-to-employer programs and retail channels. The company filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy in April 2024 to execute a value-maximizing sale, with 100% of its first-lien lenders supporting the process.
investments.end_customers: Primarily employers in foodservice, hospitality, healthcare, and industrial settings who purchase footwear as a safety benefit or uniform requirement for their workers. Shoes For Crews supplies 92 of the top 100 largest U.S. restaurant chains, making institutional B2B sales to corporations and small businesses alike who seek to reduce workplace injury liability.

investments.company: Sport Maska Inc.
investments.investment_type: Manufacturer of hockey equipment and related accessories
investments.business_description: Sport Maska Inc. is the operating company that owns and manufactures the CCM Hockey brand, one of the world's oldest and most recognized ice hockey equipment brands. Headquartered in Montreal with operations across Canada, the United States, Europe, and Asia, Sport Maska produces a full line of hockey equipment including sticks, skates, helmets, protective pads, goaltender gear, and team uniforms under the CCM and Step Steel brands. Adidas sold all of its hockey operations to private equity firm Birch Hill Equity Partners (via Sport Maska) in 2017, and the company subsequently changed hands to Altor Equity Partners in 2024.
investments.end_customers: Recreational, amateur, and professional ice hockey players worldwide who purchase equipment through sporting goods retailers, hockey specialty shops, and direct-to-consumer channels. CCM also has deep penetration in professional hockey, supplying players in the NHL and other professional leagues, which drives aspirational consumer demand at all levels of the sport.

investments.company: St Athena Global LLC and St Athena Global Holdings Limited
investments.investment_type: Designer and manufacturer of branded premium-quality tableware
investments.business_description: St Athena Global Holdings Limited is the holding company structure for Steelite International, a leading manufacturer of premium ceramic tableware and hospitality products headquartered in Stoke-on-Trent, England. Steelite specializes in alumina-strengthened vitreous porcelain dinnerware, flatware, glassware, and buffet products under brands including Steelite Distinction, William Edwards, and Homer Laughlin. The company operates production facilities and showrooms globally and serves the premium end of the foodservice market.
investments.end_customers: Hotels, restaurants, cruise lines, airlines, catering companies, and institutional foodservice operators worldwide that require durable, chip-resistant, and aesthetically sophisticated tableware. Steelite targets the premium and luxury hospitality segment, where design and durability are critical procurement criteria for purchasing managers and food and beverage directors.

investments.company: Beacon Wellness Brands, Inc. and CDI Holdings I Corp.
investments.investment_type: Provider of personal care appliances
investments.business_description: Beacon Wellness Brands is a Newton, Massachusetts-based consumer wellness and personal care platform that designs, develops, and markets sexual wellness devices, beauty tools, and personal care accessories. The company's portfolio includes plusOne and Deia (sexual wellness devices), Plum Beauty and PalmPerfect (beauty tools), and Clio and Mowbie (grooming products). CDI Holdings reflects the CD&R investment structure; Beacon was formerly associated with Yellow Wood Partners before CD&R became involved through the High Ridge Brands platform.
investments.end_customers: Consumers purchasing personal care, beauty, and sexual wellness products at mass-market retailers including Walmart, Target, CVS, Walgreens, Meijer, and Amazon, as well as through direct-to-consumer e-commerce. Products are sold in over 25,000 retail locations nationwide and are targeted at value-conscious, wellness-oriented shoppers.

investments.company: Foundation Consumer Brands, LLC
investments.investment_type: Pharmaceutical holding company of over the counter brands
investments.business_description: Foundation Consumer Brands (formerly Foundation Consumer Healthcare) is a leading marketer and distributor of over-the-counter pharmaceutical and consumer health brands, backed by Juggernaut Capital Partners and Kelso & Company. Its portfolio includes Plan B One-Step (the #1 OB-GYN-recommended emergency contraceptive), Breathe Right nasal strips, Children's Dimetapp, Anbesol, Alavert, Dristan, Primatene Tablets, and FiberCon, most of which were acquired from GlaxoSmithKline Consumer Healthcare. The company was formed in 2014 and has been widely reported as a potential $4 billion sale target.
investments.end_customers: Consumers purchasing self-care and OTC health products through mass-market drug stores (CVS, Walgreens, Rite Aid), grocery chains, big-box retailers, and online pharmacies. The portfolio addresses everyday health needs including emergency contraception, allergy relief, respiratory support, and digestive health, appealing to broad demographic segments.

investments.company: LifeStyles Bidco Ltd., Lifestyles US Holdco, Inc. and LifeStyles Parent, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Provider of intimate wellness products
investments.business_description: LifeStyles Healthcare is the world's second-largest condom manufacturer, producing and marketing sexual wellness products under a global portfolio of brands including LifeStyles, SKYN (the leading non-latex brand), Manix, Unimil, and Blowtex, among others. The company was created in 2017 when a consortium led by Humanwell Healthcare Group and CITIC Capital acquired the Sexual Wellness division from Ansell Limited. Linden Capital Partners acquired LifeStyles from that consortium in December 2022, separating the Western operations from the Chinese Jissbon business retained by the sellers.
investments.end_customers: Adults purchasing contraceptives and sexual wellness products through pharmacies, drug stores, mass-market retailers, grocery chains, convenience stores, and online platforms globally. The SKYN non-latex line also targets consumers with latex sensitivity, and the company serves both branded and private-label retail channels across more than 100 countries.

investments.company: pH Beauty Holdings III, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Beauty and personal care platform
investments.business_description: pH Beauty Holdings is the PE holding structure created by Yellow Wood Partners to acquire and manage a portfolio of legacy mass-market personal care brands divested by large consumer goods companies. While the precise current brand roster has not been publicly disclosed, Yellow Wood has historically acquired orphaned consumer brands such as Salon Selectives, Aqua Net, and related hair care lines that retain broad consumer recognition but require targeted investment to revitalize. The company operates as a brand management and marketing platform distributing through mass retail channels.
investments.end_customers: Mass-market consumers purchasing hair care and personal care products at pharmacy chains, grocery stores, and big-box retailers such as Walmart, Target, CVS, and Walgreens. The brands typically target value-oriented shoppers who have established loyalties to heritage brands, and are distributed through high-velocity retail environments.

investments.company: Premier Specialties, Inc. and RMCF V CIV XLIV, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Manufacturer and supplier of natural fragrance materials and cosmeceuticals
investments.business_description: Premier Specialties is a Middlesex, New Jersey-based formulator, manufacturer, and distributor of fragrance compounds, flavor compounds, and cosmetic specialty ingredients for the personal care, household, air care, and food and beverage industries. The company supplies fragrance blends, essential oils, aroma chemicals, and bioactive cosmetic actives to consumer goods manufacturers. In 2022, Premier Specialties merged with Intarome and Fragrance Solutions to form OnScent, a combined fragrance ingredient platform with two production facilities in New Jersey.
investments.end_customers: Consumer packaged goods manufacturers and contract manufacturers that formulate personal care products (shampoos, lotions, detergents), household cleaners, candles, air fresheners, and food and beverage products. Customers range from large multinational FMCG companies to mid-size private label manufacturers who source fragrance and flavor inputs from specialty ingredient suppliers like Premier/OnScent.

investments.company: Automotive Keys Group, LLC and Automotive Keys Investor, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of replacement wireless keys for automotive market
investments.business_description: Automotive Keys Group is a PE-backed provider of automotive key and remote replacement services, offering programming, cutting, and sourcing of transponder keys, key fobs, and push-to-start remotes for consumer vehicles. The company operates as a platform serving the fragmented automotive locksmith and key duplication market, likely through a network of service locations or through wholesale supply to auto dealers and locksmiths. The platform addresses a growing market as modern vehicle keys have become increasingly complex and expensive to replace.
investments.end_customers: Vehicle owners who need replacement keys, fobs, or remote programming services, primarily through direct consumer service channels, as well as auto dealerships and independent automotive service shops that source replacement key products on behalf of their customers. The service addresses an essential, non-discretionary need when vehicle keys are lost, stolen, or damaged.

The specific underlying company acquired through Faraday Buyer has not been confirmed.
investments.end_customers: Likely automotive repair shops, dealers, and distributors purchasing replacement parts or safety products for light vehicles, commercial trucks, or powersports equipment, depending on the underlying acquired business.

investments.company: Telle Tire & Auto Service, LLC and Next Horizon Capital TireCo SPV, LP
investments.investment_type: Provider of automobile parts and auto repair services
investments.business_description: Telle Tire & Auto Centers is a St. Louis, Missouri-based tire retailer and automotive service company founded in 1942, now operating over 35 locations across Missouri and Tennessee backed by Next Horizon Capital's growth investment in December 2024. The company offers a full range of automotive services including tire installation, brakes, oil changes, diagnostics, and preventive maintenance for all vehicle types including EVs and hybrids. Under fourth-generation leadership, Telle Tire has accelerated its geographic expansion through acquisitions of Riechers Tire & Auto, Shore Tire, Miller Tire, and Triple T Tire & Auto Service.
investments.end_customers: Vehicle owners in Missouri and Tennessee seeking tire replacement, routine maintenance, and automotive repair services at local, branded service centers. The customer base spans everyday passenger vehicle owners to light truck and SUV operators who value a trusted local automotive service provider with multi-generational community roots.

investments.company: Truck-Lite Co., LLC, ECCO Holdings Corp., and Clarity Technologies Holdings, LP
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: These entities collectively represent Clarience Technologies, a Genstar Capital-backed holding company operating multiple vehicle lighting and safety technology brands. Truck-Lite is a leading provider of safety lighting, filtration systems, and telematics for commercial vehicles; ECCO Safety Group manufactures work and warning lights, backup alarms, and fleet safety products; and together with other brands (RIGID Industries, Lumitec, Road Ready, Code 3, Safe Fleet), Clarience operates as a comprehensive commercial vehicle safety technology company with products addressing visibility, warning, and fleet management.
investments.end_customers: Commercial fleet operators, truck OEMs and their dealers, first responder agencies (police, fire, ambulance), work truck upfitters, and government procurement entities that require DOT-compliant safety lighting, warning systems, and fleet visibility solutions for commercial vehicles and emergency response vehicles.

investments.company: Badia Spices, LLC
investments.investment_type: Spices and seasonings brand
investments.business_description: Badia Spices is an American manufacturer and marketer of herbs, spices, blended seasonings, and specialty food products founded by Cuban exile José Badía in 1967 in Miami's Little Havana neighborhood. Headquartered and manufactured in Doral, Florida, the company produces over 400 products including raw spices, signature blends like Complete Seasoning, and dried chili peppers, distributed throughout the U.S. and more than 70 countries. Badia remains a family-owned and operated business under the leadership of José's son Joseph 'Pepe' Badia.
investments.end_customers: Everyday consumers—particularly Hispanic households—who purchase spices and seasonings at grocery stores, Latin food retailers, club stores, and online. Badia has built particular resonance in the U.S. Hispanic community and Latin American markets, and its products are also used by independent restaurants and foodservice operators who seek affordable, authentic Latin-flavored seasoning blends.

investments.company: Berner Food & Beverage, LLC
investments.investment_type: Supplier of dairy-based food and beverage products
investments.business_description: Berner Food & Beverage is a leading private-label and contract manufacturer of processed cheese dips, salsa con queso, alfredo sauce, aerosol cheese products, and coffee- and latte-based beverages, founded in 1943 and headquartered in Dakota, Illinois (near Chicago). The company operates a state-of-the-art SQF Level 3-certified manufacturing facility and produces products for major consumer packaged goods companies and leading private-label retailers. Peak Rock Capital acquired Berner in 2015.
investments.end_customers: National grocery retailers, club stores, and consumer packaged goods brands that outsource the manufacturing of shelf-stable dips, sauces, and canned beverages to a high-quality contract manufacturer. End consumers purchase Berner-made products as private-label store brands or under CPG company labels through major retail chains.

investments.company: Demakes Enterprises, LLC
investments.investment_type: Value-added protein manufacturer
investments.business_description: Demakes Enterprises is a fourth-generation, Lynn, Massachusetts-based specialty protein manufacturer founded in 1914, producing over 250 value-added protein products including deli meats, ready-to-eat and ready-to-cook items under brands Old Neighborhood, Waterhill Organics, and Thin N' Trim, as well as private-label products for retailers. The company emphasizes 'better-for-you' attributes such as organic, antibiotic-free, and high-pressure processing (HPP) technology, which delivers superior shelf life and food safety. TowerBrook Capital Partners acquired a majority stake in December 2023 while the Demakes family retained a significant minority position.
investments.end_customers: Grocery retailers, club stores, and foodservice operators that stock premium and natural-label deli meats and protein products, with the end consumer being health-conscious shoppers willing to pay a modest premium for organic and antibiotic-free proteins sold in the deli and fresh packaged meat sections of supermarkets.

investments.company: Florida Food Products, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of plant extracts and juices
investments.business_description: Florida Food Products (FFP) is the leading formulator and producer of clean-label, plant-based natural food ingredients for the food and beverage industry, founded in 1954 and headquartered in Eustis, Florida. The company manufactures vegetable juice concentrates, fruit-derived ingredients, natural food protection systems (antimicrobials and antioxidants), and functional food ingredients that replace synthetic additives in savory foods, beverages, sports nutrition products, and processed meats. Ardian acquired a majority stake from MidOcean Partners in 2021 at an enterprise value over $1 billion; FFP also acquired Javo Beverage in 2022 to expand its beverage ingredient platform.
investments.end_customers: Food and beverage manufacturers—including producers of processed meats, soups, snacks, beverages, and sports nutrition products—that require natural, plant-derived ingredients to replace synthetic preservatives, artificial colors, and chemical additives in order to offer clean-label products to health-conscious consumers. Customers range from multinational food companies to emerging natural food brands.

investments.company: Forward Keystone Holdings, LP
investments.investment_type: Provider of better-for-you breakfast and snacking brand
investments.business_description: Forward Keystone Holdings is an Ares Management-backed subordinated debt investment in a food company. Based on the 'Keystone' name within the food and beverage industry context and Ares' involvement as a subordinated lender, this likely represents a mezzanine or subordinated debt investment in a food processing, distribution, or food ingredients business. The specific underlying operating company has not been confirmed in publicly available sources.
investments.end_customers: Depending on the underlying food company, end customers would be food manufacturers, foodservice operators, or retail consumers purchasing food products through commercial or retail distribution channels.

investments.company: Gotham Greens Holdings, PBC
investments.investment_type: Producer of vegetables and culinary herbs for restaurants and retailers
investments.business_description: Gotham Greens is a Brooklyn, New York-founded indoor farming company and one of the largest producers of greenhouse-grown leafy greens in the world, operating 13 hydroponic greenhouse facilities totaling over 1.8 million square feet across the United States. The company uses nutrient film technique hydroponics in 100% renewable-electricity-powered greenhouses that consume 95% less water and 97% less land than conventional field agriculture while producing up to 30 times more yield per acre. In addition to leafy greens and herbs, Gotham Greens produces salad kits, dressings, dips, and cooking sauces, and is organized as a public benefit corporation reflecting its sustainability mission.
investments.end_customers: Grocery retailers, natural food chains (Whole Foods, Sprouts), conventional supermarkets, and foodservice operators that prioritize locally grown, pesticide-free, sustainably produced greens with extended freshness. The end consumer is an environmentally conscious, health-focused shopper who values local sourcing, minimal pesticide use, and year-round availability of fresh leafy greens.

investments.company: HBH Buyer, LLC
investments.investment_type: Food retailer specializing in fully cooked hams and catering services
investments.business_description: HBH Buyer, LLC is the acquisition vehicle used by Harvest Partners to acquire a food and beverage company classified in the Food and Beverage sector. Harvest Partners is a New York-based middle-market PE firm with a history of investing in consumer, food, and business services companies. The specific underlying operating company behind HBH Buyer has not been confirmed in publicly available sources.
investments.end_customers: Depending on the underlying food and beverage company, end customers would likely be retail grocery shoppers, foodservice operators, or institutional food buyers depending on the product category.

investments.company: KNPC HoldCo, LLC
investments.investment_type: Producer of trail mix and mixed nut snack products
investments.business_description: KNPC HoldCo, LLC is the holding entity for Second Nature Brands (formerly Kar's Nuts), a Madison Heights, Michigan-based manufacturer and marketer of nuts, trail mixes, and snack confections, backed by Palladium Equity Partners. The company operates under the Kar's Nuts, Second Nature, Sanders (confections), and Morley Candy Makers brands, having acquired Sanders in 2018 and Voortman Cookies' parent from J.M. Smucker. The combined platform offers a diverse snack and confection portfolio sold through grocery, mass retail, drug, and convenience store channels.
investments.end_customers: Grocery retailers, mass-market stores, club stores, drug stores, vending machine operators, and convenience stores that distribute the Kar's, Second Nature, and Sanders snack and candy brands to everyday snack-food consumers. End consumers purchasing these products are seeking convenient, portable snack options across the value-to-premium price range.

investments.company: Max US Bidco Inc.
investments.investment_type: Manufacturer of premium dry dog food
investments.business_description: Max US Bidco Inc. is the U.S. acquisition vehicle used by PAI Partners, a Paris-based private equity firm, to acquire Alphia Inc.—a Denver-based national co-manufacturer of superpremium dry pet food and treats—from J.H. Whitney Capital Partners in 2023. Alphia manufactures more than one billion pounds of dry pet food and treats annually for leading pet food brands and private-label retailers across six U.S. facilities, and also provides ingredient milling (via LANI) and warehousing and logistics (via Veracity) services. The deal was valued at approximately $1 billion.
investments.end_customers: Leading national pet food brands (both branded and private-label retailers) that outsource the manufacturing of dry dog and cat food, treats, and related products to Alphia's contract manufacturing facilities. The ultimate end consumers are pet owners purchasing premium and superpremium dry pet food at pet specialty retailers, grocery chains, and online marketplaces.

investments.company: Primo Water Holdings Inc / Triton Water Holdings Inc
investments.investment_type: Producer and provider of bottled water brands
investments.business_description: Primo Brands (Nasdaq: PRMW), formerly Primo Water Corporation, is a North American leader in sustainable hydration products and water delivery services, now operating as a combined entity following the November 2024 merger with BlueTriton Brands (the former Nestlé Waters North America business). The company offers direct-to-home and office water delivery, in-store water dispensing and refill stations, single-use bottled water, and multi-gallon jugs under brands including Primo, ReadyRefresh, Poland Spring, Deer Park, Arrowhead, and others. The combined company has more than 13,000 employees and reaches over 90% of the U.S. population through its distribution network.
investments.end_customers: Residential households, offices, and commercial businesses that subscribe to home and office water delivery services or purchase bottled and multi-gallon water at retail. The direct delivery segment targets customers who prefer the convenience of purified or spring water delivered on a recurring basis, while the retail segment serves consumers at grocery, club, and convenience stores.

investments.company: Spindrift Beverage Co., Inc. and SBC Aggregator LP
investments.investment_type: Premium flavored sparkling water brand
investments.business_description: Spindrift is a Newton, Massachusetts-based premium sparkling water brand, founded in 2010, that produces the first sparkling water in the U.S. made with real squeezed fruit — no added sugar or artificial flavors. Its product line spans flavored sparkling waters, tea-based sparkling beverages, and Spindrift Spiked alcoholic seltzers. Gryphon Investors acquired a majority stake in 2025, with founder Bill Creelman moving to a board chair role.
investments.end_customers: Spindrift sells primarily through retail grocery, convenience, and e-commerce channels to health-conscious consumers seeking a natural alternative to traditional sodas and plain seltzers. It also has a foodservice presence. The brand resonates strongly with millennial and Gen Z consumers who value clean-label, minimally processed beverages.

investments.company: Sugar PPC Buyer LLC
investments.investment_type: Manufacturer and distributor of food products
investments.business_description: Sugar PPC Buyer LLC is the acquisition vehicle used by Pritzker Private Capital (PPC) to acquire Sugar Foods Corporation in 2023. Sugar Foods, headquartered in Westlake Village, California and founded in 1948, is a leading producer of crunchy toppings, croutons (brands include Fresh Gourmet and Mrs. Cubbison's), beverage ingredients, pizza toppings, baking mixes, and confectionery ingredients for foodservice, retail, and private label channels. The company operates five processing facilities across the U.S. and Mexico and employs more than 1,400 people, serving over 3,000 customers.
investments.end_customers: Sugar Foods sells to major grocery retailers, foodservice distributors, quick-service restaurants, and casual dining chains globally. Its products appear on salad bars and restaurant tables as private-label and branded toppings, and in home kitchens via retail grocery. Customers value Sugar Foods for its broad product range, national manufacturing footprint, and the ability to deliver both branded and private-label solutions at scale.

investments.company: Sugar PPC Buyer LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)

investments.company: Watermill Express, LLC and Watermill Express Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)
investments.business_description: Watermill Express is the largest drive-up pure drinking water and ice vending franchise in the United States, founded in 1984 and headquartered in Brighton, Colorado. The company operates over 1,300 self-serve outdoor kiosks across more than a dozen states, providing 24/7 purified water through a patented 12-step purification process that includes reverse osmosis and ultraviolet disinfection. Brentwood Associates is the primary institutional backer, having partnered with the founding Dolifka family.
investments.end_customers: Watermill Express serves everyday consumers seeking high-quality purified drinking water at a fraction of the cost of bottled water, with kiosks typically located in grocery store parking lots and strip mall forecourts. Customers are price-conscious households — particularly in the Sun Belt — who refill reusable jugs rather than buying single-use plastic bottles. The business model appeals strongly to value-oriented and environmentally aware consumers.

investments.company: GNZ Energy Bidco Limited and Galileo Co-investment Trust I
investments.investment_type: Independent fuel provider in New Zealand
investments.business_description: GNZ Energy Bidco Limited is the acquisition vehicle through which Australian private equity firm Allegro Funds acquired Gull New Zealand in 2022 for NZ$572 million, following Ampol's divestiture of Gull as a condition of its merger with Z Energy. Gull is New Zealand's largest independent fuel retailer, operating approximately 115 service stations (the majority unmanned, low-cost sites) and the only independent operator with a vertically integrated fuel supply capability, including a strategic import terminal at Mount Maunganui. Galileo Co-investment Trust I represents co-investor participation in the same transaction.
investments.end_customers: Gull serves New Zealand motorists seeking low-cost fuel, differentiating from the major networks (Z Energy, BP, Mobil) through competitive pricing — a phenomenon known as the 'Gull effect' that has historically pressured pump prices in markets where it operates. Its customer base skews toward price-sensitive commuters and small fleet operators. Gull also supplies fuel to commercial and agricultural customers via its import terminal.

investments.company: HighPeak Energy, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Oil and gas exploration and production company
investments.business_description: HighPeak Energy (NASDAQ: HPK) is an independent crude oil and natural gas exploration and production company founded in 2019 and headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas. The company focuses exclusively on the Midland Basin portion of the Permian Basin, primarily in Howard and Borden Counties in West Texas. HighPeak is known for having one of the highest oil-cut percentages among Permian peers, which supports strong operating margins.
investments.end_customers: HighPeak sells its crude oil production to refiners and crude oil marketers, and its natural gas and NGLs to midstream processors and utilities. As a pure-play Permian producer, its revenue is directly tied to WTI crude oil prices. Downstream customers value the high-quality, liquids-rich production from HighPeak's acreage in one of the most prolific oil-producing counties in the U.S.

investments.company: Phoenix Operating LLC
investments.investment_type: Oil and gas investment company
investments.business_description: Phoenix Operating LLC is the operated working interest E&P subsidiary of Phoenix Capital Group Holdings LLC (now rebranded as Phoenix Energy One, LLC / Phoenix Energy), a Denver-based oil and gas mineral rights and working interest company founded in 2019. Phoenix Operating focuses on drilling and production in the Williston Basin (North Dakota), DJ Basin (Colorado/Wyoming), and Powder River Basin (Wyoming/Montana). The company drilled at record pace in the Williston Basin in 2024 and surpassed 1.57 million barrels of production in H1 2024, tracking toward a ~25,000 BOEPD exit rate.
investments.end_customers: Phoenix Operating sells crude oil production to crude oil purchasers and pipeline operators serving Upper Midwest and Rocky Mountain refineries. Natural gas and NGL production is sold to midstream gatherers and processors. The company is not traditional PE-backed; it raises capital directly from investors via its parent's mineral rights and working interest acquisition model.

investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)

investments.company: VPROP Operating, LLC and V SandCo, LLC
investments.investment_type: Sand-based proppant producer and distributor to the oil and natural gas industry
investments.business_description: VPROP Operating and V SandCo are operating entities of Vista Proppants and Logistics (VPROP), a Fort Worth, Texas-based in-basin frac sand supplier serving oil and gas operators in the Permian Basin and other prolific producing regions. Vista filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in 2020 amid the oil price collapse, reorganized with backing from Ares Capital, MSD Partners, and Angelo Gordon, and emerged as V SandCo LLC. The company provides frac sand (proppant) and logistics services — including transloading and trucking — to E&P customers completing horizontal oil and gas wells.
investments.end_customers: V SandCo's customers are oil and gas exploration and production companies operating horizontal wells in the Permian Basin and adjacent basins, who require large volumes of high-quality frac sand to stimulate production. The company competes on proximity (in-basin supply reduces trucking cost and delivery time) and logistics reliability. ARCC's first-lien position reflects debt extended as part of the post-bankruptcy capital restructuring.

investments.company: Opal Fuels Intermediate HoldCo LLC, and Opal Fuels Inc.
investments.investment_type: Owner of natural gas facilities
investments.business_description: OPAL Fuels (NASDAQ: OPAL), headquartered in White Plains, New York, is a vertically integrated renewable natural gas (RNG) producer and distributor that captures methane emissions from landfills and dairy farms, purifies them into pipeline-quality RNG, and delivers that fuel to heavy-duty trucking fleets through a network of compressed natural gas (CNG) fueling stations. The company develops, constructs, owns, and operates RNG production facilities and CNG fueling stations across the U.S., and also designs hydrogen fueling infrastructure. OPAL went public via SPAC merger with ArcLight Clean Transition Corp. II in 2022.
investments.end_customers: OPAL Fuels' primary customers are large trucking fleets and logistics operators seeking low-carbon, cost-competitive alternatives to diesel fuel, including waste haulers, food distributors, and municipal transit agencies. RNG is sold to energy marketers and utilities seeking to meet renewable fuel standard (RFS) obligations and carbon credit (D3 RIN) requirements. The company also sells electricity produced at some landfill gas sites to regional grid operators.

investments.company: Excelitas Technologies Corp.
investments.investment_type: Provider of photonic solutions
investments.business_description: Excelitas Technologies, headquartered in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, is a global photonics and optoelectronics manufacturer serving OEM customers across defense, aerospace, medical diagnostics, semiconductor equipment, industrial, and consumer sectors. The company designs and manufactures lasers, specialty illumination systems, optical components, sensors, detectors, and power electronics — over nearly 7,000 employees across North America, Europe, and Asia. Excelitas is owned by AEA Investors (not AE Industrial Partners as sometimes listed in market data; AEA Investors is the confirmed owner).
investments.end_customers: Excelitas' end customers include defense and intelligence agencies (electro-optical systems, night vision, missile seekers), medical device OEMs (surgical visualization, clinical diagnostics, blood analyzers), semiconductor equipment makers (lithography light sources), and industrial automation companies (machine vision, 3D sensing). Customers are principally OEMs who embed Excelitas components into larger systems and who value mission-critical reliability, customization capability, and the company's deep photonics engineering expertise.

investments.company: FL Hawk Intermediate Holdings, Inc.
investments.investment_type: Provider of variable data labeling for the apparel industry
investments.business_description: FL Hawk Intermediate Holdings, Inc. is the holding company for Cooper's Hawk Winery & Restaurants, an upscale casual dining and lifestyle brand founded in 2005 by Tim McEnery and headquartered in Downers Grove, Illinois. Cooper's Hawk operates 69+ locations across the Midwest and Southeast, each combining a full-service restaurant, bar, private dining room, Napa-style tasting room, and retail wine and gift shop. Ares Management (through its private equity funds) acquired a majority stake in 2019 for approximately $700 million; the company was reported to be pursuing an IPO as of mid-2025.
investments.end_customers: Cooper's Hawk serves affluent suburban diners and wine enthusiasts, anchored by America's largest wine club with over 650,000 members who receive monthly curated wine selections and exclusive member benefits. End customers are primarily upper-middle-income adults who value the bundled experience of restaurant dining, wine education, and retail in a single destination. The wine club membership model generates recurring revenue and high customer loyalty independent of individual dining visits.

investments.company: PerkinElmer U.S. LLC and NM Polaris Co-Invest, L.P.
investments.investment_type: Provider of analytical instrumentation and testing equipment and services
investments.business_description: PerkinElmer U.S. LLC (as held in ARCC's portfolio) is the operating entity of the Applied, Food, and Enterprise Services businesses that New Mountain Capital (NMC) acquired from PerkinElmer, Inc. for $2.45 billion in March 2023. The business includes the OneSource laboratory services platform (lab instrument maintenance, repair, and calibration for biopharma and research organizations), as well as a portfolio of atomic spectroscopy, molecular spectroscopy, and chromatography instruments, consumables, and reagents. The parent company PerkinElmer rebranded its remaining life sciences and diagnostics operations as Revvity. NM Polaris Co-Invest, L.P. is a co-investment vehicle of New Mountain Capital alongside the main fund.
investments.end_customers: Customers include pharmaceutical and biotech companies (instruments and lab services for drug development and QC), food safety testing laboratories (contaminant detection and composition analysis), environmental testing labs (water, soil, and air analysis), and applied industrial customers. End users choose this platform for its breadth of analytical instruments, global field service network, and the OneSource outsourced lab management model that reduces overhead and ensures uptime for regulated laboratories.

investments.company: Repairify, Inc. and Repairify Holdings, LLC
investments.investment_type: Provider of automotive diagnostics scans and solutions
investments.business_description: Repairify, Inc. (branded externally as asTech) is a Plano, Texas-based automotive technology and intelligence company that provides OEM-grade remote and local vehicle diagnostics, ADAS calibration, and ECU programming services to auto body shops, collision repair centers, and dealerships across North America and internationally. Its proprietary asTech hardware devices connect to vehicles via OBD-II and enable certified remote technicians to perform manufacturer-required scans and calibrations without an OEM factory tool on site. Kinderhook Industries is the private equity sponsor; the Repairify family of brands also includes BlueDriver, MobileTechRX, and One Guard Inspections.
investments.end_customers: Repairify's primary customers are auto body and collision repair shops (MSOs and independent shops) that must perform pre- and post-repair OEM diagnostic scans to satisfy insurer requirements and safety standards. Insurance carriers and fleet operators that route vehicle repairs also influence purchasing decisions. Technicians and shop owners value Repairify for reducing the need to purchase expensive OEM scan tools or sublet work to dealerships, enabling them to keep complex diagnostics in-house while ensuring OEM compliance.

investments.investment_type: WEIGHTED AVERAGE SHARES OF COMMON STOCK OUTSTANDING (see Note 10)

investments.company: Expereo USA, Inc. and Ristretto Bidco B.V.
investments.investment_type: Global internet managed service provider
investments.business_description: Expereo is the world's largest provider of managed global internet connectivity and SD-WAN services to multinational enterprises and government organizations. Headquartered in Amsterdam (Netherlands), Expereo aggregates and manages internet circuits from hundreds of local providers across 190+ countries through a single platform, enabling consistent network performance for distributed enterprise locations. Ristretto Bidco B.V. is the acquisition holding vehicle through which Vitruvian Partners acquired Expereo in 2021. The company has expanded via acquisitions of Videns IT Services and Brodynt.
investments.end_customers: Expereo serves large multinational corporations, financial institutions, retailers, and government agencies that operate dozens to thousands of sites across multiple countries and require centrally managed, reliable internet connectivity without dealing with dozens of local ISP contracts. Customers value Expereo's single SLA, unified management portal, and the ability to rapidly provision and monitor internet connectivity globally. The SD-WAN and cloud acceleration layer helps improve application performance for remote workers and distributed offices.

investments.company: Retained Vantage Data Centers Intermediate Holdco, LP and Retained Vantage Data Centers Assets, LP
investments.investment_type: Global data center developer and operator
investments.business_description: These entities represent a retained interest in Vantage Data Centers, one of the world's largest hyperscale data center developers and operators, backed by DigitalBridge Group alongside Silver Lake, AustralianSuper, and PSP Investments. Vantage was launched in 2010 with the acquisition of Intel's Santa Clara data center and has grown into a global wholesale colocation platform with campuses across North America, Europe (including a 180MW+ facility in Wales), and Asia-Pacific (Japan, Australia, Hong Kong). The company develops and leases large-scale (1MW+) data center capacity to cloud providers and large enterprises on long-term contracts.
investments.end_customers: Vantage's customers are hyperscale cloud providers (e.g., Microsoft, AWS, Google), large financial institutions, and other technology companies that require massive, dedicated data center infrastructure built to their specifications. Clients lease capacity on multi-year agreements, typically 10–15 years, valuing Vantage's ability to rapidly develop purpose-built campuses with guaranteed power delivery, cooling redundancy, and network connectivity in strategic markets.

investments.company: Nordic Ferry Infrastructure AS
investments.investment_type: Private passenger & freight ferry transportation company
investments.business_description: Nordic Ferry Infrastructure AS (NFI) is the leading pan-Nordic ferry and express boat operator, formed in February 2022 through the combination of Molslinjen (Denmark) and Torghatten (Norway), with ForSea (Sweden) added in January 2023. NFI operates over 60 routes across Norway, Denmark, and Sweden using a fleet of more than 100 vessels, transporting over 25 million passengers per year — functioning as essential 'floating bridges' on routes where no fixed road link exists. EQT Infrastructure is the equity owner; ARCC holds a subordinated debt position as part of the infrastructure financing.
investments.end_customers: NFI serves commuters, tourists, and freight operators who depend on ferry routes as primary transportation links across Norwegian fjords, Danish straits, and the Oresund region. Municipal and national governments contract with NFI to operate subsidized public service routes. Commercial freight customers use NFI's roll-on/roll-off capacity for cross-fjord and cross-strait truck transport. The company's essential-infrastructure nature provides stable, contract-based revenue largely insulated from economic cycles.
